Confluence Data Center documentation
====================================

   

Confluence is where you create, organize, and discuss work with your team.

* * *

Get started
===========

Kick start your Confluence journey with our navigation and space tutorials.

[View guide](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/get-started-777010817.html)

What’s new in 10.2
==================

Time to upgrade? Get the low down on the latest and greatest changes in Confluence.

[View release notes](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/confluence-release-notes-327.html)

[Spaces\
======\
\
Learn about spaces and how to set up your Confluence site for success. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/spaces-139459.html)
[Pages and blogs\
===============\
\
Everything you need to know about creating pages, blogs and using the editor.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/pages-and-blogs-320602215.html)
[Files\
=====\
\
Share and preview Office documents, images, PDFs and more.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/files-139465.html)
[Confluence Mobile\
=================\
\
Use Confluence on your phone or tablet's browser, or download the Confluence Data Center app for iOS and Android.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/confluence-mobile-284365706.html)
[Macros\
======\
\
From formatting to dynamic content, there's a macro for just about everything.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/macros-139387.html)
[Your profile and settings\
=========================\
\
Upload a profile pic, change your password and set Confluence up just how you like it. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/your-profile-and-settings-139467.html)
[Collaboration\
=============\
\
Comment on pages and files, mention people, get notifications and share stuff with your team.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/collaboration-251724137.html)
[Analytics\
=========\
\
Find out how often spaces and pages are viewed, and see how active your users are.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/analytics-1044780333.html)
[Search\
======\
\
Find it first time, every time with our tips to unlock the power of Confluence search. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/search-139528.html)
[Permissions and restrictions\
============================\
\
Learn how to restrict your work to some people or share it with everyone. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/permissions-and-restrictions-139557.html)

==============\
\
Keep track of team leave, rosters, birthdays, launches—you name it!—right in Confluence.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/team-calendars-1044780345.html)
[Add-ons and integrations\
========================\
\
Maximize your team’s potential by connecting Confluence with other products and applications, such as Jira.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/add-ons-and-integrations-720410414.html)
[Confluence use-cases\
====================\
\
Get practical tips to help you use Confluence for software teams, as a knowledge base or as your intranet. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/confluence-use-cases-283641068.html)

================================\

=========================================\

# Server and Data Center GDPR support guides | GDPR Cloud | Atlassian Documentation

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/staticassets/4.8.27/dist/common/images/media-viewer-image-icon.svg)

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/gdpr)

*   #### Viewport

Server and Data Center GDPR support guides
==========================================

GDPR and Atlassian Server and Data Center applications
------------------------------------------------------

We understand that customers using Atlassian Server or Data Center applications may have GDPR-specific obligations with respect to the data stored in and processed through those applications. For example, customers may need to access, modify or delete personal data upon request by certain individuals.  We've prepared a GDPR FAQ, and guides for our Server and Data Center products, and hope they'll be helpful in assisting our customers with their GDPR-specific obligations.

Please note, when you use Atlassian Server or Data Center applications, Atlassian does not access, collect, store or otherwise process personal data you choose to store within those applications, except in limited cases where such data is provided for incidental support services.  For this reason, Atlassian is neither a data processor nor data controller under GDPR for the purposes of the personal data you choose to store within Atlassian Server or Data Center applications.  Atlassian may access analytics events associated with your technical use of the products, but only where permitted by your administrator.  Such analytics information is filtered to exclude any personal data prior to the analytics information leaving your environment.  For more information on the analytics information collected through Atlassian downloadable products, please see our [Privacy Policy](https://www.atlassian.com/legal/privacy-policy)
.  

**Using multiple Atlassian products?**

Check our our GDPR support guides for your Atlassian products:

*   [Bamboo Server GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/gdpr/bamboo-server-gdpr-support-guides-949245655.html)
    
*   [Bitbucket Server and Data Center GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/bitbucketserver/bitbucket-server-and-data-center-gdpr-support-guides-962360101.html)
    
*   [Confluence Server and Data Center GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/gdpr-support-guides-for-confluence-data-center-960696666.html)
    
*   [Crowd Server and Data Center GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/gdpr/crowd-server-and-data-center-gdpr-support-guides-949245650.html)
    
*   [Fisheye and Crucible Server GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/gdpr/fisheye-and-crucible-server-gdpr-support-guides-949245659.html)
    
*   [Hipchat Server GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/hc/hipchat-server-gdpr-support-guides-962356659.html)
    
*   [Hipchat Data Center GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/hipchatdc3/hipchat-data-center-gdpr-support-guides-962356685.html)
    
*   [Jira Core, Jira Software and Jira Service Management (formerly Jira Service Desk) Server and Data Center GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/gdpr/jira-core-jira-software-and-jira-service-desk-server-and-data-center-gdpr-support-guides-949245619.html)
     
*   [Advanced Roadmaps (formerly Portfolio for Jira) Server and Data Center GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/advancedroadmapsserver/advanced-roadmaps-gdpr-support-guides-962968641.html)
    

GDPR and Atlassian Cloud applications
-------------------------------------

Using an Atlassian Cloud application?  Head to [www.atlassian.com/trust/privacy/gdpr](https://www.atlassian.com/trust/privacy/gdpr)
 for more information.

\

=========================\

=========\

[View guide](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/get-started-1425051809.html)

What’s new in 9.0
=================

Time to upgrade? Get the low down on the latest and greatest changes in Confluence.

[View release notes](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/confluence-release-notes-1425053881.html)

Last modified on Jul 30, 2024

[Spaces\
======\
\
Learn about spaces and how to set up your Confluence site for success. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/spaces-1425051872.html)
[Pages and blogs\
===============\
\
Everything you need to know about creating pages, blogs and using the editor.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/pages-and-blogs-1425051971.html)
[Files\
=====\
\
Share and preview Office documents, images, PDFs and more.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/files-1425052364.html)
[Confluence Mobile\
=================\
\
Use Confluence on your phone or tablet's browser, or download the Confluence Data Center app for iOS and Android.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/confluence-mobile-1425052429.html)
[Macros\
======\
\
From formatting to dynamic content, there's a macro for just about everything.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/macros-1425052464.html)
[Your profile and settings\
=========================\
\
Upload a profile pic, change your password and set Confluence up just how you like it. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/your-profile-and-settings-1425052772.html)
[Collaboration\
=============\
\
Comment on pages and files, mention people, get notifications and share stuff with your team.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/collaboration-1425052816.html)
[Analytics\
=========\
\
Find out how often spaces and pages are viewed, and see how active your users are.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/analytics-1425052876.html)
[Search\
======\
\
Find it first time, every time with our tips to unlock the power of Confluence search. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/search-1425052886.html)
[Permissions and restrictions\
============================\
\
Learn how to restrict your work to some people or share it with everyone. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/permissions-and-restrictions-1425052920.html)
[Team Calendars\
==============\
\
Keep track of team leave, rosters, birthdays, launches—you name it!—right in Confluence.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/team-calendars-1425052935.html)
[Add-ons and integrations\
========================\
\
Maximize your team’s potential by connecting Confluence with other products and applications, such as Jira.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/add-ons-and-integrations-1425053118.html)
[Confluence use-cases\
====================\
\
Get practical tips to help you use Confluence for software teams, as a knowledge base or as your intranet. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/confluence-use-cases-1425053157.html)

================================\
\

\

=========================================\

\

============================\

Confluence Data Center and Server documentation
===============================================

   

Confluence is where you create, organize, and discuss work with your team.

* * *

Get started
===========

Kick start your Confluence journey with our navigation and space tutorials.

[View guide](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/get-started-1283359459.html)

What’s new in 8.5
=================

Time to upgrade? Get the low down on the latest and greatest changes in Confluence.

[View release notes](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/confluence-release-notes-1283361505.html)

Last modified on Aug 22, 2023

[Spaces\
======\
\
Learn about spaces and how to set up your Confluence site for success. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/spaces-1283359521.html)
[Pages and blogs\
===============\
\
Everything you need to know about creating pages, blogs and using the editor.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/pages-and-blogs-1283359620.html)
[Files\
=====\
\
Share and preview Office documents, images, PDFs and more.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/files-1283360007.html)
[Confluence Mobile\
=================\
\
Use Confluence on your phone or tablet's browser, or download the Confluence Data Center app for iOS and Android.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/confluence-mobile-1283360067.html)
[Macros\
======\
\
From formatting to dynamic content, there's a macro for just about everything.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/macros-1283360102.html)
[Your profile and settings\
=========================\
\
Upload a profile pic, change your password and set Confluence up just how you like it. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/your-profile-and-settings-1283360410.html)
[Collaboration\
=============\
\
Comment on pages and files, mention people, get notifications and share stuff with your team.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/collaboration-1283360449.html)
[Analytics\
=========\
\
Find out how often spaces and pages are viewed, and see how active your users are.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/analytics-1283360509.html)
[Search\
======\
\
Find it first time, every time with our tips to unlock the power of Confluence search. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/search-1283360519.html)
[Permissions and restrictions\
============================\
\
Learn how to restrict your work to some people or share it with everyone. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/permissions-and-restrictions-1283360553.html)
[Team Calendars\
==============\
\
Keep track of team leave, rosters, birthdays, launches—you name it!—right in Confluence.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/team-calendars-1283360568.html)
[Add-ons and integrations\
========================\
\
Maximize your team's potential by connecting Confluence to Jira, Hipchat and more. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/add-ons-and-integrations-1283360751.html)
[Confluence use-cases\
====================\
\
Get practical tips to help you use Confluence for software teams, as a knowledge base or as your intranet. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/confluence-use-cases-1283360795.html)
[Confluence administrator's guide\
================================\
\
Configure and customize Confluence, manage users, view your license and more. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/confluence-administrator-s-guide-1283360824.html)
[Confluence installation and upgrade guide\
=========================================\
\
Get step-by-step guides for installing and upgrading your Confluence server. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/confluence-installation-and-upgrade-guide-1283361316.html)
[Migrate your Confluence site\
============================\
\
Everything you need to know to migrate your Confluence site to cloud or Data Center.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/migrate-your-confluence-site-1283361433.html)
[Confluence Data Center\
======================\
\
Everything you need to make sure Confluence can scale with your organization.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/confluence-data-center-1283361443.html)
[GDPR support guides for Confluence Server and Data Center\
=========================================================\
\
Information to help you with any GDPR-specific obligations that you may have.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/gdpr-support-guides-for-confluence-server-and-data-center-1283361492.html)
[Confluence Release Notes\
========================\
\
Ready to upgrade? Find out what's new in the latest Confluence release.  \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/confluence-release-notes-1283361505.html)
[Getting help and support\
========================\
\

\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/getting-help-and-support-1283361509.html)

Confluence Data Center documentation
====================================

   

Confluence is where you create, organize, and discuss work with your team.

* * *

Get started
===========

Kick start your Confluence journey with our navigation and space tutorials.

[View guide](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/get-started-1477575467.html)

What’s new in 9.2
=================

Time to upgrade? Get the low down on the latest and greatest changes in Confluence.

[View release notes](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/confluence-release-notes-1627458800.html)

Last modified on Dec 10, 2024

[Spaces\
======\
\
Learn about spaces and how to set up your Confluence site for success. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/spaces-1477575530.html)
[Pages and blogs\
===============\
\
Everything you need to know about creating pages, blogs and using the editor.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/pages-and-blogs-1477575630.html)
[Files\
=====\
\
Share and preview Office documents, images, PDFs and more.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/files-1477576023.html)
[Confluence Mobile\
=================\
\
Use Confluence on your phone or tablet's browser, or download the Confluence Data Center app for iOS and Android.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/confluence-mobile-1477576088.html)
[Macros\
======\
\
From formatting to dynamic content, there's a macro for just about everything.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/macros-1477576130.html)
[Your profile and settings\
=========================\
\
Upload a profile pic, change your password and set Confluence up just how you like it. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/your-profile-and-settings-1477576443.html)
[Collaboration\
=============\
\
Comment on pages and files, mention people, get notifications and share stuff with your team.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/collaboration-1477576487.html)
[Analytics\
=========\
\
Find out how often spaces and pages are viewed, and see how active your users are.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/analytics-1477576547.html)
[Search\
======\
\
Find it first time, every time with our tips to unlock the power of Confluence search. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/search-1477576557.html)
[Permissions and restrictions\
============================\
\
Learn how to restrict your work to some people or share it with everyone. \
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/permissions-and-restrictions-1477576591.html)
[Team Calendars\
==============\
\
Keep track of team leave, rosters, birthdays, launches—you name it!—right in Confluence.\
\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/team-calendars-1477576606.html)

\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/add-ons-and-integrations-1477576789.html)

\
View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/confluence-use-cases-1477576828.html)

\

\

============================\

Permissions and restrictions
============================

#### Related content

*   No related content found

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

As a tool for communication and collaboration, we believe Confluence is at its best when everyone can participate fully. Confluence keeps a history of all changes to pages and other content, so it's easy to see who has changed what, and reverse any changes if you need to.

Confluence does, however, give you the choice to make your site, spaces, and pages as open or closed as you want to.

Levels of permission
--------------------

There are three levels of permissions in Confluence:

*   [global permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/permissions-and-restrictions-139557.html#Permissionsandrestrictions-global)
    
*   [space permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/permissions-and-restrictions-139557.html#Permissionsandrestrictions-space)
    , and 
*   [page restrictions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/permissions-and-restrictions-139557.html#Permissionsandrestrictions-page)
    .

**On this page:**

**Related pages:**

*   [Confluence Groups](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/confluence-groups-139478.html)
    
*   [Global Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/global-permissions-overview-138709.html)
    
*   [Space Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/space-permissions-overview-139521.html)
    
*   [Page restrictions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/page-restrictions-139414.html)
    
*   [Configuring Confluence Security](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/configuring-confluence-security-151515.html)
    

### Global permissions

[Global permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/global-permissions-overview-138709.html)
 are site-wide permissions, and are assigned by a Confluence administrator or system administrator.

Global permissions cover things like whether a user can log in or create a space. They don't really interact with space permissions or page restrictions.

For full details, check out the [Global Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/global-permissions-overview-138709.html)
.

### Space permissions

Every space has its [own independent set of permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/space-permissions-overview-139521.html)
, managed by the space administrators, which determine the access settings for different users and groups.

They can be used to [grant or revoke permission](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/assign-space-permissions-139460.html)
 to view, add, edit, and delete content within that space, and can be applied to groups, users, and even to anonymous users (people who aren't logged in) if need be.

For full details, check out the [Space Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/space-permissions-overview-139521.html)
.

tip/resting Created with Sketch.

One thing to watch out for is where a user is a member of multiple groups. You may have revoked permission for that individual user to add pages, for example, but if they're a member of a group that _is_ allowed to add pages, they'll still be able to create new pages in the space.

If you can't get the result you want from space permissions, or you're not sure, check with one of your Confluence administrators to determine what permissions you should apply to individuals and groups.

  

### Page restrictions

Page restrictions work a little differently to global and space permissions. **Pages are open for viewing or editing by default**, but you can [restrict either viewing or editing](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/page-restrictions-139414.html)
 to certain users or groups if you need to. Page restrictions can be applied to published or unpublished pages and blog posts (drafts). 

Don't forget, every page in Confluence lives within a space, and space permissions allow the [space admin](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/space-permissions-overview-139521.html)
 to revoke permission to view content for the whole space. Even the ability to apply restrictions to pages is controlled by the 'restrict pages' space permission.

For full details, check out [Page restrictions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/page-restrictions-139414.html)
.

How do permissions and restrictions interact?
---------------------------------------------

You can restrict viewing of a page or blog post to certain users or groups, so that even if someone has the 'view' permission for the space, they won't be able to view the content of the page or blog post.

If someone is a space admin and you've used page restrictions to prevent them viewing a page, they won't be able to see the page when they navigate to it. As a space admin though, they can see a list of restricted pages in the space and remove the restrictions. 

Check who can view a page
-------------------------

To check who can view a page, go to 

**More options** ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/files/704577869/704577870/2/1517274795952/ellipsis.png)

 > **People who can view**. This will show a list of people, including administrators, who have space permission to see the space, and are not prevented by page restrictions from seeing the page.  See [Check who can view a page](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/check-who-can-view-a-page-992678937.html)
 for more information. 

The diagram below shows the points at which someone could be prevented from viewing a page. 

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/files/139557/992678933/5/1587357848300/permissions-restrictions-bar-chart.png)

*   **Site** - they don't have permission to log in to Confluence.
*   **Space** - they don't have permission to view the space.
*   **Parent page** - the current page has a view restriction preventing them from viewing.
*   **Child page** - the current page is inheriting a view restriction from another page higher up in the page hierarchy, preventing them from viewing.  

  

**What about links?**

Space permissions and page restrictions affect how [links between Confluence pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/links-776656293.html)
 are displayed.

*   If someone doesn't have 'View' space permission, links to pages in that space will be visible, but they'll get a "page not found" message. The space key is not revealed in the link URL. 
*   If someone has the "View" space permission, but the page has view restrictions, the link will be visible but they'll get an "access denied" message when they click the link. 

[Links to attachments](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/links-776656293.html)
 are also affected. If the visitor doesn't have permission to view the page the attachment lives on, the link won't be rendered.

Last modified on Mar 3, 2026

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Confluence has a wide range of features on its own, but you can also extend those features with Marketplace apps, and by integrating Confluence with other applications. Integrating with [Jira](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/use-jira-applications-and-confluence-together-427623543.html)
 applications can really take your Confluence experience to the next level by improving the way your teams track vital work, and plan and release new products.

If there's an extra piece of functionality you need, the [Atlassian Marketplace](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/)
 is the place to look for useful [Confluence apps](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/home/confluence)
. Whether you need to create diagrams, like the ones you can create with [Gliffy](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/plugins/com.gliffy.integration.confluence)
, or you want to make awesome mockups and wireframes with [Balsamiq](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/plugins/com.balsamiq.confluence.plugins.mockups)
, there are heaps of great apps in the marketplace. You may even find a really useful app you never knew you needed, but now can't live without.

**In this section:**

*   [Use Jira applications and Confluence together](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/use-jira-applications-and-confluence-together-427623543.html)
    
*   [Request Marketplace Apps](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/request-marketplace-apps-313460777.html)
    
*   [Use a WebDAV Client to Work with Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/use-a-webdav-client-to-work-with-pages-200704169.html)
    
*   [Mail Archives](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/mail-archives-139502.html)
    

**Related pages:**

*   [Use Jira applications and Confluence together](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/use-jira-applications-and-confluence-together-427623543.html)

*   [Request Marketplace Apps](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/request-marketplace-apps-313460777.html)

Confluence use-cases
====================

#### Confluence Data Center documentation

Analytics
=========

#### Confluence Data Center documentation

#### Related content

*   No related content found

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

This feature is available with a Confluence Data Center license.

Use analytics data to understand how your site, spaces, and pages are performing, and how active your users are. You can also export reports to share with your team.

**On this page:**

Analytics data is useful for identifying:

*   most viewed content  
*   usage and activity trends across your site 
*   frequent contributors and viewers 
*   spaces with few views that could potentially be archived. 

If required, you can set who can view analytics on your site and spaces. 

How to access analytics
-----------------------

Analytics is only available with a Confluence Data Center license. 

Your administrator may have limited who can view analytics reports. See [Administer analytics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/administer-analytics-1044780575.html)
 for more information. 

### Access Analytics from the Confluence navigation

When you access analytics from the navigation, you get an overview of your site's usage and access to insights on spaces, users, and searches.

Go to Analytics in the Confluence header. 

See [View insights on your site](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/view-insights-on-your-site-1044780335.html)
 for more information. 

### Access Analytics from a space

When you access analytics from a space, you get an overview of all the pages and attached files in that space. 

Go to Analytics in the space sidebar. 

See [View insights on spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/view-insights-on-spaces-1044780337.html)
 for more information. 

### Access Analytics from a page

When you access analytics from a page, you can get a quick summary of insights on your page, such as total page views. From there, you can view more detailed page analytics, such as which users have viewed the page, which versions of the page they viewed, and more.

At the top of any page, click  Analytics.  Click View analytics to see the detailed analytics about your page.

See [View insights on pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/view-insights-on-pages-1044780341.html)
 for more information. 

Limit who can view analytics reports for specific spaces
--------------------------------------------------------

You can further limit who can view analytics reports for specific spaces. You need Space Administrator permission to do this. 

To change who can see analytics reports in a space:

1.  Go to the space and select **Space tools** > **Permissions** from the bottom of the sidebar.
2.  Select the **Analytics Permissions** tab. 
3.  Select **Viewing Analytics Restricted** from the drop down.
4.  Enter the users and/or groups you want to allow to view analytics reports. 
5.  Save your changes. 

Good to know:

*   If a Confluence administrator has denied a group permission to view analytics, adding the group at the space level will not grant this permission. 
*   The space will still appear in the site analytics report (if the user has permission to see the space, and use analytics globally), but they will be prevented from viewing the space analytics report if they don't have space permissions.

Analytics for Confluence marketplace app
----------------------------------------

If you previously installed the Good Software Analytics for Confluence marketplace app, you can continue to use the app until it [reaches end of life](https://confluence.atlassian.com/support/atlassian-end-of-support-policy-201851003.html)
, but your version may not contain the same features. 

  

**Analytics for Confluence** **is now part of Confluence Data Center**  
To get access to the features described on this page upgrade to Confluence Data Center 7.11 or later. [See our FAQ for all the details](https://www.atlassian.com/migration/faqs#data-center)

Last modified on Aug 22, 2023

=================

#### On this page

#### In this section

*   [Using the Confluence Data Center and Server mobile app](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/using-the-confluence-data-center-and-server-mobile-app-947169252.html)
    
*   [Using Confluence via your mobile browser](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/using-confluence-via-your-mobile-browser-947169262.html)
    
*   [Mobile Device Management (MDM)](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/mobile-device-management-mdm-993923036.html)
    
*   [Configure OAuth 2.0 for Confluence iOS](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/configure-oauth-2-0-for-confluence-ios-1442846594.html)
    

#### Related content

*   No related content found

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

There are two ways to stay connected to your team's work in Confluence while you're out and about – the Confluence Data Center and Server mobile app for iOS and Android, or via your device's browser. 

If you're a Confluence user, check out the two ways you can use Confluence on your device. 

*   [Using the Confluence Data Center mobile app](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/using-the-confluence-data-center-and-server-mobile-app-947169252.html)
     (recommended)
*   [Using Confluence via your mobile browser](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/using-confluence-via-your-mobile-browser-947169262.html)
     (no longer in maintenance)

**On this page:**

### What you'll need

### Confluence requirements

In order for your users to try the Confluence Data Center mobile app, you will need to:

*   upgrade your Confluence site to ****Confluence 6.8**** or later
*   allow users to access your site on their device (if your site isn't accessible on the public internet, people will need be connected to your network or use a [VPN](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/confluence-mobile-284365706.html#ConfluenceMobile-vpn)
    )

### Device requirements

In order to use the app, your users will need a device with either:

*   Android 10 or later, or 
*   iOS 17 or later (iPhone, iPad)

Users will need to log in to use the app, even if your site allows anonymous users.

Considerations for administrators
---------------------------------

Here are some things to consider when determining whether your users will be able to use the app.

#### VPN and firewalls

If your Confluence site is not accessible on the public internet, users will need to connect their device to your network or virtual private network (VPN) in order to use the app.

We recommend providing your users with step-by-step instructions on how to connect to your VPN when you let them know the mobile app is available, as this is something Atlassian Support will not be able to help them with.  

The mobile app will also attempt to check the compatibility of your site prior to presenting the login screen. If you've configured a custom filter to prevent unauthenticated requests to your server, you will need to change it to allow `<confluence-base-url>/rest/nativemobile/1.0/info/login`  to pass through without authentication.

#### HTTPS and certificate requirements

In the latest version of the iOS and Android apps, you can connect to the app using either HTTP or HTTPS.

If you're using HTTPS your proxy must allow TLS 1.2 traffic. This is an iOS requirement that we've chosen to implement for both the iOS and Android apps to prevent confusion (for example where one device can log in, and another cannot).

Ideally, your certificate should be from a trusted Certificate Authority. If you have certificate that is self-signed, or from an unknown Certificate Authority (for example, you are your own CA), users may still be able to use the app by manually installing your certificate on their device. See our [Knowledge base article](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/can-t-get-a-secure-connection-error-on-confluence-data-center-and-server-mobile-app-940695063.html)
 for more information on how to do this. 

iOS 13 introduced a number of other requirements that your certificate will need to meet if your users will be using the app on iOS devices. See [Requirements for trusted certificates in iOS 13](https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT210176)
. 

#### Login and authentication

The app supports all common Confluence user management configurations, including external user directories and SAML single sign-on. Users will need to sign in to use the app, even if your site allows anonymous access.  

Starting from Confluence Data Center iOS mobile app 2.0, you can use the OAuth 2.0 authentication flow to:

*   improve your Confluence’s security
*   improve session restoration
*   support Fast IDentity Online 2 (FIDO2) and other certificate-based authentication standards.

For more information, see [Configure OAuth 2.0 for Confluence iOS](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/configure-oauth-2-0-for-confluence-ios-1442846594.html)
.

User-Agent header format

The app uses an [RFC-compliant](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-5.5.3)
 user-agent header for making the requests during login. This allows us to effectively identify the app and apply further processing based on the user’s hardware and software.

[User-Agent format examples...](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/confluence-mobile-284365706.html#)

Given the wide variety of supported configurations we decided to adopt the most versatile format. For both iOS and Android we compose the User-Agent header value by concatenating the standard WebView User-Agent and unique app identifier.

**iOS User-Agent**

Format

    Mozilla/5.0 (<system-information>) <platform> (<platform-details>) Safari/<technical-version> AtlassianMobileApp

Example

    Mozilla/5.0 (iPhone; CPU iPhone OS 14_7 like Mac OS X) AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) Safari/604.1 AtlassianMobileApp

**Android User-Agent**

Format

    Mozilla/5.0 (<system-information>) <platform> (<platform-details>) AtlassianMobileApp

Example

    Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 9; Mi A1 Build/PKQ1.180917.001) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) AtlassianMobileApp

#### Storage and encryption

The iOS and Android apps cache some content (spaces, pages, attachments) locally on the user's device. This helps keep the app responsive when navigating around pages and spaces. We don't use any application-level encryption when storing cached data, but the device's internal storage may be encrypted by the operating system.  

When a user logs out, all cached data is deleted. 

We don't store passwords in the app. Instead we use session cookies, which are encrypted by default. 

#### Session timeout

Mobile apps rely only on the 401 response status code to recognize a session timeout event. If your environment has a proxy, make sure that it doesn’t override the response status code (e.g. in case of ADFS you may run into a redirect to [login.microsoft.com](http://login.microsoft.com/)
, a 3xx status code, when your session expires). Otherwise, you might see errors like `No connection`  or `Can't show the issue/page`  when your session expires, and you should log in again explicitly to resolve them.

#### Mobile Device Management (MDM)

You can distribute the Confluence Data Center app to people in your organisation using your MDM solution. For more info on how to do this, see [Mobile Device Management](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/mobile-device-management-mdm-993923036.html)
. 

#### Marketplace apps, themes, and visual customizations

The mobile app provides a simple, lightweight way for users to view, create, edit and collaborate on pages. Complex interactions, including those provided by Marketplace apps, such as blueprints, calendars, workflows will not be available in the app. Some third party macros may be available, depending on whether the third-party app supports rendering these macros on mobile. 

Any theming or look and feel customizations you've made to your site will not be reflected in the mobile app. 

#### Cloud services

In order to provide push notifications to users' devices, we have developed a cloud-based notification service. This service is developed and maintained by Atlassian, and is hosted on our AWS infrastructure (AWS SNS). See [Push notifications service](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/confluence-mobile-284365706.html#ConfluenceMobile-push)
 below for more information. 

This is the only cloud-based service used by the app.

#### Push notifications service

The Confluence Data Center mobile app can push notifications directly to users' devices. Users choose whether they'd like to receive push notifications from the app, and can opt out at any time. This feature uses a cloud-based notifications service developed and maintained by Atlassian and hosted on our AWS infrastructure. No user or message content is sent to the service, only notification IDs, and we don't store any data.

If you need to avoid using any cloud-based services you can choose to disable push notifications entirely. Head to **Administration** menu ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/files/747833194/747833195/2/1517274813281/cog.png) , then **General Configuration** > **Mobile apps**.

If you're using restrictive firewall or proxy server settings, you'll need to allow (whitelist)  `https://mobile-server-push-notification.atlassian.com`  to ensure push notifications work as expected.

For sites that are not accessible on the public internet (for example users need to be connected via VPN to use the app) we adapt the push notification message as follows:

*   If the user is connected to your network or VPN, we'll show the full notification, for example "Sara Leung shared 'End of year party' with you"
*   If the user is not currently connected to your network or VPN, we'll show a shorter notification, for example "1 new notification".

#### Mobile web and linking directly to pages

It is not possible to go directly from a link, for example in an email notification, to the app. To help with this limitation, when people land on a Confluence page in their device's browser, they'll see the **Open in app** button. Tapping this prompt will open the app, if they have it installed, or take them to the App or Play store to download it. 

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/files/284365706/947169249/1/1521512603982/open-in-app-mobile-web.png)

If you don't want this button to display in mobile web, you'll need to disable the entire Confluence Mobile plugin, which is required to use the mobile app. There is currently a known issue with this workaround. See  [CONFSERVER-57423](https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/CONFSERVER-57423)
 - Getting issue details... STATUS  for details.

Limitations and known issues
----------------------------

### Not all macros are available

Not all macros will display in the app or mobile web. If a macro can't be displayed, you'll see the message below, and have the option to tap through to the desktop version of the page, in your device's browser. 

_Screenshot: Error that appears when a macro is not rendered in Confluence mobile_

![Error that appears when a macro is not rendered in Confluence mobile](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/files/284365706/431521991/5/1521512602543/ConfluenceMobileMacro-trunc-50pc.PNG)

### Administrators can disable Confluence mobile on your site

If you're not able to use the mobile app or mobile web, it may be because your administrator has disabled one or both of the following system apps:

*   **Mobile plugin for Confluence Data Center** (formerly called **Confluence mobile plugin**). This is required to use the mobile app.
*   **Confluence mobile web plugin.** This is required to use mobile web.

  

Disabling the Mobile plugin for Confluence Data Center (formerly known as 'Confluence Mobile Plugin') will also disable all the modules of the Workbox - Host Plugin plugin. This issue is being tracked at [CONFSERVER-40782](https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/CONFSERVER-40782)
 - Getting issue details... STATUS

### Seperate Cloud and Data Center mobile apps

While the functionality of the two mobile apps is very similar, you will need to download the **Confluence Data Center and Server mobile app** to be able to authenticate with a Data Center site. You can't use the [Confluence Cloud](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confcloud/get-the-confluence-cloud-mobile-app-724764728.html)
 app with a Confluence Data Center site or vice versa. 

### Troubleshooting

Having other problems with the app? See [Confluence Server and Data Center mobile apps](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/confluence-server-and-data-center-mobile-apps-938025496.html)
 in the knowledge base. 

Last modified on Jan 8, 2026

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Confluence gives you a few ways to find what you're looking for. Here's an overview of Confluence search, and a few tips to help you find things more easily.  

How Confluence search works
---------------------------

When you enter a search term, Confluence looks for content in all spaces (including personal spaces), pages, mail, personal profiles, and space descriptions. It also looks at the content of some attached file types (Word, Text, PowerPoint, Excel, PDF, and HTML).

Search results are based on your Confluence permissions, so you'll only see content you're allowed to view.

[Learn how Confluence ranks search results](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/ranking-of-search-results-1188406620.html)

**On this page:**

**Related pages:**

*   [Confluence Search Syntax](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/confluence-search-syntax-158720.html)
    
*   [Confluence Search Fields](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/confluence-search-fields-161188.html)
    
*   [Ranking of Search Results](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/ranking-of-search-results-1188406620.html)
    
*   [Recently Viewed Pages and Blog Posts](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/recently-viewed-pages-and-blog-posts-179440601.html)
    
*   [Search Results Macro](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/search-results-macro-139511.html)
    
*   [Livesearch Macro](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/livesearch-macro-163415902.html)
    

*   [Search the People Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/search-the-people-directory-201706.html)
    

  

Start a search
--------------

To search Confluence:

1.  Click the search field in the top-right of Confluence to open the search panel.
2.  Start typing your search term.

Results will appear as you type — you don't need to hit enter. 

We exclude comments from your search results unless you select the comment option from the type filter.

_Screenshot: the search panel_

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/files/139528/965544637/2/1552538714726/Confluence-search-results.png)

1.  **Search filters** – refine your results by space, contributor, type, date, label, or space category.
2.  **Advanced search** – go the the advanced search page.
3.  **Search tips** – get search help, and tips for refining your search.

tip/resting Created with Sketch.

**Tip:** Type **/** on your keyboard to quickly open the search panel.

Filter your search results
--------------------------

You can refine your search using interactive filters on the left-hand side of the search panel.

### Search within a space

Use the **space** filter to find content within a particular space or list of spaces. The space you're currently in will appear at the top of the list by default. Start typing the space name and choose from the list of suggested spaces.

Click the ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/files/139528/965544642/1/1548117071222/search-archived-spaces-toggle.png) toggle to search within archived spaces.

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/files/139528/965544651/2/1552538716400/space-dropdown-filter.png)

### Search for content by a specific person

Use the **contributor** filter to restrict your search to content modified (created, updated, or commented on) by particular people. Start typing the person's username, or part of their name, and we'll show you a list of possible matches. You can add as many people as you like.

tip/resting Created with Sketch.

**Tip:** To search for your own work, click the **Contributor** filter, then select your profile from the drop-down menu. Your name appears here by default, so it's easy to find.

### Filter by content type

Use the **type** filter to only show content of a certain type, such as pages, blog posts, comments or user profiles.

### Search within a specific time frame

Use the **date** filter to search for content last modified (created, updated, or commented on) within a particular period of time. 

To search within a specific date range:

1.  Click **Advanced Search** on the left-side of the search panel.
2.  In the **Last modified** section, choose **Custom**.
3.  Select the date range from the drop-down date picker.

### Search for content with a specific label

Use the **label** filter to search for content containing a specific label. Start typing the name of the label and choose from the list of possible matches.

### Search within a space category

Use the space category filter to search within a group of related spaces. Start typing the category name and choose from the list of possible matches. You can browse existing categories from the [Space directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf614/the-space-directory-965545160.html)
.

tip/resting Created with Sketch.

**Tip:** Space admins can organize spaces into categories. You can create space categories for departments, subject areas, office locations — whatever works for your team. [Learn how to create a space category](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/use-labels-to-categorize-spaces-136647.html)

  

Search for admin options
------------------------

As a Confluence admin, you can can quickly access admin options from the search panel.

Start typing what you want to do. We'll show the top three matching admin items at the top of your search results. You'll only see options you have permission to perform.

If you apply a search filter, admin items will no longer appear in your results.

Advanced search
---------------

The advanced search page allows you to add more search filters, such as creator, title, date range or ancestor page. 

To use advanced search:

1.  Click the search field at the top-right of Confluence.
2.  Click **Advanced search** on the left-side of the search panel.
3.  Type your keyword in the search field and hit enter.

### Adding a filter from the advanced search page

To add a search filter from the advanced search page:

1.  Click **Add a filter** on the bottom left of the advanced search page.
2.  From the drop-down menu, select the relevant filter.

_Screenshot: advanced search page_

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/files/139528/965544636/1/1548117069886/advanced-search.png)

1.  **Search filters** – narrow your search by adding filters.
2.  **Add a filter** – add more filters for even more precise search.

You can choose from these additional filters:

*   **Creator** – Restrict your search to content created by a particular person. Start typing the person's username or part of their name and Confluence will offer you a list of possible matches.
*   **Label** – Only search for content containing specific labels.
*   **With parent** – Only search for direct children of a specific parent page.
*   **With ancestor** – Only search for pages below a certain page in the hierarchy.
*   **Created** – Choose or enter a date to only show content created within a particular period of time.
*   **Mentioning user** – Only search for content that mentions a particular Confluence user.
*   **With title** – Only search within page or blog titles.

These filters are provided by [Confluence Query Language (CQL)](https://developer.atlassian.com/server/confluence/advanced-searching-using-cql/)
.

Advanced search syntax
----------------------

You can also refine your search using Confluence search syntax. These are words or symbols you enter into the search field to help narrow down your results. Learn more about [Confluence Search Syntax](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/confluence-search-syntax-158720.html)
. 

Last modified on Nov 29, 2022

Get started
===========

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Welcome to the Confluence getting started documentation. In this section, you'll find tutorials and other information that'll be useful for evaluating Confluence, and getting to know it when you're starting out.

Teams in Space
--------------

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/files/777010817/777010818/1/1439878470494/tis-header.jpg)

For each tutorial in this section, we'll use a fictional organization known as 'Teams in Space'. Their mission is to:

> _"Perform flight research and technology integration to revolutionize aviation and pioneer aerospace technology. Also, land the first humans on Mars by 2020."_

You're an astronaut in the 'See Space EZ' team, which is working on the upcoming colonization of Mars.

Go ahead – dive into the tutorials, and let us show you around Confluence and some of its handy features.

*   [Tutorial: Navigate Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/tutorial-navigate-confluence-251005338.html)
    *   [The dashboard](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/the-dashboard-139374.html)
        
    *   [The space directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/the-space-directory-777010843.html)
        
    *   [The space sidebar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/the-space-sidebar-777010845.html)
        
    *   [Keyboard shortcuts](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/keyboard-shortcuts-139456.html)
        
    *   [Complete your mission](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/complete-your-mission-777010857.html)
        
*   [Tutorial: Space ace](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/tutorial-space-ace-777010865.html)
    *   [Create a project space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/create-a-project-space-777010867.html)
        
    *   [Create your personal space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/create-your-personal-space-777010869.html)
        
    *   [Create the team's PR space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/create-the-team-s-pr-space-777010871.html)
        
    *   [Delete and archive spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/delete-and-archive-spaces-777010873.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Share your team's PDFs, Office documents, images, and more in one place by uploading your files to Confluence. Automatic versioning, instant previews, permissions, and full-text search, means shared network drives can be a thing of the past for your team.

When you upload a file it is _attached_ to the current page or blog post. This is why files are often referred to as _attachments_ in Confluence. 

You can attach anything from project plans and design mockups to video and audio files. You and your colleagues can also collaborate by [commenting on files](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/share-and-comment-on-files-704578253.html)
 displayed on Confluence pages.

Using Files
-----------

*   [Upload Files](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/upload-files-139513.html)
    
*   [Display Files and Images](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/display-files-and-images-245827130.html)
    
*   [Manage Files](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/manage-files-139415.html)
    
*   [Share and Comment on Files](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/share-and-comment-on-files-704578253.html)
    
*   [Edit Files](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/edit-files-170494553.html)
    
*   [Edit in Office using the Office Connector](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/edit-in-office-using-the-office-connector-170493096.html)
    
*   [Install Atlassian Companion](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/install-atlassian-companion-992678880.html)
    

**On this page:**

**Related pages:**

*   [Configuring Attachment Size](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/configuring-attachment-size-150040.html)
    
*   [Configuring your Attachment Storage](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/attachment-storage-configuration-166876.html)
    

Permissions
-----------

The 'Add Attachment' and 'Delete Attachment' permissions are used to control who can upload and delete attachments in a space.

Users with 'Add Page' or 'Add Blog' permissions can insert existing attachments to their pages, but not upload new attachments unless they also have the 'Add Attachment' permission.  

There is no permission that controls downloading attachments. See our knowledge base article about [disabling the download of attachments](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/how-to-disable-attachment-downloads-215484007.html)
 if you need to do this.

Last modified on Mar 1, 2017

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Confluence is very flexible – not only in the many ways you can create and share content, but also in how you can tailor your own Confluence experience. Things like your [profile picture](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/set-your-profile-picture-203199.html)
, [favorite spaces and pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/save-for-later-136552.html)
, and your [personal space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/create-a-space-139463.html)
 can say a lot about you, and can also make navigating Confluence much quicker and easier. Even a simple thing like [adding shortcut links to the sidebar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/configure-the-sidebar-317194694.html)
 of your personal space, can save you a lot of time in finding the things you use all the time.

[Set up your personal space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/create-a-space-139463.html)
, and take a look at any of the pages below, to start making Confluence feel like home.

*   [Your user profile](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/your-user-profile-139549.html)
    
*   [Change Your Password](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/change-your-password-139416.html)
    
*   [Edit Your User Settings](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/edit-your-user-settings-1016023.html)
    
*   [Set Your Profile Picture](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/set-your-profile-picture-203199.html)
    
*   [Choose Your Home Page](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/choose-your-home-page-139452.html)
    
*   [Save for later](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/save-for-later-136552.html)
    
*   [View and revoke OAuth access tokens](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/view-and-revoke-oauth-access-tokens-208961965.html)
    
*   [Set and use your preferred theme](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/set-and-use-your-preferred-theme-1425051235.html)
    

**Related pages:**

*   [Watch Pages, Spaces and Blogs](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-139579.html)
    
*   [Create a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/create-a-space-139463.html)
    
*   [Save for later](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/save-for-later-136552.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Confluence is all about encouraging team collaboration to get the best results, so we've built in a number of ways you can notify other people about content that may be of interest to them.

You can:

*   [Work together with your team](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/collaborative-editing-858771779.html)
     on a page or blog and see their changes in real time
*   [Share a link](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/share-a-page-or-blog-post-16482511.html)
     to a page or blog post via email
*   [Mention](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/mentions-251725350.html)
     a user when you write a page, blog post, comment, or add a task
*   [Like](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/likes-and-popular-content-278695530.html)
     a page, blog post or comment

Whenever you mention another user, they'll receive an email notification; if you like a page, blog post, or comment, the author will be notified that you _like_ the content.

Other users can also find out about changes to content in Confluence by [watching](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-139579.html)
 pages and spaces.

Another way to share Confluence content is by [exporting it to other formats](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml-139475.html)
 such as XML, HTML, Microsoft Word and PDF.

**Related pages:**

*   [Pages and blogs](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/pages-and-blogs-320602215.html)
    
*   [Watch Pages, Spaces and Blogs](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-139579.html)
    
*   [Export Content to Word, PDF, HTML and XML](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml-139475.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

What is a space?
----------------

Spaces are Confluence's way of organizing content into meaningful categories. Think of it like having different folders into which you can put your work.

  
Spaces come in two main varieties:

*   **Site spaces** – These spaces are found in the **[Space Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/the-space-directory-777010843.html)** and are the areas where you create content and collaborate with others. They are sometimes called global spaces.
*   **Personal spaces** – Every Confluence user can set up a personal space which they can keep private or make public so others can view and edit. Personal spaces are listed in the **[People Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/search-the-people-directory-201706.html)** and found under your personal profile.

How do I use a space?
---------------------

**On this page:**

**Related pages:**

*   [Create a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/create-a-space-139463.html)
    
*   [Space Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/space-permissions-overview-139521.html)
    
*   [Navigate Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/navigate-spaces-829076190.html)
    
*   [Organize your Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/organize-your-space-829076206.html)
    
*   [Customize your Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/customize-your-space-139423.html)
    
*   [Archive a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/archive-a-space-284368719.html)
    
*   [Export Content to Word, PDF, HTML and XML](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml-139475.html)
    
*   [Delete a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/delete-a-space-139524.html)
    

**[Create](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/create-a-space-139463.html)
 as many spaces as you need to get things done:**

*   **Team spaces** – Give each team (QA, HR, Engineering, Support, ...) their own space so they can focus and make their information easier for everyone to find.
*   **Project spaces** – Put all the information related to your project in one place. This allows everyone to work together in Confluence instead of emailing back and forth.
*   **Personal space** – Store everything you're working on individually, keep your to-do lists, and polish any content before you move it into another shared space.

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/files/139459/829076177/2/1504658273643/dashboard-space-page-hierarchy_02.png)

**Stay up to date with spaces**

*   [Watch a space to stay up-to-date with any changes](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-139579.html)
    .
    
*   [Add important spaces to your dashboard so that they're easy to find again](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/organize-your-space-829076206.html#OrganizeyourSpace-my)
    .

**Administer spaces**  
If you have admin permissions for a space you can:

*   [Change the](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/customize-your-space-139423.html)
     [colors, logo, sidebar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/customize-your-space-139423.html)
     [and](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/set-up-a-space-home-page-829076213.html)
     [homepage](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/set-up-a-space-home-page-829076213.html)
     [of that space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/set-up-a-space-home-page-829076213.html)
    .
*   [Control who can view and edit your space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/space-permissions-overview-139521.html)
    .
*   [Make your space public to share it with people who don't have access to Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/make-a-space-public-829076202.html)
    .

**Space Permissions**  
Some things we should make clear about space admin permissions:

*   The person who creates a space automatically has admin permissions for that space.
*   Space admins can grant admin permissions to others.
*   Space admins don't have to be Confluence admins and can have special permissions for a single space. For example, you are the admin for your personal space, no matter what kind of access you have anywhere else.

Want more ideas for using spaces? Check out our kickass guides on how to:

*   [Use Confluence as a Knowledge Base](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/use-confluence-as-a-knowledge-base-218275154.html)
    
*   [Develop Technical Documentation in Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/develop-technical-documentation-in-confluence-226166494.html)
    
*   [Use Confluence as your Intranet](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/use-confluence-as-your-intranet-230819424.html)
    
*   [Use Confluence for Software Teams](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/confluence-for-software-teams-776656419.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Pages and blog posts allow you to capture and share information in Confluence.

Whether it's taking down some quick [notes from a meeting](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/meeting-notes-blueprint-329975386.html)
, writing a [requirements page](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/product-requirements-blueprint-329975392.html)
, or letting your teammates know about the company's latest marketing push – you can create it as a Confluence page or blog post.

[Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/create-and-edit-pages-139476.html)
 are great for when you want the information to last and evolve over time. If it's a point-in-time update or one-time communication then a [blog post](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/blog-posts-834222533.html)
 is the way to go. These aren't hard-and-fast rules; they're just pointers to give you a place to start.

Each Confluence space, including your personal space, allows you to create pages in it, and has its own blog where you can create posts. If you're not sure what a space is, or what you can do with spaces, check out our page on [Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/spaces-139459.html)
.

**Related pages:**

*   [Create and Edit Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/create-and-edit-pages-139476.html)

*   [Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/spaces-139459.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

**Team Calendars for Confluence** **is now part of Confluence Data Center**  
To get access to the features described on this page upgrade to Confluence Data Center 7.11 or later. Can’t upgrade yet? Depending on your current Data Center version, you can access these features by installing the latest version of the app (at no cost). [See our FAQ for all the details](https://www.atlassian.com/migration/faqs#data-center)

Team Calendars provides one place for fast-moving teams working in Confluence to connect their schedules of leave, Jira projects and events, or whatever use case your team has.

*   [Team Calendars Quick Tour](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/team-calendars-quick-tour-1044780350.html)
    
*   [Create, Add, and Edit Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/create-add-and-edit-calendars-1044780370.html)
    
*   [Restrict a Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/restrict-a-calendar-1044780379.html)
    
*   [Watch a Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/watch-a-calendar-1044780432.html)
    
*   [Share Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/share-calendars-1044780439.html)
    
*   [Export Team Calendars Content to Other Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/export-team-calendars-content-to-other-calendars-1044780512.html)
    
*   [Delete or Remove a Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/delete-or-remove-a-calendar-1044780517.html)
    

Create, organize, and find events easily. Team Calendars offers great tools for planning and managing your events, whether they're travel, conferences, birthdays, or JIRA issues and sprints. 

*   [Add Events](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/add-events-1044780384.html)
    
*   [Add Jira Events](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/add-jira-events-1044780393.html)
    
*   [Event Types](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/event-types-1044780411.html)
    
*   [Custom Event Types](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/custom-event-types-1044780415.html)
    
*   [Reminders](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/reminders-1044780420.html)
    
*   [Embed Calendars on Confluence Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/embed-calendars-on-confluence-pages-1044780427.html)
    

Calendar subscriptions provide a simple and effective way to stay up to date with holidays, conferences, and more. 

You can subscribe to team calendars from many of your your favourite third-party calendar apps:

*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Microsoft Outlook](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-microsoft-outlook-1044780452.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Outlook on the web](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-outlook-on-the-web-1044780467.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Apple Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-apple-calendar-1044780468.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Apple iOS Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-apple-ios-calendar-1044780473.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Google Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-google-calendar-1044780478.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Android Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-android-calendar-1044780488.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Thunderbird](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-thunderbird-1044780491.html)
    

Alternatively, subscribe to a third-party calendar in Team Calendars:

*   [Subscribe to Outlook Calendar from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/subscribe-to-outlook-calendar-from-team-calendars-1044780495.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Apple Calendar from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/subscribe-to-apple-calendar-from-team-calendars-1044780497.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Google Calendar from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/subscribe-to-google-calendar-from-team-calendars-1044780500.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Opsgenie Calendars from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/subscribe-to-opsgenie-calendars-from-team-calendars-1044780505.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to PagerDuty Schedules from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/subscribe-to-pagerduty-schedules-from-team-calendars-1044780507.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Teamup Calendars from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/subscribe-to-teamup-calendars-from-team-calendars-1044780509.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Macros are how you supercharge your Confluence pages. 

You can use macros to:

*   change the format and layout of your page
*   display media like video, audio, and social media content
*   collate and organise Confluence pages, blogs, and files
*   perform actions from a page, such as creating a page from a template. 

Take your Confluence space to the next level using macros. 

**On this page:**

_Screenshot: Page containing_ _Status, Page Properties Report, Livesearch, and Profile Picture macros to_ _help people find information about particular projects._

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/files/139387/1005333502/1/1589961742968/many-macros-view.png)

Macro basics
------------

### Add a macro to your page

To add a macro to your page:

1.  From the editor toolbar, select **Insert** ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/files/1005333342/1005333343/1/1589961486578/insert-macro-icon.png), then **Other Macros**.
    
2.  Select a macro from the list.
3.  Enter any required parameters.
4.  Choose **Insert**.

In the editor you'll see a placeholder that represents the macro. Once you publish your page, you'll see the macro in its full glory. 

### Edit a macro

Macro parameters are used to change the behavior of a macro.

To change the macro parameters:

1.  In the editor, click the macro placeholder and select **Edit**.  
    ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/files/1005333337/1005333338/1/1589961475281/edit-cheese.png)
2.  Update the parameters as required then select **Insert**.

Head to the documentation for specific macros below for full details of the parameters available in each macro.

Confluence macros
-----------------

Here's a list of macros currently available with Confluence Data Center.

Click a macro name for details of the usage, including optional parameters and examples.

Get more macros
---------------

This documentation provides information on all the macros that are provided with Confluence by default. There may be other macros in your site, from Marketplace Apps, or perhaps developed in-house. 

### Third-party macros from the Atlassian Marketplace

You can find a wide range of Atlassian and third party macros at [The Marketplace](http://marketplace.atlassian.com/)
. These are distributed as apps and can be installed by a Confluence Administrator.

### Create your own macros

Users with System Administrator permissions can create user macros - see [Writing User Macros](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/writing-user-macros-4485.html)
. 

If you want to create something more complex, you can develop your own plugin - see [Writing Confluence Plugins](https://developer.atlassian.com/display/CONFDEV/Writing+Confluence+Plugins)
.

Macro usage statistics
----------------------

Confluence administrators can check how often each macro is used in your site.

Macro usage reporting helps you make informed decisions about your macros. You can view which macros are used most often, grouped by app, and easily distinguish between Atlassian and third-party macros. You’ll also find direct links to manage third-party apps in the Universal Plugin Manager (UPM).

You can identify underused apps, plan for cloud migration, and troubleshoot performance issues without running manual database queries or raising support tickets to understand macro and app usage across your site.

To see how often a macro is used:

1.  Go to the **Administration** menu.
2.  Go to **General** **Configuration**.
3.  Select **Macro Usage**.

![Image of the Macro usage feature in Confluence Administration menu](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/files/139387/1652919654/1/1759138125226/macro-usage.png)

Unknown macro
-------------

In view page mode, disabled macros show a placeholder image:

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/files/139387/1021243789/1/1601356204270/unknown-macro.png)

When macros with body content are disabled, the content will be preserved and display in the editor. 

To display the content in view mode:

1.  In the editor, select the macro and choose **Edit**.
2.  In the macro browser, select **Back.**
3.  Search for a new macro and select it.
4.  Check your content displays in the **Preview** pane.
5.  Select **Insert**.

In the editor, the disabled macro will switch to the new macro and display your content. Update the page to display the content in view mode.

  

tip/resting Created with Sketch.

**Do more with Confluence**

Extend Confluence with one of the hundreds of other macros in the [Atlassian Marketplace](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/search?category=Macros&hosting=server)
, such as:

*   [Composition Tabs & Page Layouts](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/245/composition-tabs-page-layout?hosting=server&tab=overview)
    : Toggle or expand the visible of portions of your pages with the Toggle and Cloak macro
*   [Content Formatting for Confluence](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/247/content-formatting-for-confluence?hosting=server&tab=overview)
    : Over 30 easy-to-use Confluence macros gives you the ability to create better, more engaging content

Last modified on Sep 29, 2025

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

About the Confluence administrator's guide
------------------------------------------

This guide covers features and functions that are only available to administrators. 

For information on creating and administering spaces, See [Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/spaces-139459.html)
.

This guide assumes that you are using the Confluence default theme. If your Confluence site has been customized the header may look different, and menu items appear in different locations to the examples given in this guide. 

*   [Getting Started as Confluence Administrator](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/getting-started-as-confluence-administrator-313465596.html)
    
*   [Manage Users](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/manage-users-151516.html)
    *   [Add and Invite Users](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/add-and-invite-users-138313.html)
        
    *   [Delete or Disable Users](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/delete-or-disable-users-138318.html)
