#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Confluence is very flexible – not only in the many ways you can create and share content, but also in how you can tailor your own Confluence experience. Things like your [profile picture](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/set-your-profile-picture-1652925384.html)
, [favorite spaces and pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/save-for-later-1652925403.html)
, and your [personal space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/create-a-space-1652924459.html)
 can say a lot about you, and can also make navigating Confluence much quicker and easier. Even a simple thing like [adding shortcut links to the sidebar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/configure-the-sidebar-1652924510.html)
 of your personal space, can save you a lot of time in finding the things you use all the time.

[Set up your personal space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/create-a-space-1652924459.html)
, and take a look at any of the pages below, to start making Confluence feel like home.

*   [Your user profile](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/your-user-profile-1652925376.html)
    
*   [Change Your Password](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/change-your-password-1652925379.html)
    
*   [Edit Your User Settings](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/edit-your-user-settings-1652925381.html)
    
*   [Set Your Profile Picture](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/set-your-profile-picture-1652925384.html)
    
*   [Choose Your Home Page](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/choose-your-home-page-1652925398.html)
    
*   [Save for later](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/save-for-later-1652925403.html)
    
*   [View and revoke OAuth access tokens](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/view-and-revoke-oauth-access-tokens-1652925412.html)
    
*   [Set and use your preferred theme](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/set-and-use-your-preferred-theme-1652925415.html)
    

**Related pages:**

*   [Watch Pages, Spaces and Blogs](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-1652925450.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

There are two ways to stay connected to your team's work in Confluence while you're out and about – the Confluence Data Center and Server mobile app for iOS and Android, or via your device's browser. 

If you're a Confluence user, check out the two ways you can use Confluence on your device. 

*   [Using the Confluence Data Center mobile app](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/using-the-confluence-data-center-and-server-mobile-app-1627457012.html)
     (recommended)
*   [Using Confluence via your mobile browser](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/using-confluence-via-your-mobile-browser-1627457022.html)
     (no longer in maintenance)

**On this page:**

### What you'll need

### Confluence requirements

In order for your users to try the Confluence Data Center mobile app, you will need to:

*   upgrade your Confluence site to ****Confluence 6.8**** or later
*   allow users to access your site on their device (if your site isn't accessible on the public internet, people will need be connected to your network or use a [VPN](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/confluence-mobile-1627456998.html#ConfluenceMobile-vpn)
    )

### Device requirements

In order to use the app, your users will need a device with either:

*   Android 10 or later, or 
*   iOS 17 or later (iPhone, iPad)

Users will need to log in to use the app, even if your site allows anonymous users.

Considerations for administrators
---------------------------------

Here are some things to consider when determining whether your users will be able to use the app.

#### VPN and firewalls

If your Confluence site is not accessible on the public internet, users will need to connect their device to your network or virtual private network (VPN) in order to use the app.

We recommend providing your users with step-by-step instructions on how to connect to your VPN when you let them know the mobile app is available, as this is something Atlassian Support will not be able to help them with.  

The mobile app will also attempt to check the compatibility of your site prior to presenting the login screen. If you've configured a custom filter to prevent unauthenticated requests to your server, you will need to change it to allow `<confluence-base-url>/rest/nativemobile/1.0/info/login`  to pass through without authentication.

#### HTTPS and certificate requirements

In the latest version of the iOS and Android apps, you can connect to the app using either HTTP or HTTPS.

If you're using HTTPS your proxy must allow TLS 1.2 traffic. This is an iOS requirement that we've chosen to implement for both the iOS and Android apps to prevent confusion (for example where one device can log in, and another cannot).

Ideally, your certificate should be from a trusted Certificate Authority. If you have certificate that is self-signed, or from an unknown Certificate Authority (for example, you are your own CA), users may still be able to use the app by manually installing your certificate on their device. See our [Knowledge base article](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/can-t-get-a-secure-connection-error-on-confluence-data-center-and-server-mobile-app-940695063.html)
 for more information on how to do this. 

iOS 13 introduced a number of other requirements that your certificate will need to meet if your users will be using the app on iOS devices. See [Requirements for trusted certificates in iOS 13](https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT210176)
. 

#### Login and authentication

The app supports all common Confluence user management configurations, including external user directories and SAML single sign-on. Users will need to sign in to use the app, even if your site allows anonymous access.  

Starting from Confluence Data Center iOS mobile app 2.0, you can use the OAuth 2.0 authentication flow to:

*   improve your Confluence’s security
*   improve session restoration
*   support Fast IDentity Online 2 (FIDO2) and other certificate-based authentication standards.

For more information, see [Configure OAuth 2.0 for Confluence iOS](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/configure-oauth-2-0-for-confluence-ios-1442846594.html)
.

User-Agent header format

The app uses an [RFC-compliant](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-5.5.3)
 user-agent header for making the requests during login. This allows us to effectively identify the app and apply further processing based on the user’s hardware and software.

[User-Agent format examples...](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/confluence-mobile-1627456998.html#)

Given the wide variety of supported configurations we decided to adopt the most versatile format. For both iOS and Android we compose the User-Agent header value by concatenating the standard WebView User-Agent and unique app identifier.

**iOS User-Agent**

Format

    Mozilla/5.0 (<system-information>) <platform> (<platform-details>) Safari/<technical-version> AtlassianMobileApp

Example

    Mozilla/5.0 (iPhone; CPU iPhone OS 14_7 like Mac OS X) AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) Safari/604.1 AtlassianMobileApp

**Android User-Agent**

Format

    Mozilla/5.0 (<system-information>) <platform> (<platform-details>) AtlassianMobileApp

Example

    Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 9; Mi A1 Build/PKQ1.180917.001) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) AtlassianMobileApp

#### Storage and encryption

The iOS and Android apps cache some content (spaces, pages, attachments) locally on the user's device. This helps keep the app responsive when navigating around pages and spaces. We don't use any application-level encryption when storing cached data, but the device's internal storage may be encrypted by the operating system.  

When a user logs out, all cached data is deleted. 

We don't store passwords in the app. Instead we use session cookies, which are encrypted by default. 

#### Session timeout

Mobile apps rely only on the 401 response status code to recognize a session timeout event. If your environment has a proxy, make sure that it doesn’t override the response status code (e.g. in case of ADFS you may run into a redirect to [login.microsoft.com](http://login.microsoft.com/)
, a 3xx status code, when your session expires). Otherwise, you might see errors like `No connection`  or `Can't show the issue/page`  when your session expires, and you should log in again explicitly to resolve them.

#### Mobile Device Management (MDM)

You can distribute the Confluence Data Center app to people in your organisation using your MDM solution. For more info on how to do this, see [Mobile Device Management](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/mobile-device-management-mdm-1627457028.html)
. 

#### Marketplace apps, themes, and visual customizations

The mobile app provides a simple, lightweight way for users to view, create, edit and collaborate on pages. Complex interactions, including those provided by Marketplace apps, such as blueprints, calendars, workflows will not be available in the app. Some third party macros may be available, depending on whether the third-party app supports rendering these macros on mobile. 

Any theming or look and feel customizations you've made to your site will not be reflected in the mobile app. 

#### Cloud services

In order to provide push notifications to users' devices, we have developed a cloud-based notification service. This service is developed and maintained by Atlassian, and is hosted on our AWS infrastructure (AWS SNS). See [Push notifications service](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/confluence-mobile-1627456998.html#ConfluenceMobile-push)
 below for more information. 

This is the only cloud-based service used by the app.

#### Push notifications service

The Confluence Data Center mobile app can push notifications directly to users' devices. Users choose whether they'd like to receive push notifications from the app, and can opt out at any time. This feature uses a cloud-based notifications service developed and maintained by Atlassian and hosted on our AWS infrastructure. No user or message content is sent to the service, only notification IDs, and we don't store any data.

If you need to avoid using any cloud-based services you can choose to disable push notifications entirely. Head to **Administration** menu ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/files/1627456231/1627456232/1/1755867814320/cog.png) , then **General Configuration** > **Mobile apps**.

If you're using restrictive firewall or proxy server settings, you'll need to allow (whitelist)  `https://mobile-server-push-notification.atlassian.com`  to ensure push notifications work as expected.

For sites that are not accessible on the public internet (for example users need to be connected via VPN to use the app) we adapt the push notification message as follows:

*   If the user is connected to your network or VPN, we'll show the full notification, for example "Sara Leung shared 'End of year party' with you"
*   If the user is not currently connected to your network or VPN, we'll show a shorter notification, for example "1 new notification".

#### Mobile web and linking directly to pages

It is not possible to go directly from a link, for example in an email notification, to the app. To help with this limitation, when people land on a Confluence page in their device's browser, they'll see the **Open in app** button. Tapping this prompt will open the app, if they have it installed, or take them to the App or Play store to download it. 

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/files/1627456998/1627456999/1/1755867895727/open-in-app-mobile-web.png)

If you don't want this button to display in mobile web, you'll need to disable the entire Confluence Mobile plugin, which is required to use the mobile app. There is currently a known issue with this workaround. See  [CONFSERVER-57423](https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/CONFSERVER-57423)
 - Getting issue details... STATUS  for details.

Limitations and known issues
----------------------------

### Not all macros are available

Not all macros will display in the app or mobile web. If a macro can't be displayed, you'll see the message below, and have the option to tap through to the desktop version of the page, in your device's browser. 

_Screenshot: Error that appears when a macro is not rendered in Confluence mobile_

![Error that appears when a macro is not rendered in Confluence mobile](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/files/1627456998/1627457007/1/1755867896348/ConfluenceMobileMacro-trunc-50pc.PNG)

### Administrators can disable Confluence mobile on your site

If you're not able to use the mobile app or mobile web, it may be because your administrator has disabled one or both of the following system apps:

*   **Mobile plugin for Confluence Data Center** (formerly called **Confluence mobile plugin**). This is required to use the mobile app.
*   **Confluence mobile web plugin.** This is required to use mobile web.

  

Disabling the Mobile plugin for Confluence Data Center (formerly known as 'Confluence Mobile Plugin') will also disable all the modules of the Workbox - Host Plugin plugin. This issue is being tracked at [CONFSERVER-40782](https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/CONFSERVER-40782)
 - Getting issue details... STATUS

### Seperate Cloud and Data Center mobile apps

While the functionality of the two mobile apps is very similar, you will need to download the **Confluence Data Center and Server mobile app** to be able to authenticate with a Data Center site. You can't use the [Confluence Cloud](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confcloud/get-the-confluence-cloud-mobile-app-724764728.html)
 app with a Confluence Data Center site or vice versa. 

### Troubleshooting

Having other problems with the app? See [Confluence Server and Data Center mobile apps](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/confluence-server-and-data-center-mobile-apps-938025496.html)
 in the knowledge base. 

Last modified on Jan 8, 2026

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=GDPR)

We've created this set of support guides to help you with any GDPR-specific obligations that you may have. The guides are designed to identify where personal data may be located within the product, and, where possible, assist with responding to data subject access requests, including in modifying, restricting or deleting data.

Where a workaround involves running SQL scripts directly on your database, we strongly recommend you do this first on a non-production, staging or test environment before running the scripts on your production database. We also strongly recommend you take a backup of your data before making any modifications via SQL scripts.

#### Related content

*   No related content found

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Confluence is all about encouraging team collaboration to get the best results, so we've built in a number of ways you can notify other people about content that may be of interest to them.

You can:

*   [Work together with your team](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/collaborative-editing-1652924606.html)
     on a page or blog and see their changes in real time
*   [Share a link](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/share-a-page-or-blog-post-1652925435.html)
     to a page or blog post via email
*   [Mention](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/mentions-1652925425.html)
     a user when you write a page, blog post, comment, or add a task
*   [Like](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/likes-and-popular-content-1652925421.html)
     a page, blog post or comment

Whenever you mention another user, they'll receive an email notification; if you like a page, blog post, or comment, the author will be notified that you _like_ the content.

Other users can also find out about changes to content in Confluence by [watching](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-1652925450.html)
 pages and spaces.

Another way to share Confluence content is by [exporting it to other formats](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml-1652924546.html)
 such as XML, HTML, Microsoft Word and PDF.

**Related pages:**

*   [Pages and blogs](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/pages-and-blogs-1652924553.html)
    
*   [Watch Pages, Spaces and Blogs](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-1652925450.html)
    
*   [Export Content to Word, PDF, HTML and XML](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml-1652924546.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Confluence is all about encouraging team collaboration to get the best results, so we've built in a number of ways you can notify other people about content that may be of interest to them.

You can:

*   [Work together with your team](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/collaborative-editing-1627456591.html)
     on a page or blog and see their changes in real time
*   [Share a link](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/share-a-page-or-blog-post-1627457419.html)
     to a page or blog post via email
*   [Mention](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/mentions-1627457409.html)
     a user when you write a page, blog post, comment, or add a task
*   [Like](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/likes-and-popular-content-1627457405.html)
     a page, blog post or comment

Whenever you mention another user, they'll receive an email notification; if you like a page, blog post, or comment, the author will be notified that you _like_ the content.

Other users can also find out about changes to content in Confluence by [watching](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-1627457434.html)
 pages and spaces.

Another way to share Confluence content is by [exporting it to other formats](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml-1627456531.html)
 such as XML, HTML, Microsoft Word and PDF.

**Related pages:**

*   [Pages and blogs](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/pages-and-blogs-1627456538.html)
    
*   [Watch Pages, Spaces and Blogs](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-1627457434.html)
    
*   [Export Content to Word, PDF, HTML and XML](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml-1627456531.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Confluence has a wide range of features on its own, but you can also extend those features with Marketplace apps, and by integrating Confluence with other applications. Integrating with [Jira](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/use-jira-applications-and-confluence-together-1573751215.html)
 applications can really take your Confluence experience to the next level by improving the way your teams track vital work, and plan and release new products.

If there's an extra piece of functionality you need, the [Atlassian Marketplace](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/)
 is the place to look for useful [Confluence apps](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/home/confluence)
. Whether you need to create diagrams, like the ones you can create with [Gliffy](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/plugins/com.gliffy.integration.confluence)
, or you want to make awesome mockups and wireframes with [Balsamiq](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/plugins/com.balsamiq.confluence.plugins.mockups)
, there are heaps of great apps in the marketplace. You may even find a really useful app you never knew you needed, but now can't live without.

**In this section:**

*   [Use Jira applications and Confluence together](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/use-jira-applications-and-confluence-together-1573751215.html)
    
*   [Request Marketplace Apps](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/request-marketplace-apps-1573751222.html)
    
*   [Use a WebDAV Client to Work with Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/use-a-webdav-client-to-work-with-pages-1573751234.html)
    
*   [Mail Archives](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/mail-archives-1573751239.html)
    

**Related pages:**

*   [Use Jira applications and Confluence together](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/use-jira-applications-and-confluence-together-1573751215.html)
    
*   [Use a WebDAV Client to Work with Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/use-a-webdav-client-to-work-with-pages-1573751234.html)
    
*   [Request Marketplace Apps](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/request-marketplace-apps-1573751222.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Confluence has a wide range of features on its own, but you can also extend those features with Marketplace apps, and by integrating Confluence with other applications. Integrating with [Jira](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/use-jira-applications-and-confluence-together-1540722581.html)
 applications can really take your Confluence experience to the next level by improving the way your teams track vital work, and plan and release new products.

If there's an extra piece of functionality you need, the [Atlassian Marketplace](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/)
 is the place to look for useful [Confluence apps](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/home/confluence)
. Whether you need to create diagrams, like the ones you can create with [Gliffy](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/plugins/com.gliffy.integration.confluence)
, or you want to make awesome mockups and wireframes with [Balsamiq](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/plugins/com.balsamiq.confluence.plugins.mockups)
, there are heaps of great apps in the marketplace. You may even find a really useful app you never knew you needed, but now can't live without.

Permissions and restrictions
============================

#### Confluence Data Center documentation

#### Related content

*   No related content found

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

As a tool for communication and collaboration, we believe Confluence is at its best when everyone can participate fully. Confluence keeps a history of all changes to pages and other content, so it's easy to see who has changed what, and reverse any changes if you need to.

Confluence does, however, give you the choice to make your site, spaces, and pages as open or closed as you want to.

Levels of permission
--------------------

There are three levels of permissions in Confluence:

*   [global permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/permissions-and-restrictions-1652925521.html#Permissionsandrestrictions-global)
    
*   [space permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/permissions-and-restrictions-1652925521.html#Permissionsandrestrictions-space)
    , and 
*   [page restrictions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/permissions-and-restrictions-1652925521.html#Permissionsandrestrictions-page)
    .

**On this page:**

**Related pages:**

*   [Confluence Groups](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/confluence-groups-1652925524.html)
    
*   [Global Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/global-permissions-overview-1652925827.html)
    
*   [Space Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/space-permissions-overview-1652924473.html)
    
*   [Page restrictions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/page-restrictions-1652924671.html)
    
*   [Configuring Confluence Security](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/configuring-confluence-security-1652926193.html)
    

### Global permissions

[Global permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/global-permissions-overview-1652925827.html)
 are site-wide permissions, and are assigned by a Confluence administrator or system administrator.

Global permissions cover things like whether a user can log in or create a space. They don't really interact with space permissions or page restrictions.

For full details, check out the [Global Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/global-permissions-overview-1652925827.html)
.

### Space permissions

Every space has its [own independent set of permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/space-permissions-overview-1652924473.html)
, managed by the space administrators, which determine the access settings for different users and groups.

They can be used to [grant or revoke permission](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/assign-space-permissions-1652924476.html)
 to view, add, edit, and delete content within that space, and can be applied to groups, users, and even to anonymous users (people who aren't logged in) if need be.

For full details, check out the [Space Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/space-permissions-overview-1652924473.html)
.

tip/resting Created with Sketch.

One thing to watch out for is where a user is a member of multiple groups. You may have revoked permission for that individual user to add pages, for example, but if they're a member of a group that _is_ allowed to add pages, they'll still be able to create new pages in the space.

If you can't get the result you want from space permissions, or you're not sure, check with one of your Confluence administrators to determine what permissions you should apply to individuals and groups.

  

### Page restrictions

Page restrictions work a little differently to global and space permissions. **Pages are open for viewing or editing by default**, but you can [restrict either viewing or editing](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/page-restrictions-1652924671.html)
 to certain users or groups if you need to. Page restrictions can be applied to published or unpublished pages and blog posts (drafts). 

Don't forget, every page in Confluence lives within a space, and space permissions allow the [space admin](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/space-permissions-overview-1652924473.html)
 to revoke permission to view content for the whole space. Even the ability to apply restrictions to pages is controlled by the 'restrict pages' space permission.

For full details, check out [Page restrictions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/page-restrictions-1652924671.html)
.

How do permissions and restrictions interact?
---------------------------------------------

You can restrict viewing of a page or blog post to certain users or groups, so that even if someone has the 'view' permission for the space, they won't be able to view the content of the page or blog post.

If someone is a space admin and you've used page restrictions to prevent them viewing a page, they won't be able to see the page when they navigate to it. As a space admin though, they can see a list of restricted pages in the space and remove the restrictions. 

Check who can view a page
-------------------------

To check who can view a page, go to 

**More options** ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/files/1652924139/1652924140/1/1738654769772/ellipsis.png) 

 > **People who can view**. This will show a list of people, including administrators, who have space permission to see the space, and are not prevented by page restrictions from seeing the page.  See [Check who can view a page](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/check-who-can-view-a-page-1652925525.html)
 for more information. 

The diagram below shows the points at which someone could be prevented from viewing a page. 

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/files/1652925521/1652925522/1/1579235260772/permissions-restrictions-bar-chart.png)

*   **Site** - they don't have permission to log in to Confluence.
*   **Space** - they don't have permission to view the space.
*   **Parent page** - the current page has a view restriction preventing them from viewing.
*   **Child page** - the current page is inheriting a view restriction from another page higher up in the page hierarchy, preventing them from viewing.  

  

**What about links?**

Space permissions and page restrictions affect how [links between Confluence pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/links-1652924688.html)
 are displayed.

*   If someone doesn't have 'View' space permission, links to pages in that space will be visible, but they'll get a "page not found" message. The space key is not revealed in the link URL. 
*   If someone has the "View" space permission, but the page has view restrictions, the link will be visible but they'll get an "access denied" message when they click the link. 

[Links to attachments](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/links-1652924688.html)
 are also affected. If the visitor doesn't have permission to view the page the attachment lives on, the link won't be rendered.

Last modified on Jul 27, 2020

*   No related content found

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

This feature is available with a Confluence Data Center license.

Use analytics data to understand how your site, spaces, and pages are performing, and how active your users are. You can also export reports to share with your team.

**On this page:**

Analytics data is useful for identifying:

*   most viewed content  
*   usage and activity trends across your site 
*   frequent contributors and viewers 
*   spaces with few views that could potentially be archived. 

If required, you can set who can view analytics on your site and spaces. 

How to access analytics
-----------------------

Analytics is only available with a Confluence Data Center license. 

Your administrator may have limited who can view analytics reports. See [Administer analytics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/administer-analytics-1573751794.html)
 for more information. 

### Access Analytics from the Confluence navigation

When you access analytics from the navigation, you get an overview of your site's usage and access to insights on spaces, users, and searches.

Go to Analytics in the Confluence header. 

See [View insights on your site](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/view-insights-on-your-site-1573750973.html)
 for more information. 

### Access Analytics from a space

When you access analytics from a space, you get an overview of all the pages and attached files in that space. 

Go to Analytics in the space sidebar. 

See [View insights on spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/view-insights-on-spaces-1573750975.html)
 for more information. 

### Access Analytics from a page

When you access analytics from a page, you can get a quick summary of insights on your page, such as total page views. From there, you can view more detailed page analytics, such as which users have viewed the page, which versions of the page they viewed, and more.

At the top of any page, click  Analytics.  Click View analytics to see the detailed analytics about your page.

See [View insights on pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/view-insights-on-pages-1573750979.html)
 for more information. 

Limit who can view analytics reports for specific spaces
--------------------------------------------------------

You can further limit who can view analytics reports for specific spaces. You need Space Administrator permission to do this. 

To change who can see analytics reports in a space:

1.  Go to the space and select **Space tools** > **Permissions** from the bottom of the sidebar.
2.  Select the **Analytics Permissions** tab. 
3.  Select **Viewing Analytics Restricted** from the drop down.
4.  Enter the users and/or groups you want to allow to view analytics reports. 
5.  Save your changes. 

Good to know:

*   If a Confluence administrator has denied a group permission to view analytics, adding the group at the space level will not grant this permission. 
*   The space will still appear in the site analytics report (if the user has permission to see the space, and use analytics globally), but they will be prevented from viewing the space analytics report if they don't have space permissions.

Analytics for Confluence marketplace app
----------------------------------------

If you previously installed the Good Software Analytics for Confluence marketplace app, you can continue to use the app until it [reaches end of life](https://confluence.atlassian.com/support/atlassian-end-of-support-policy-201851003.html)
, but your version may not contain the same features. 

  

**Analytics for Confluence** **is now part of Confluence Data Center**  
To get access to the features described on this page upgrade to Confluence Data Center 7.11 or later. [See our FAQ for all the details](https://www.atlassian.com/migration/faqs#data-center)

Last modified on Jun 13, 2025

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Confluence is all about encouraging team collaboration to get the best results, so we've built in a number of ways you can notify other people about content that may be of interest to them.

You can:

*   [Work together with your team](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/collaborative-editing-1573750101.html)
     on a page or blog and see their changes in real time
*   [Share a link](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/share-a-page-or-blog-post-1573750929.html)
     to a page or blog post via email
*   [Mention](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/mentions-1573750919.html)
     a user when you write a page, blog post, comment, or add a task
*   [Like](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/likes-and-popular-content-1573750915.html)
     a page, blog post or comment

Whenever you mention another user, they'll receive an email notification; if you like a page, blog post, or comment, the author will be notified that you _like_ the content.

Other users can also find out about changes to content in Confluence by [watching](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-1573750944.html)
 pages and spaces.

Another way to share Confluence content is by [exporting it to other formats](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml-1573750041.html)
 such as XML, HTML, Microsoft Word and PDF.

**Related pages:**

*   [Pages and blogs](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/pages-and-blogs-1573750048.html)
    
*   [Watch Pages, Spaces and Blogs](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-1573750944.html)
    
*   [Export Content to Word, PDF, HTML and XML](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml-1573750041.html)

Jira Data Center automation
===========================

   

The simple way to automate and extend Jira. All power, no scripts.

* * *

Get started with Jira automation
================================

If you've never used Jira automation before, start with this quick guide.

[View guide](https://confluence.atlassian.com/automation107/get-started-with-jira-automation-1595113486.html)

Automation basics
=================

Learn about key concepts, use cases, template library, and best practices for optimizing rules.

[View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/automation107/automation-basics-1595113497.html)

Last modified on Feb 12, 2024

==========================\
\

\

[Components in Jira automation\

Analytics
=========

#### Confluence Data Center documentation

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

This feature is available with a Confluence Data Center license.

Use analytics data to understand how your site, spaces, and pages are performing, and how active your users are. You can also export reports to share with your team.

**On this page:**

Analytics data is useful for identifying:

*   most viewed content  
*   usage and activity trends across your site 
*   frequent contributors and viewers 
*   spaces with few views that could potentially be archived. 

If required, you can set who can view analytics on your site and spaces. 

How to access analytics
-----------------------

Analytics is only available with a Confluence Data Center license. 

Your administrator may have limited who can view analytics reports. See [Administer analytics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/administer-analytics-1540723160.html)
 for more information. 

### Access Analytics from the Confluence navigation

When you access analytics from the navigation, you get an overview of your site's usage and access to insights on spaces, users, and searches.

Go to Analytics in the Confluence header. 

See [View insights on your site](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/view-insights-on-your-site-1540722339.html)
 for more information. 

### Access Analytics from a space

When you access analytics from a space, you get an overview of all the pages and attached files in that space. 

Go to Analytics in the space sidebar. 

See [View insights on spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/view-insights-on-spaces-1540722341.html)
 for more information. 

### Access Analytics from a page

When you access analytics from a page, you can get a quick summary of insights on your page, such as total page views. From there, you can view more detailed page analytics, such as which users have viewed the page, which versions of the page they viewed, and more.

At the top of any page, click  Analytics.  Click View analytics to see the detailed analytics about your page.

See [View insights on pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/view-insights-on-pages-1540722345.html)
 for more information. 

Limit who can view analytics reports for specific spaces
--------------------------------------------------------

You can further limit who can view analytics reports for specific spaces. You need Space Administrator permission to do this. 

To change who can see analytics reports in a space:

1.  Go to the space and select **Space tools** > **Permissions** from the bottom of the sidebar.
2.  Select the **Analytics Permissions** tab. 
3.  Select **Viewing Analytics Restricted** from the drop down.
4.  Enter the users and/or groups you want to allow to view analytics reports. 
5.  Save your changes. 

Good to know:

*   If a Confluence administrator has denied a group permission to view analytics, adding the group at the space level will not grant this permission. 
*   The space will still appear in the site analytics report (if the user has permission to see the space, and use analytics globally), but they will be prevented from viewing the space analytics report if they don't have space permissions.

Analytics for Confluence marketplace app
----------------------------------------

If you previously installed the Good Software Analytics for Confluence marketplace app, you can continue to use the app until it [reaches end of life](https://confluence.atlassian.com/support/atlassian-end-of-support-policy-201851003.html)
, but your version may not contain the same features. 

  

**Analytics for Confluence** **is now part of Confluence Data Center**  
To get access to the features described on this page upgrade to Confluence Data Center 7.11 or later. [See our FAQ for all the details](https://www.atlassian.com/migration/faqs#data-center)

Last modified on Apr 4, 2025

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Welcome to the Confluence getting started documentation. In this section, you'll find tutorials and other information that'll be useful for evaluating Confluence, and getting to know it when you're starting out.

Teams in Space
--------------

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf88/files/1354498935/1354498936/1/1707432254048/tis-header.jpg)

For each tutorial in this section, we'll use a fictional organization known as 'Teams in Space'. Their mission is to:

> _"Perform flight research and technology integration to revolutionize aviation and pioneer aerospace technology. Also, land the first humans on Mars by 2020."_

You're an astronaut in the 'See Space EZ' team, which is working on the upcoming colonization of Mars.

Go ahead – dive into the tutorials, and let us show you around Confluence and some of its handy features.

*   [Tutorial: Navigate Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf88/tutorial-navigate-confluence-1354498937.html)
    *   [The dashboard](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf88/the-dashboard-1354498945.html)
        
    *   [The space directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf88/the-space-directory-1354498961.html)
        
    *   [The space sidebar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf88/the-space-sidebar-1354498964.html)
        
    *   [Keyboard shortcuts](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf88/keyboard-shortcuts-1354498966.html)
        
    *   [Complete your mission](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf88/complete-your-mission-1354498981.html)
        
*   [Tutorial: Space ace](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf88/tutorial-space-ace-1354498988.html)
    *   [Create a project space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf88/create-a-project-space-1354498990.html)
        
    *   [Create your personal space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf88/create-your-personal-space-1354498992.html)
        
    *   [Create the team's PR space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf88/create-the-team-s-pr-space-1354498994.html)
        
    *   [Delete and archive spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf88/delete-and-archive-spaces-1354498996.html)
        

Last modified on Feb 8, 2024

Welcome to the Confluence getting started documentation. In this section, you'll find tutorials and other information that'll be useful for evaluating Confluence, and getting to know it when you're starting out.

Teams in Space
--------------

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/files/1573749884/1573749885/1/1749821397248/tis-header.jpg)

For each tutorial in this section, we'll use a fictional organization known as 'Teams in Space'. Their mission is to:

> _"Perform flight research and technology integration to revolutionize aviation and pioneer aerospace technology. Also, land the first humans on Mars by 2020."_

You're an astronaut in the 'See Space EZ' team, which is working on the upcoming colonization of Mars.

Go ahead – dive into the tutorials, and let us show you around Confluence and some of its handy features.

*   [Tutorial: Navigate Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/tutorial-navigate-confluence-1573749886.html)
    *   [The dashboard](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/the-dashboard-1573749894.html)
        
    *   [The space directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/the-space-directory-1573749910.html)
        
    *   [The space sidebar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/the-space-sidebar-1573749913.html)
        
    *   [Keyboard shortcuts](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/keyboard-shortcuts-1573749915.html)
        
    *   [Complete your mission](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/complete-your-mission-1573749931.html)
        
*   [Tutorial: Space ace](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/tutorial-space-ace-1573749938.html)
    *   [Create a project space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/create-a-project-space-1573749940.html)
        
    *   [Create your personal space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/create-your-personal-space-1573749942.html)
        
    *   [Create the team's PR space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/create-the-team-s-pr-space-1573749944.html)
        
    *   [Delete and archive spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/delete-and-archive-spaces-1573749946.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Welcome to the Confluence getting started documentation. In this section, you'll find tutorials and other information that'll be useful for evaluating Confluence, and getting to know it when you're starting out.

Teams in Space
--------------

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/files/1540721251/1540721252/1/1743761968627/tis-header.jpg)

For each tutorial in this section, we'll use a fictional organization known as 'Teams in Space'. Their mission is to:

> _"Perform flight research and technology integration to revolutionize aviation and pioneer aerospace technology. Also, land the first humans on Mars by 2020."_

You're an astronaut in the 'See Space EZ' team, which is working on the upcoming colonization of Mars.

Go ahead – dive into the tutorials, and let us show you around Confluence and some of its handy features.

*   [Tutorial: Navigate Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/tutorial-navigate-confluence-1540721253.html)
    *   [The dashboard](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/the-dashboard-1540721261.html)
        
    *   [The space directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/the-space-directory-1540721277.html)

    *   [Keyboard shortcuts](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/keyboard-shortcuts-1540721282.html)
        
    *   [Complete your mission](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/complete-your-mission-1540721298.html)
        
*   [Tutorial: Space ace](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/tutorial-space-ace-1540721305.html)
    *   [Create a project space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/create-a-project-space-1540721307.html)
        
    *   [Create your personal space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/create-your-personal-space-1540721309.html)
        
    *   [Create the team's PR space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/create-the-team-s-pr-space-1540721311.html)
        
    *   [Delete and archive spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/delete-and-archive-spaces-1540721313.html)

Permissions and restrictions
============================

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

As a tool for communication and collaboration, we believe Confluence is at its best when everyone can participate fully. Confluence keeps a history of all changes to pages and other content, so it's easy to see who has changed what, and reverse any changes if you need to.

Confluence does, however, give you the choice to make your site, spaces, and pages as open or closed as you want to.

Levels of permission
--------------------

There are three levels of permissions in Confluence:

*   [global permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/permissions-and-restrictions-1573751015.html#Permissionsandrestrictions-global)
    
*   [space permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/permissions-and-restrictions-1573751015.html#Permissionsandrestrictions-space)
    , and 
*   [page restrictions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/permissions-and-restrictions-1573751015.html#Permissionsandrestrictions-page)
    .

**On this page:**

**Related pages:**

*   [Confluence Groups](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/confluence-groups-1573751018.html)
    
*   [Global Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/global-permissions-overview-1573751311.html)
    
*   [Space Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/space-permissions-overview-1573749968.html)
    
*   [Page restrictions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/page-restrictions-1573750166.html)
    
*   [Configuring Confluence Security](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/configuring-confluence-security-1573751677.html)
    

### Global permissions

[Global permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/global-permissions-overview-1573751311.html)
 are site-wide permissions, and are assigned by a Confluence administrator or system administrator.

Global permissions cover things like whether a user can log in or create a space. They don't really interact with space permissions or page restrictions.

For full details, check out the [Global Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/global-permissions-overview-1573751311.html)
.

### Space permissions

Every space has its [own independent set of permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/space-permissions-overview-1573749968.html)
, managed by the space administrators, which determine the access settings for different users and groups.

They can be used to [grant or revoke permission](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/assign-space-permissions-1573749971.html)
 to view, add, edit, and delete content within that space, and can be applied to groups, users, and even to anonymous users (people who aren't logged in) if need be.

For full details, check out the [Space Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/space-permissions-overview-1573749968.html)
.

tip/resting Created with Sketch.

One thing to watch out for is where a user is a member of multiple groups. You may have revoked permission for that individual user to add pages, for example, but if they're a member of a group that _is_ allowed to add pages, they'll still be able to create new pages in the space.

If you can't get the result you want from space permissions, or you're not sure, check with one of your Confluence administrators to determine what permissions you should apply to individuals and groups.

  

### Page restrictions

Page restrictions work a little differently to global and space permissions. **Pages are open for viewing or editing by default**, but you can [restrict either viewing or editing](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/page-restrictions-1573750166.html)
 to certain users or groups if you need to. Page restrictions can be applied to published or unpublished pages and blog posts (drafts). 

Don't forget, every page in Confluence lives within a space, and space permissions allow the [space admin](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/space-permissions-overview-1573749968.html)
 to revoke permission to view content for the whole space. Even the ability to apply restrictions to pages is controlled by the 'restrict pages' space permission.

For full details, check out [Page restrictions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/page-restrictions-1573750166.html)
.

How do permissions and restrictions interact?
---------------------------------------------

You can restrict viewing of a page or blog post to certain users or groups, so that even if someone has the 'view' permission for the space, they won't be able to view the content of the page or blog post.

If someone is a space admin and you've used page restrictions to prevent them viewing a page, they won't be able to see the page when they navigate to it. As a space admin though, they can see a list of restricted pages in the space and remove the restrictions. 

Check who can view a page
-------------------------

To check who can view a page, go to 

**More options** ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/files/1573749635/1573749636/1/1749821370123/ellipsis.png) 

 > **People who can view**. This will show a list of people, including administrators, who have space permission to see the space, and are not prevented by page restrictions from seeing the page.  See [Check who can view a page](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/check-who-can-view-a-page-1573751019.html)
 for more information. 

The diagram below shows the points at which someone could be prevented from viewing a page. 

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/files/1573751015/1573751016/1/1749821530341/permissions-restrictions-bar-chart.png)

*   **Site** - they don't have permission to log in to Confluence.
*   **Space** - they don't have permission to view the space.
*   **Parent page** - the current page has a view restriction preventing them from viewing.
*   **Child page** - the current page is inheriting a view restriction from another page higher up in the page hierarchy, preventing them from viewing.  

  

**What about links?**

Space permissions and page restrictions affect how [links between Confluence pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/links-1573750183.html)
 are displayed.

*   If someone doesn't have 'View' space permission, links to pages in that space will be visible, but they'll get a "page not found" message. The space key is not revealed in the link URL. 
*   If someone has the "View" space permission, but the page has view restrictions, the link will be visible but they'll get an "access denied" message when they click the link. 

[Links to attachments](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/links-1573750183.html)
 are also affected. If the visitor doesn't have permission to view the page the attachment lives on, the link won't be rendered.

Last modified on Jun 13, 2025

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

**Team Calendars for Confluence** **is now part of Confluence Data Center**  
To get access to the features described on this page upgrade to Confluence Data Center 7.11 or later. Can’t upgrade yet? Depending on your current Data Center version, you can access these features by installing the latest version of the app (at no cost). [See our FAQ for all the details](https://www.atlassian.com/migration/faqs#data-center)

Team Calendars provides one place for fast-moving teams working in Confluence to connect their schedules of leave, Jira projects and events, or whatever use case your team has.

*   [Team Calendars Quick Tour](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/team-calendars-quick-tour-1652925541.html)
    
*   [Create, Add, and Edit Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/create-add-and-edit-calendars-1652925562.html)
    
*   [Restrict a Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/restrict-a-calendar-1652925575.html)
    
*   [Watch a Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/watch-a-calendar-1652925634.html)
    
*   [Share Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/share-calendars-1652925641.html)
    
*   [Export Team Calendars Content to Other Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/export-team-calendars-content-to-other-calendars-1652925714.html)
    
*   [Delete or Remove a Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/delete-or-remove-a-calendar-1652925721.html)
    

Create, organize, and find events easily. Team Calendars offers great tools for planning and managing your events, whether they're travel, conferences, birthdays, or JIRA issues and sprints. 

*   [Add Events](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/add-events-1652925582.html)
    
*   [Add Jira Events](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/add-jira-events-1652925591.html)
    
*   [Event Types](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/event-types-1652925609.html)
    
*   [Custom Event Types](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/custom-event-types-1652925614.html)
    
*   [Reminders](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/reminders-1652925620.html)
    
*   [Embed Calendars on Confluence Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/embed-calendars-on-confluence-pages-1652925628.html)
    

Calendar subscriptions provide a simple and effective way to stay up to date with holidays, conferences, and more. 

You can subscribe to team calendars from many of your your favourite third-party calendar apps:

*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Microsoft Outlook](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-microsoft-outlook-1652925654.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Outlook on the web](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-outlook-on-the-web-1652925669.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Apple Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-apple-calendar-1652925670.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Apple iOS Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-apple-ios-calendar-1652925675.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Google Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-google-calendar-1652925680.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Android Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-android-calendar-1652925690.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Thunderbird](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-thunderbird-1652925693.html)
    

Alternatively, subscribe to a third-party calendar in Team Calendars:

*   [Subscribe to Outlook Calendar from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/subscribe-to-outlook-calendar-from-team-calendars-1652925697.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Apple Calendar from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/subscribe-to-apple-calendar-from-team-calendars-1652925699.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Google Calendar from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/subscribe-to-google-calendar-from-team-calendars-1652925702.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Opsgenie Calendars from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/subscribe-to-opsgenie-calendars-from-team-calendars-1652925707.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to PagerDuty Schedules from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/subscribe-to-pagerduty-schedules-from-team-calendars-1652925709.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Teamup Calendars from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/subscribe-to-teamup-calendars-from-team-calendars-1652925711.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

This section describes some specific use cases for Confluence.

*   **Using Confluence for technical documentation  
    **
    
    A technical communicator's guide to using Confluence – see [Develop Technical Documentation in Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/develop-technical-documentation-in-confluence-1627457753.html)
    .  
      
*   **Setting up a knowledge base  
    **
    
    A support engineer's guide to using Confluence as a knowledge base – see [Use Confluence as a Knowledge Base](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/use-confluence-as-a-knowledge-base-1627457758.html)
    .  
      
*   **Setting up an intranet  
    **
    
    A quick guide to setting up an intranet – see [Use Confluence as your Intranet](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/use-confluence-as-your-intranet-1627457759.html)
    .  
      
*   **Confluence for software teams**  
    A series of blog posts to help your agile team get the most out of Confluence. See [Confluence for Software Teams](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/confluence-for-software-teams-1627457769.html)
    .

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

There are two ways to stay connected to your team's work in Confluence while you're out and about – the Confluence Data Center and Server mobile app for iOS and Android, or via your device's browser. 

If you're a Confluence user, check out the two ways you can use Confluence on your device. 

*   [Using the Confluence Data Center mobile app](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/using-the-confluence-data-center-and-server-mobile-app-1573750522.html)
     (recommended)
*   [Using Confluence via your mobile browser](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/using-confluence-via-your-mobile-browser-1573750532.html)
     (no longer in maintenance)

**On this page:**

### What you'll need

### Confluence requirements

In order for your users to try the Confluence Data Center mobile app, you will need to:

*   upgrade your Confluence site to ****Confluence 6.8**** or later
*   allow users to access your site on their device (if your site isn't accessible on the public internet, people will need be connected to your network or use a [VPN](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/confluence-mobile-1573750508.html#ConfluenceMobile-vpn)
    )

### Device requirements

In order to use the app, your users will need a device with either:

*   Android 10 or later, or 
*   iOS 17 or later (iPhone, iPad)

Users will need to log in to use the app, even if your site allows anonymous users.

Considerations for administrators
---------------------------------

Here are some things to consider when determining whether your users will be able to use the app.

#### VPN and firewalls

If your Confluence site is not accessible on the public internet, users will need to connect their device to your network or virtual private network (VPN) in order to use the app.

We recommend providing your users with step-by-step instructions on how to connect to your VPN when you let them know the mobile app is available, as this is something Atlassian Support will not be able to help them with.  

The mobile app will also attempt to check the compatibility of your site prior to presenting the login screen. If you've configured a custom filter to prevent unauthenticated requests to your server, you will need to change it to allow `<confluence-base-url>/rest/nativemobile/1.0/info/login`  to pass through without authentication.

#### HTTPS and certificate requirements

In the latest version of the iOS and Android apps, you can connect to the app using either HTTP or HTTPS.

If you're using HTTPS your proxy must allow TLS 1.2 traffic. This is an iOS requirement that we've chosen to implement for both the iOS and Android apps to prevent confusion (for example where one device can log in, and another cannot).

Ideally, your certificate should be from a trusted Certificate Authority. If you have certificate that is self-signed, or from an unknown Certificate Authority (for example, you are your own CA), users may still be able to use the app by manually installing your certificate on their device. See our [Knowledge base article](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/can-t-get-a-secure-connection-error-on-confluence-data-center-and-server-mobile-app-940695063.html)
 for more information on how to do this. 

iOS 13 introduced a number of other requirements that your certificate will need to meet if your users will be using the app on iOS devices. See [Requirements for trusted certificates in iOS 13](https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT210176)
. 

#### Login and authentication

The app supports all common Confluence user management configurations, including external user directories and SAML single sign-on. Users will need to sign in to use the app, even if your site allows anonymous access.  

Starting from Confluence Data Center iOS mobile app 2.0, you can use the OAuth 2.0 authentication flow to:

*   improve your Confluence’s security
*   improve session restoration
*   support Fast IDentity Online 2 (FIDO2) and other certificate-based authentication standards.

For more information, see [Configure OAuth 2.0 for Confluence iOS](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/configure-oauth-2-0-for-confluence-ios-1442846594.html)
.

User-Agent header format

The app uses an [RFC-compliant](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-5.5.3)
 user-agent header for making the requests during login. This allows us to effectively identify the app and apply further processing based on the user’s hardware and software.

[User-Agent format examples...](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/confluence-mobile-1573750508.html#)

Given the wide variety of supported configurations we decided to adopt the most versatile format. For both iOS and Android we compose the User-Agent header value by concatenating the standard WebView User-Agent and unique app identifier.

**iOS User-Agent**

Format

    Mozilla/5.0 (<system-information>) <platform> (<platform-details>) Safari/<technical-version> AtlassianMobileApp

Example

    Mozilla/5.0 (iPhone; CPU iPhone OS 14_7 like Mac OS X) AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) Safari/604.1 AtlassianMobileApp

**Android User-Agent**

Format

    Mozilla/5.0 (<system-information>) <platform> (<platform-details>) AtlassianMobileApp

Example

    Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 9; Mi A1 Build/PKQ1.180917.001) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) AtlassianMobileApp

#### Storage and encryption

The iOS and Android apps cache some content (spaces, pages, attachments) locally on the user's device. This helps keep the app responsive when navigating around pages and spaces. We don't use any application-level encryption when storing cached data, but the device's internal storage may be encrypted by the operating system.  

When a user logs out, all cached data is deleted. 

We don't store passwords in the app. Instead we use session cookies, which are encrypted by default. 

#### Session timeout

Mobile apps rely only on the 401 response status code to recognize a session timeout event. If your environment has a proxy, make sure that it doesn’t override the response status code (e.g. in case of ADFS you may run into a redirect to [login.microsoft.com](http://login.microsoft.com/)
, a 3xx status code, when your session expires). Otherwise, you might see errors like `No connection`  or `Can't show the issue/page`  when your session expires, and you should log in again explicitly to resolve them.

#### Mobile Device Management (MDM)

You can distribute the Confluence Data Center app to people in your organisation using your MDM solution. For more info on how to do this, see [Mobile Device Management](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/mobile-device-management-mdm-1573750538.html)
. 

#### Marketplace apps, themes, and visual customizations

The mobile app provides a simple, lightweight way for users to view, create, edit and collaborate on pages. Complex interactions, including those provided by Marketplace apps, such as blueprints, calendars, workflows will not be available in the app. Some third party macros may be available, depending on whether the third-party app supports rendering these macros on mobile. 

Any theming or look and feel customizations you've made to your site will not be reflected in the mobile app. 

#### Cloud services

In order to provide push notifications to users' devices, we have developed a cloud-based notification service. This service is developed and maintained by Atlassian, and is hosted on our AWS infrastructure (AWS SNS). See [Push notifications service](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/confluence-mobile-1573750508.html#ConfluenceMobile-push)
 below for more information. 

This is the only cloud-based service used by the app.

#### Push notifications service

The Confluence Data Center mobile app can push notifications directly to users' devices. Users choose whether they'd like to receive push notifications from the app, and can opt out at any time. This feature uses a cloud-based notifications service developed and maintained by Atlassian and hosted on our AWS infrastructure. No user or message content is sent to the service, only notification IDs, and we don't store any data.

If you need to avoid using any cloud-based services you can choose to disable push notifications entirely. Head to **Administration** menu ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/files/1573749741/1573749742/1/1749821380723/cog.png) , then **General Configuration** > **Mobile apps**.

If you're using restrictive firewall or proxy server settings, you'll need to allow (whitelist)  `https://mobile-server-push-notification.atlassian.com`  to ensure push notifications work as expected.

For sites that are not accessible on the public internet (for example users need to be connected via VPN to use the app) we adapt the push notification message as follows:

*   If the user is connected to your network or VPN, we'll show the full notification, for example "Sara Leung shared 'End of year party' with you"
*   If the user is not currently connected to your network or VPN, we'll show a shorter notification, for example "1 new notification".

#### Mobile web and linking directly to pages

It is not possible to go directly from a link, for example in an email notification, to the app. To help with this limitation, when people land on a Confluence page in their device's browser, they'll see the **Open in app** button. Tapping this prompt will open the app, if they have it installed, or take them to the App or Play store to download it. 

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/files/1573750508/1573750509/1/1749821471450/open-in-app-mobile-web.png)

If you don't want this button to display in mobile web, you'll need to disable the entire Confluence Mobile plugin, which is required to use the mobile app. There is currently a known issue with this workaround. See  [CONFSERVER-57423](https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/CONFSERVER-57423)
 - Getting issue details... STATUS  for details.

Limitations and known issues
----------------------------

### Not all macros are available

Not all macros will display in the app or mobile web. If a macro can't be displayed, you'll see the message below, and have the option to tap through to the desktop version of the page, in your device's browser. 

_Screenshot: Error that appears when a macro is not rendered in Confluence mobile_

![Error that appears when a macro is not rendered in Confluence mobile](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/files/1573750508/1573750517/1/1749821472148/ConfluenceMobileMacro-trunc-50pc.PNG)

### Administrators can disable Confluence mobile on your site

If you're not able to use the mobile app or mobile web, it may be because your administrator has disabled one or both of the following system apps:

*   **Mobile plugin for Confluence Data Center** (formerly called **Confluence mobile plugin**). This is required to use the mobile app.
*   **Confluence mobile web plugin.** This is required to use mobile web.

  

Disabling the Mobile plugin for Confluence Data Center (formerly known as 'Confluence Mobile Plugin') will also disable all the modules of the Workbox - Host Plugin plugin. This issue is being tracked at [CONFSERVER-40782](https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/CONFSERVER-40782)
 - Getting issue details... STATUS

### Seperate Cloud and Data Center mobile apps

While the functionality of the two mobile apps is very similar, you will need to download the **Confluence Data Center and Server mobile app** to be able to authenticate with a Data Center site. You can't use the [Confluence Cloud](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confcloud/get-the-confluence-cloud-mobile-app-724764728.html)
 app with a Confluence Data Center site or vice versa. 

### Troubleshooting

Having other problems with the app? See [Confluence Server and Data Center mobile apps](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/confluence-server-and-data-center-mobile-apps-938025496.html)
 in the knowledge base. 

Last modified on Jan 8, 2026

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Welcome to the Confluence getting started documentation. In this section, you'll find tutorials and other information that'll be useful for evaluating Confluence, and getting to know it when you're starting out.

Teams in Space
--------------

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/files/1652924389/1652924390/1/1589159916901/tis-header.jpg)

For each tutorial in this section, we'll use a fictional organization known as 'Teams in Space'. Their mission is to:

> _"Perform flight research and technology integration to revolutionize aviation and pioneer aerospace technology. Also, land the first humans on Mars by 2020."_

You're an astronaut in the 'See Space EZ' team, which is working on the upcoming colonization of Mars.

Go ahead – dive into the tutorials, and let us show you around Confluence and some of its handy features.

*   [Tutorial: Navigate Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/tutorial-navigate-confluence-1652924391.html)
    *   [The dashboard](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/the-dashboard-1652924399.html)
        
    *   [The space directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/the-space-directory-1652924415.html)
        
    *   [The space sidebar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/the-space-sidebar-1652924418.html)
        
    *   [Keyboard shortcuts](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/keyboard-shortcuts-1652924420.html)
        
    *   [Complete your mission](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/complete-your-mission-1652924436.html)
        
*   [Tutorial: Space ace](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/tutorial-space-ace-1652924443.html)
    *   [Create a project space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/create-a-project-space-1652924445.html)
        
    *   [Create your personal space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/create-your-personal-space-1652924447.html)
        
    *   [Create the team's PR space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/create-the-team-s-pr-space-1652924449.html)
        
    *   [Delete and archive spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/delete-and-archive-spaces-1652924451.html)

Permissions and restrictions
============================

#### Confluence Data Center documentation

*   [Get started](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/get-started-1627456374.html)
    
*   [Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/spaces-1627456438.html)
    
*   [Pages and blogs](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/pages-and-blogs-1627456538.html)
    
*   [Files](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/files-1627456933.html)
