#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Confluence gives you a few ways to find what you're looking for. Here's an overview of Confluence search, and a few tips to help you find things more easily.  

How Confluence search works
---------------------------

When you enter a search term, Confluence looks for content in all spaces (including personal spaces), pages, mail, personal profiles, and space descriptions. It also looks at the content of some attached file types (Word, Text, PowerPoint, Excel, PDF, and HTML).

Search results are based on your Confluence permissions, so you'll only see content you're allowed to view.

[Learn how Confluence ranks search results](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/ranking-of-search-results-1502350759.html)

**On this page:**

**Related pages:**

*   [Confluence Search Syntax](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/confluence-search-syntax-1502350748.html)
    
*   [Confluence Search Fields](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/confluence-search-fields-1502350751.html)
    
*   [Ranking of Search Results](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/ranking-of-search-results-1502350759.html)
    
*   [Recently Viewed Pages and Blog Posts](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/recently-viewed-pages-and-blog-posts-1502350755.html)
    
*   [Search Results Macro](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/search-results-macro-1502350544.html)
    
*   [Livesearch Macro](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/livesearch-macro-1502350439.html)
    

*   [Search the People Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/search-the-people-directory-1502350752.html)
    

  

Start a search
--------------

To search Confluence:

1.  Click the search field in the top-right of Confluence to open the search panel.
2.  Start typing your search term.

Results will appear as you type — you don't need to hit enter. 

We exclude comments from your search results unless you select the comment option from the type filter.

_Screenshot: the search panel_

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/files/1502350726/1502350747/1/1738669774224/Confluence-search-results.png)

1.  **Search filters** – refine your results by space, contributor, type, date, label, or space category.
2.  **Advanced search** – go the the advanced search page.
3.  **Search tips** – get search help, and tips for refining your search.

tip/resting Created with Sketch.

**Tip:** Type **/** on your keyboard to quickly open the search panel.

Filter your search results
--------------------------

You can refine your search using interactive filters on the left-hand side of the search panel.

### Search within a space

Use the **space** filter to find content within a particular space or list of spaces. The space you're currently in will appear at the top of the list by default. Start typing the space name and choose from the list of suggested spaces.

Click the ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/files/1502350726/1502350735/1/1738669773253/search-archived-spaces-toggle.png) toggle to search within archived spaces.

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/files/1502350726/1502350746/1/1738669774141/space-dropdown-filter.png)

### Search for content by a specific person

Use the **contributor** filter to restrict your search to content modified (created, updated, or commented on) by particular people. Start typing the person's username, or part of their name, and we'll show you a list of possible matches. You can add as many people as you like.

tip/resting Created with Sketch.

**Tip:** To search for your own work, click the **Contributor** filter, then select your profile from the drop-down menu. Your name appears here by default, so it's easy to find.

### Filter by content type

Use the **type** filter to only show content of a certain type, such as pages, blog posts, comments or user profiles.

### Search within a specific time frame

Use the **date** filter to search for content last modified (created, updated, or commented on) within a particular period of time. 

To search within a specific date range:

1.  Click **Advanced Search** on the left-side of the search panel.
2.  In the **Last modified** section, choose **Custom**.
3.  Select the date range from the drop-down date picker.

### Search for content with a specific label

Use the **label** filter to search for content containing a specific label. Start typing the name of the label and choose from the list of possible matches.

### Search within a space category

Use the space category filter to search within a group of related spaces. Start typing the category name and choose from the list of possible matches. You can browse existing categories from the [Space directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf614/the-space-directory-965545160.html)
.

tip/resting Created with Sketch.

**Tip:** Space admins can organize spaces into categories. You can create space categories for departments, subject areas, office locations — whatever works for your team. [Learn how to create a space category](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/use-labels-to-categorize-spaces-136647.html)

  

Search for admin options
------------------------

As a Confluence admin, you can can quickly access admin options from the search panel.

Start typing what you want to do. We'll show the top three matching admin items at the top of your search results. You'll only see options you have permission to perform.

If you apply a search filter, admin items will no longer appear in your results.

Advanced search
---------------

The advanced search page allows you to add more search filters, such as creator, title, date range or ancestor page. 

To use advanced search:

1.  Click the search field at the top-right of Confluence.
2.  Click **Advanced search** on the left-side of the search panel.
3.  Type your keyword in the search field and hit enter.

### Adding a filter from the advanced search page

To add a search filter from the advanced search page:

1.  Click **Add a filter** on the bottom left of the advanced search page.
2.  From the drop-down menu, select the relevant filter.

_Screenshot: advanced search page_

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/files/1502350726/1502350727/1/1738669772582/advanced-search.png)

1.  **Search filters** – narrow your search by adding filters.
2.  **Add a filter** – add more filters for even more precise search.

You can choose from these additional filters:

*   **Creator** – Restrict your search to content created by a particular person. Start typing the person's username or part of their name and Confluence will offer you a list of possible matches.
*   **Label** – Only search for content containing specific labels.
*   **With parent** – Only search for direct children of a specific parent page.
*   **With ancestor** – Only search for pages below a certain page in the hierarchy.
*   **Created** – Choose or enter a date to only show content created within a particular period of time.
*   **Mentioning user** – Only search for content that mentions a particular Confluence user.
*   **With title** – Only search within page or blog titles.

These filters are provided by [Confluence Query Language (CQL)](https://developer.atlassian.com/server/confluence/advanced-searching-using-cql/)
.

Advanced search syntax
----------------------

You can also refine your search using Confluence search syntax. These are words or symbols you enter into the search field to help narrow down your results. Learn more about [Confluence Search Syntax](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/confluence-search-syntax-1502350748.html)
. 

Last modified on Feb 4, 2025

====================================================

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Bitbucket)

We've created this set of support guides to help you with any GDPR-specific obligations that you may have. The guides are designed to identify where personal data may be located within the product, and, where possible, assist with responding to data subject access requests, including in modifying, restricting or deleting data.

Where a workaround involves running SQL scripts directly on your database, we strongly recommend you do this first on a non-production, staging or test environment before running the scripts on your production database. We also strongly recommend you take a backup of your data before making any modifications via SQL scripts.

*   [Automated individual decision-making, including profiling in Bitbucket Server and Data Center](https://confluence.atlassian.com/bitbucketserver/automated-individual-decision-making-including-profiling-inbitbucket-server-and-data-center-962360102.html)

*   [Data protection by design and by default in Bitbucket Server and Data Center](https://confluence.atlassian.com/bitbucketserver/data-protection-by-design-and-by-default-inbitbucket-server-and-data-center-962360104.html)

*   [Right of access by the data subject in Bitbucket Server and Data Center](https://confluence.atlassian.com/bitbucketserver/right-of-access-by-the-data-subject-inbitbucket-server-and-data-center-962360106.html)
    
*   [Right to data portability in Bitbucket Server and Data Center](https://confluence.atlassian.com/bitbucketserver/right-to-data-portability-inbitbucket-server-and-data-center-962360107.html)
    
*   [Right to erasure in Bitbucket Server and Data Center](https://confluence.atlassian.com/bitbucketserver/right-to-erasure-inbitbucket-server-and-data-center-962360108.html)
    
*   [Right to object in Bitbucket Server and Data Center](https://confluence.atlassian.com/bitbucketserver/right-to-object-inbitbucket-server-and-data-center-962360109.html)
    
*   [Right to rectification in Bitbucket Server and Data Center](https://confluence.atlassian.com/bitbucketserver/right-to-rectification-inbitbucket-server-and-data-center-962360110.html)
    
*   [Right to restriction of processing in Bitbucket Server and Data Center](https://confluence.atlassian.com/bitbucketserver/right-to-restriction-of-processinginbitbucket-server-and-data-center-962360111.html)
    
*   [Security of processing in Bitbucket Server and Data Center](https://confluence.atlassian.com/bitbucketserver/security-of-processing-inbitbucket-server-and-data-center-962360112.html)
    
*   [Transfers of personal data to third countries or international organisations in Bitbucket Server and Data Center](https://confluence.atlassian.com/bitbucketserver/transfers-of-personal-data-to-third-countries-or-international-organisations-inbitbucket-server-and-data-center-962360113.html)
    

GDPR and Atlassian Server and Data Center applications
------------------------------------------------------

We understand that customers using Atlassian Server or Data Center applications may have GDPR-specific obligations with respect to the data stored in and processed through those applications. For example, customers may need to access, modify or delete personal data upon request by certain individuals.  We've prepared a GDPR FAQ, and guides for our Server and Data Center products, and hope they'll be helpful in assisting our customers with their GDPR-specific obligations.

Please note, when you use Atlassian Server or Data Center applications, Atlassian does not access, collect, store or otherwise process personal data you choose to store within those applications, except in limited cases where such data is provided for incidental support services.  For this reason, Atlassian is neither a data processor nor data controller under GDPR for the purposes of the personal data you choose to store within Atlassian Server or Data Center applications.  Atlassian may access analytics events associated with your technical use of the products, but only where permitted by your administrator.  Such analytics information is filtered to exclude any personal data prior to the analytics information leaving your environment.  For more information on the analytics information collected through Atlassian downloadable products, please see our [Privacy Policy](https://www.atlassian.com/legal/privacy-policy)
.  

**Using multiple Atlassian products?**

Check our our GDPR support guides for your Atlassian products:

*   [Bamboo Server GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/gdpr/bamboo-server-gdpr-support-guides-949245655.html)
    
*   [Bitbucket Server and Data Center GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/bitbucketserver/bitbucket-server-and-data-center-gdpr-support-guides-962360101.html)
    
*   [Confluence Server and Data Center GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/gdpr-support-guides-for-confluence-data-center-960696666.html)
    
*   [Crowd Server and Data Center GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/gdpr/crowd-server-and-data-center-gdpr-support-guides-949245650.html)
    
*   [Fisheye and Crucible Server GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/gdpr/fisheye-and-crucible-server-gdpr-support-guides-949245659.html)
    
*   [Hipchat Server GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/hc/hipchat-server-gdpr-support-guides-962356659.html)
    
*   [Hipchat Data Center GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/hipchatdc3/hipchat-data-center-gdpr-support-guides-962356685.html)
    
*   [Jira Core, Jira Software and Jira Service Management (formerly Jira Service Desk) Server and Data Center GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/gdpr/jira-core-jira-software-and-jira-service-desk-server-and-data-center-gdpr-support-guides-949245619.html)
     
*   [Advanced Roadmaps (formerly Portfolio for Jira) Server and Data Center GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/advancedroadmapsserver/advanced-roadmaps-gdpr-support-guides-962968641.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Confluence is very flexible – not only in the many ways you can create and share content, but also in how you can tailor your own Confluence experience. Things like your [profile picture](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/set-your-profile-picture-1627457368.html)
, [favorite spaces and pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/save-for-later-1627457387.html)
, and your [personal space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/create-a-space-1627456444.html)
 can say a lot about you, and can also make navigating Confluence much quicker and easier. Even a simple thing like [adding shortcut links to the sidebar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/configure-the-sidebar-1627456495.html)
 of your personal space, can save you a lot of time in finding the things you use all the time.

[Set up your personal space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/create-a-space-1627456444.html)
, and take a look at any of the pages below, to start making Confluence feel like home.

*   [Your user profile](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/your-user-profile-1627457360.html)
    
*   [Change Your Password](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/change-your-password-1627457363.html)
    
*   [Edit Your User Settings](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/edit-your-user-settings-1627457365.html)
    
*   [Set Your Profile Picture](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/set-your-profile-picture-1627457368.html)
    
*   [Choose Your Home Page](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/choose-your-home-page-1627457382.html)
    
*   [Save for later](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/save-for-later-1627457387.html)
    
*   [View and revoke OAuth access tokens](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/view-and-revoke-oauth-access-tokens-1627457396.html)
    
*   [Set and use your preferred theme](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/set-and-use-your-preferred-theme-1627457399.html)
    

**Related pages:**

Jira Data Center automation
===========================

   

The simple way to automate and extend Jira. All power, no scripts.

* * *

Get started with Jira automation
================================

If you've never used Jira automation before, start with this quick guide.

[View guide](https://confluence.atlassian.com/automation106/get-started-with-jira-automation-1573487404.html)

Automation basics
=================

Learn about key concepts, use cases, template library, and best practices for optimizing rules.

[View topics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/automation106/automation-basics-1573487415.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

**Team Calendars for Confluence** **is now part of Confluence Data Center**  
To get access to the features described on this page upgrade to Confluence Data Center 7.11 or later. Can’t upgrade yet? Depending on your current Data Center version, you can access these features by installing the latest version of the app (at no cost). [See our FAQ for all the details](https://www.atlassian.com/migration/faqs#data-center)

Team Calendars provides one place for fast-moving teams working in Confluence to connect their schedules of leave, Jira projects and events, or whatever use case your team has.

*   [Team Calendars Quick Tour](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/team-calendars-quick-tour-1573751035.html)
    
*   [Create, Add, and Edit Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/create-add-and-edit-calendars-1573751055.html)
    
*   [Restrict a Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/restrict-a-calendar-1573751066.html)
    
*   [Watch a Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/watch-a-calendar-1573751120.html)
    
*   [Share Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/share-calendars-1573751127.html)
    
*   [Export Team Calendars Content to Other Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/export-team-calendars-content-to-other-calendars-1573751200.html)
    
*   [Delete or Remove a Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/delete-or-remove-a-calendar-1573751206.html)
    

Create, organize, and find events easily. Team Calendars offers great tools for planning and managing your events, whether they're travel, conferences, birthdays, or JIRA issues and sprints. 

*   [Add Events](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/add-events-1573751072.html)
    
*   [Add Jira Events](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/add-jira-events-1573751081.html)
    
*   [Event Types](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/event-types-1573751099.html)
    
*   [Custom Event Types](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/custom-event-types-1573751103.html)
    
*   [Reminders](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/reminders-1573751108.html)
    
*   [Embed Calendars on Confluence Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/embed-calendars-on-confluence-pages-1573751115.html)
    

Calendar subscriptions provide a simple and effective way to stay up to date with holidays, conferences, and more. 

You can subscribe to team calendars from many of your your favourite third-party calendar apps:

*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Microsoft Outlook](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-microsoft-outlook-1573751140.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Outlook on the web](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-outlook-on-the-web-1573751155.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Apple Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-apple-calendar-1573751156.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Apple iOS Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-apple-ios-calendar-1573751161.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Google Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-google-calendar-1573751166.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Android Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-android-calendar-1573751176.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Thunderbird](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-thunderbird-1573751179.html)
    

Alternatively, subscribe to a third-party calendar in Team Calendars:

*   [Subscribe to Outlook Calendar from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/subscribe-to-outlook-calendar-from-team-calendars-1573751183.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Apple Calendar from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/subscribe-to-apple-calendar-from-team-calendars-1573751185.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Google Calendar from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/subscribe-to-google-calendar-from-team-calendars-1573751188.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Opsgenie Calendars from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/subscribe-to-opsgenie-calendars-from-team-calendars-1573751193.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to PagerDuty Schedules from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/subscribe-to-pagerduty-schedules-from-team-calendars-1573751195.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Teamup Calendars from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/subscribe-to-teamup-calendars-from-team-calendars-1573751197.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Confluence is very flexible – not only in the many ways you can create and share content, but also in how you can tailor your own Confluence experience. Things like your [profile picture](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/set-your-profile-picture-1502350623.html)
, [favorite spaces and pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/save-for-later-1502350642.html)
, and your [personal space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/create-a-space-1502349700.html)
 can say a lot about you, and can also make navigating Confluence much quicker and easier. Even a simple thing like [adding shortcut links to the sidebar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/configure-the-sidebar-1502349751.html)
 of your personal space, can save you a lot of time in finding the things you use all the time.

[Set up your personal space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/create-a-space-1502349700.html)
, and take a look at any of the pages below, to start making Confluence feel like home.

*   [Your user profile](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/your-user-profile-1502350615.html)
    
*   [Change Your Password](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/change-your-password-1502350618.html)
    
*   [Edit Your User Settings](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/edit-your-user-settings-1502350620.html)
    
*   [Set Your Profile Picture](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/set-your-profile-picture-1502350623.html)
    
*   [Choose Your Home Page](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/choose-your-home-page-1502350637.html)
    
*   [Save for later](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/save-for-later-1502350642.html)
    
*   [View and revoke OAuth access tokens](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/view-and-revoke-oauth-access-tokens-1502350651.html)
    
*   [Set and use your preferred theme](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/set-and-use-your-preferred-theme-1502350654.html)
    

**Related pages:**

*   [Watch Pages, Spaces and Blogs](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-1502350689.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

What is a space?
----------------

Spaces are Confluence's way of organizing content into meaningful categories. Think of it like having different folders into which you can put your work.

  
Spaces come in two main varieties:

*   **Site spaces** – These spaces are found in the **[Space Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/the-space-directory-1627456400.html)** and are the areas where you create content and collaborate with others. They are sometimes called global spaces.
*   **Personal spaces** – Every Confluence user can set up a personal space which they can keep private or make public so others can view and edit. Personal spaces are listed in the **[People Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/search-the-people-directory-1627457497.html)** and found under your personal profile.

How do I use a space?
---------------------

**On this page:**

**Related pages:**

*   [Create a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/create-a-space-1627456444.html)
    
*   [Space Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/space-permissions-overview-1627456458.html)
    
*   [Navigate Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/navigate-spaces-1627456455.html)
    
*   [Organize your Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/organize-your-space-1627456476.html)
    
*   [Customize your Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/customize-your-space-1627456492.html)
    
*   [Archive a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/archive-a-space-1627456523.html)
    
*   [Export Content to Word, PDF, HTML and XML](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml-1627456531.html)
    
*   [Delete a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/delete-a-space-1627456528.html)
    

**[Create](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/create-a-space-1627456444.html)
 as many spaces as you need to get things done:**

*   **Team spaces** – Give each team (QA, HR, Engineering, Support, ...) their own space so they can focus and make their information easier for everyone to find.
*   **Project spaces** – Put all the information related to your project in one place. This allows everyone to work together in Confluence instead of emailing back and forth.
*   **Personal space** – Store everything you're working on individually, keep your to-do lists, and polish any content before you move it into another shared space.

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/files/1627456438/1627456439/1/1755867835501/dashboard-space-page-hierarchy_02.png)

**Stay up to date with spaces**

*   [Watch a space to stay up-to-date with any changes](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-1627457434.html)
    .
    
*   [Add important spaces to your dashboard so that they're easy to find again](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/organize-your-space-1627456476.html#OrganizeyourSpace-my)
    .

**Administer spaces**  
If you have admin permissions for a space you can:

*   [Change the](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/customize-your-space-1627456492.html)
     [colors, logo, sidebar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/customize-your-space-1627456492.html)
     [and](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/set-up-a-space-home-page-1627456484.html)
     [homepage](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/set-up-a-space-home-page-1627456484.html)
     [of that space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/set-up-a-space-home-page-1627456484.html)
    .
*   [Control who can view and edit your space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/space-permissions-overview-1627456458.html)
    .
*   [Make your space public to share it with people who don't have access to Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/make-a-space-public-1627456472.html)
    .

**Space Permissions**  
Some things we should make clear about space admin permissions:

*   The person who creates a space automatically has admin permissions for that space.
*   Space admins can grant admin permissions to others.
*   Space admins don't have to be Confluence admins and can have special permissions for a single space. For example, you are the admin for your personal space, no matter what kind of access you have anywhere else.

Want more ideas for using spaces? Check out our kickass guides on how to:

*   [Use Confluence as a Knowledge Base](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/use-confluence-as-a-knowledge-base-1627457758.html)
    
*   [Develop Technical Documentation in Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/develop-technical-documentation-in-confluence-1627457753.html)
    
*   [Use Confluence as your Intranet](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/use-confluence-as-your-intranet-1627457759.html)
    
*   [Use Confluence for Software Teams](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/confluence-for-software-teams-1627457769.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

What is a space?
----------------

Spaces are Confluence's way of organizing content into meaningful categories. Think of it like having different folders into which you can put your work.

  
Spaces come in two main varieties:

*   **Site spaces** – These spaces are found in the **[Space Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/the-space-directory-1540721277.html)** and are the areas where you create content and collaborate with others. They are sometimes called global spaces.
*   **Personal spaces** – Every Confluence user can set up a personal space which they can keep private or make public so others can view and edit. Personal spaces are listed in the **[People Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/search-the-people-directory-1540722373.html)** and found under your personal profile.

How do I use a space?
---------------------

**On this page:**

**Related pages:**

*   [Create a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/create-a-space-1540721321.html)

**[Create](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/create-a-space-1540721321.html)
 as many spaces as you need to get things done:**

*   **Team spaces** – Give each team (QA, HR, Engineering, Support, ...) their own space so they can focus and make their information easier for everyone to find.
*   **Project spaces** – Put all the information related to your project in one place. This allows everyone to work together in Confluence instead of emailing back and forth.
*   **Personal space** – Store everything you're working on individually, keep your to-do lists, and polish any content before you move it into another shared space.

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/files/1540721315/1540721316/1/1743761975158/dashboard-space-page-hierarchy_02.png)

**Stay up to date with spaces**

*   [Watch a space to stay up-to-date with any changes](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-1540722310.html)
    .
    
*   [Add important spaces to your dashboard so that they're easy to find again](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/organize-your-space-1540721353.html#OrganizeyourSpace-my)
    .

**Administer spaces**  
If you have admin permissions for a space you can:

*   [Change the](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/customize-your-space-1540721369.html)
     [colors, logo, sidebar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/customize-your-space-1540721369.html)
     [and](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/set-up-a-space-home-page-1540721361.html)
     [homepage](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/set-up-a-space-home-page-1540721361.html)
     [of that space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/set-up-a-space-home-page-1540721361.html)
    .
*   [Control who can view and edit your space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/space-permissions-overview-1540721335.html)
    .
*   [Make your space public to share it with people who don't have access to Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/make-a-space-public-1540721349.html)
    .

**Space Permissions**  
Some things we should make clear about space admin permissions:

*   The person who creates a space automatically has admin permissions for that space.
*   Space admins can grant admin permissions to others.
*   Space admins don't have to be Confluence admins and can have special permissions for a single space. For example, you are the admin for your personal space, no matter what kind of access you have anywhere else.

Want more ideas for using spaces? Check out our kickass guides on how to:

*   [Use Confluence as a Knowledge Base](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/use-confluence-as-a-knowledge-base-1540722624.html)

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

What is a space?
----------------

Spaces are Confluence's way of organizing content into meaningful categories. Think of it like having different folders into which you can put your work.

  
Spaces come in two main varieties:

*   **Site spaces** – These spaces are found in the **[Space Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/the-space-directory-1442847255.html)** and are the areas where you create content and collaborate with others. They are sometimes called global spaces.
*   **Personal spaces** – Every Confluence user can set up a personal space which they can keep private or make public so others can view and edit. Personal spaces are listed in the **[People Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/search-the-people-directory-1442848344.html)** and found under your personal profile.

How do I use a space?
---------------------

**On this page:**

**Related pages:**

*   [Create a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/create-a-space-1442847298.html)
    
*   [Space Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/space-permissions-overview-1442847312.html)
    
*   [Navigate Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/navigate-spaces-1442847309.html)
    
*   [Organize your Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/organize-your-space-1442847329.html)
    
*   [Customize your Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/customize-your-space-1442847345.html)
    
*   [Archive a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/archive-a-space-1442847376.html)
    
*   [Export Content to Word, PDF, HTML and XML](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml-1442847384.html)
    
*   [Delete a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/delete-a-space-1442847381.html)
    

**[Create](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/create-a-space-1442847298.html)
 as many spaces as you need to get things done:**

*   **Team spaces** – Give each team (QA, HR, Engineering, Support, ...) their own space so they can focus and make their information easier for everyone to find.
*   **Project spaces** – Put all the information related to your project in one place. This allows everyone to work together in Confluence instead of emailing back and forth.
*   **Personal space** – Store everything you're working on individually, keep your to-do lists, and polish any content before you move it into another shared space.

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/files/1442847292/1442847293/1/1727949178137/dashboard-space-page-hierarchy_02.png)

**Stay up to date with spaces**

*   [Watch a space to stay up-to-date with any changes](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-1442848281.html)
    .
    
*   [Add important spaces to your dashboard so that they're easy to find again](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/organize-your-space-1442847329.html#OrganizeyourSpace-my)
    .

**Administer spaces**  
If you have admin permissions for a space you can:

*   [Change the](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/customize-your-space-1442847345.html)
     [colors, logo, sidebar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/customize-your-space-1442847345.html)
     [and](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/set-up-a-space-home-page-1442847337.html)
     [homepage](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/set-up-a-space-home-page-1442847337.html)
     [of that space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/set-up-a-space-home-page-1442847337.html)
    .
*   [Control who can view and edit your space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/space-permissions-overview-1442847312.html)
    .
*   [Make your space public to share it with people who don't have access to Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/make-a-space-public-1442847325.html)
    .

**Space Permissions**  
Some things we should make clear about space admin permissions:

*   The person who creates a space automatically has admin permissions for that space.
*   Space admins can grant admin permissions to others.
*   Space admins don't have to be Confluence admins and can have special permissions for a single space. For example, you are the admin for your personal space, no matter what kind of access you have anywhere else.

Want more ideas for using spaces? Check out our kickass guides on how to:

*   [Use Confluence as a Knowledge Base](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/use-confluence-as-a-knowledge-base-1442848595.html)
    
*   [Develop Technical Documentation in Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/develop-technical-documentation-in-confluence-1442848590.html)
    
*   [Use Confluence as your Intranet](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/use-confluence-as-your-intranet-1442848596.html)
    
*   [Use Confluence for Software Teams](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/confluence-for-software-teams-1442848606.html)
    

Last modified on Oct 3, 2024

Personal spaces are listed in the **[People Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf101/search-the-people-directory-1652925513.html)** and found under your personal profile.

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Welcome to the Confluence getting started documentation. In this section, you'll find tutorials and other information that'll be useful for evaluating Confluence, and getting to know it when you're starting out.

Teams in Space
--------------

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/files/1387594583/1387594584/1/1712046218979/tis-header.jpg)

For each tutorial in this section, we'll use a fictional organization known as 'Teams in Space'. Their mission is to:

> _"Perform flight research and technology integration to revolutionize aviation and pioneer aerospace technology. Also, land the first humans on Mars by 2020."_

You're an astronaut in the 'See Space EZ' team, which is working on the upcoming colonization of Mars.

Go ahead – dive into the tutorials, and let us show you around Confluence and some of its handy features.

*   [Tutorial: Navigate Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/tutorial-navigate-confluence-1387594585.html)
    *   [The dashboard](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/the-dashboard-1387594593.html)
        
    *   [The space directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/the-space-directory-1387594609.html)
        
    *   [The space sidebar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/the-space-sidebar-1387594612.html)
        
    *   [Keyboard shortcuts](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/keyboard-shortcuts-1387594614.html)
        
    *   [Complete your mission](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/complete-your-mission-1387594629.html)
        
*   [Tutorial: Space ace](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/tutorial-space-ace-1387594636.html)
    *   [Create a project space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/create-a-project-space-1387594638.html)
        
    *   [Create your personal space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/create-your-personal-space-1387594640.html)
        
    *   [Create the team's PR space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/create-the-team-s-pr-space-1387594642.html)
        
    *   [Delete and archive spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/delete-and-archive-spaces-1387594644.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

What is a space?
----------------

Spaces are Confluence's way of organizing content into meaningful categories. Think of it like having different folders into which you can put your work.

  
Spaces come in two main varieties:

*   **Site spaces** – These spaces are found in the **[Space Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/the-space-directory-1573749910.html)** and are the areas where you create content and collaborate with others. They are sometimes called global spaces.
*   **Personal spaces** – Every Confluence user can set up a personal space which they can keep private or make public so others can view and edit. Personal spaces are listed in the **[People Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/search-the-people-directory-1573751007.html)** and found under your personal profile.

How do I use a space?
---------------------

**On this page:**

**Related pages:**

*   [Create a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/create-a-space-1573749954.html)
    
*   [Space Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/space-permissions-overview-1573749968.html)
    
*   [Navigate Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/navigate-spaces-1573749965.html)
    
*   [Organize your Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/organize-your-space-1573749986.html)
    
*   [Customize your Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/customize-your-space-1573750002.html)
    
*   [Archive a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/archive-a-space-1573750033.html)
    
*   [Export Content to Word, PDF, HTML and XML](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml-1573750041.html)
    
*   [Delete a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/delete-a-space-1573750038.html)
    

**[Create](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/create-a-space-1573749954.html)
 as many spaces as you need to get things done:**

*   **Team spaces** – Give each team (QA, HR, Engineering, Support, ...) their own space so they can focus and make their information easier for everyone to find.
*   **Project spaces** – Put all the information related to your project in one place. This allows everyone to work together in Confluence instead of emailing back and forth.
*   **Personal space** – Store everything you're working on individually, keep your to-do lists, and polish any content before you move it into another shared space.

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/files/1573749948/1573749949/1/1749821405216/dashboard-space-page-hierarchy_02.png)

**Stay up to date with spaces**

*   [Watch a space to stay up-to-date with any changes](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-1573750944.html)
    .
    
*   [Add important spaces to your dashboard so that they're easy to find again](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/organize-your-space-1573749986.html#OrganizeyourSpace-my)
    .

**Administer spaces**  
If you have admin permissions for a space you can:

*   [Change the](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/customize-your-space-1573750002.html)
     [colors, logo, sidebar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/customize-your-space-1573750002.html)
     [and](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/set-up-a-space-home-page-1573749994.html)
     [homepage](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/set-up-a-space-home-page-1573749994.html)
     [of that space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/set-up-a-space-home-page-1573749994.html)
    .
*   [Control who can view and edit your space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/space-permissions-overview-1573749968.html)
    .
*   [Make your space public to share it with people who don't have access to Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/make-a-space-public-1573749982.html)
    .

**Space Permissions**  
Some things we should make clear about space admin permissions:

*   The person who creates a space automatically has admin permissions for that space.
*   Space admins can grant admin permissions to others.
*   Space admins don't have to be Confluence admins and can have special permissions for a single space. For example, you are the admin for your personal space, no matter what kind of access you have anywhere else.

Want more ideas for using spaces? Check out our kickass guides on how to:

*   [Use Confluence as a Knowledge Base](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/use-confluence-as-a-knowledge-base-1573751258.html)
    
*   [Develop Technical Documentation in Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/develop-technical-documentation-in-confluence-1573751253.html)
    
*   [Use Confluence as your Intranet](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/use-confluence-as-your-intranet-1573751259.html)
    
*   [Use Confluence for Software Teams](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/confluence-for-software-teams-1573751269.html)

What is a space?
----------------

Spaces are Confluence's way of organizing content into meaningful categories. Think of it like having different folders into which you can put your work.

  
Spaces come in two main varieties:

*   **Site spaces** – These spaces are found in the **[Space Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/the-space-directory-1477575493.html)** and are the areas where you create content and collaborate with others. They are sometimes called global spaces.
*   **Personal spaces** – Every Confluence user can set up a personal space which they can keep private or make public so others can view and edit. Personal spaces are listed in the **[People Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/search-the-people-directory-1477576583.html)** and found under your personal profile.

How do I use a space?
---------------------

**On this page:**

**Related pages:**

*   [Create a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/create-a-space-1477575536.html)
    
*   [Space Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/space-permissions-overview-1477575550.html)
    
*   [Navigate Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/navigate-spaces-1477575547.html)
    
*   [Organize your Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/organize-your-space-1477575568.html)
    
*   [Customize your Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/customize-your-space-1477575584.html)
    
*   [Archive a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/archive-a-space-1477575615.html)
    
*   [Export Content to Word, PDF, HTML and XML](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml-1477575623.html)
    
*   [Delete a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/delete-a-space-1477575620.html)
    

**[Create](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/create-a-space-1477575536.html)
 as many spaces as you need to get things done:**

*   **Team spaces** – Give each team (QA, HR, Engineering, Support, ...) their own space so they can focus and make their information easier for everyone to find.
*   **Project spaces** – Put all the information related to your project in one place. This allows everyone to work together in Confluence instead of emailing back and forth.
*   **Personal space** – Store everything you're working on individually, keep your to-do lists, and polish any content before you move it into another shared space.

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/files/1477575530/1477575531/1/1733814880419/dashboard-space-page-hierarchy_02.png)

**Stay up to date with spaces**

*   [Watch a space to stay up-to-date with any changes](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-1477576520.html)
    .
    
*   [Add important spaces to your dashboard so that they're easy to find again](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/organize-your-space-1477575568.html#OrganizeyourSpace-my)
    .

**Administer spaces**  
If you have admin permissions for a space you can:

*   [Change the](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/customize-your-space-1477575584.html)
     [colors, logo, sidebar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/customize-your-space-1477575584.html)
     [and](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/set-up-a-space-home-page-1477575576.html)
     [homepage](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/set-up-a-space-home-page-1477575576.html)
     [of that space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/set-up-a-space-home-page-1477575576.html)
    .
*   [Control who can view and edit your space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/space-permissions-overview-1477575550.html)
    .
*   [Make your space public to share it with people who don't have access to Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/make-a-space-public-1477575564.html)
    .

**Space Permissions**  
Some things we should make clear about space admin permissions:

*   The person who creates a space automatically has admin permissions for that space.
*   Space admins can grant admin permissions to others.
*   Space admins don't have to be Confluence admins and can have special permissions for a single space. For example, you are the admin for your personal space, no matter what kind of access you have anywhere else.

Want more ideas for using spaces? Check out our kickass guides on how to:

*   [Use Confluence as a Knowledge Base](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/use-confluence-as-a-knowledge-base-1477576834.html)
    
*   [Develop Technical Documentation in Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/develop-technical-documentation-in-confluence-1477576829.html)
    
*   [Use Confluence as your Intranet](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/use-confluence-as-your-intranet-1477576835.html)
    
*   [Use Confluence for Software Teams](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/confluence-for-software-teams-1477576845.html)
    

Last modified on Dec 10, 2024

Jira Data Center automation
===========================

   

The simple way to automate and extend Jira. All power, no scripts.

* * *

================================

=================

Learn about key concepts, use cases, template library, and best practices for optimizing rules.

#### Still need help?

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Atlassian+Support)

This page provides links to all the Clover documentation. We supply documentation for the latest version and previous versions of Clover, in both online and offline (downloadable) form.

Latest versions of downloadable documentation
---------------------------------------------

Use these links to download the latest version of the Clover documentation in PDF, HTML and XML formats:

*   [Clover 4.0 documentation PDF](https://confluence.atlassian.com/alldoc/files/71598770/650641563/1/1408328787139/CLOVER-4-0-20140818-PDF.pdf)
    

#### Creating your Own Local Version of our Documentation

We provide XML zip files of the documentation space, for you to download and use to create your own local version of our documentation on your Confluence site. Each XML zip file contains a Confluence-specific XML format plus attachments (screenshots etc), which you can upload to your Confluence site. Please take a look at the [instructions for uploading an XML file](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/restore-a-space-or-multiple-spaces-152036.html)
.

_Note:_ When you create your new space from our XML source code, the space will inherit the Confluence 'Documentation' theme and the customizations we have made to the header, footer and left-hand navigation bar. You will need to adjust or remove these customizations. Please refer to the [Confluence documentation](https://confluence.atlassian.com/spaces/CONF54/pages/428804673/Local+Confluence+Documentation)
 or [Jira documentation](https://confluence.atlassian.com/jira/local-jira-documentation-185729488.html)
 for details.

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Confluence has a wide range of features on its own, but you can also extend those features with Marketplace apps, and by integrating Confluence with other applications. Integrating with [Jira](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/use-jira-applications-and-confluence-together-1477576791.html)
 applications can really take your Confluence experience to the next level by improving the way your teams track vital work, and plan and release new products.

If there's an extra piece of functionality you need, the [Atlassian Marketplace](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/)
 is the place to look for useful [Confluence apps](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/home/confluence)
. Whether you need to create diagrams, like the ones you can create with [Gliffy](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/plugins/com.gliffy.integration.confluence)
, or you want to make awesome mockups and wireframes with [Balsamiq](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/plugins/com.balsamiq.confluence.plugins.mockups)
, there are heaps of great apps in the marketplace. You may even find a really useful app you never knew you needed, but now can't live without.

**In this section:**

*   [Use Jira applications and Confluence together](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/use-jira-applications-and-confluence-together-1477576791.html)
    
*   [Request Marketplace Apps](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/request-marketplace-apps-1477576798.html)

*   [Request Marketplace Apps](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/request-marketplace-apps-1477576798.html)

Analytics
=========

#### Confluence Data Center documentation

#### Related content

*   No related content found

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

This feature is available with a Confluence Data Center license.

Use analytics data to understand how your site, spaces, and pages are performing, and how active your users are. You can also export reports to share with your team.

**On this page:**

Analytics data is useful for identifying:

*   most viewed content  
*   usage and activity trends across your site 
*   frequent contributors and viewers 
*   spaces with few views that could potentially be archived. 

If required, you can set who can view analytics on your site and spaces. 

How to access analytics
-----------------------

Analytics is only available with a Confluence Data Center license. 

Your administrator may have limited who can view analytics reports. See [Administer analytics](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/administer-analytics-1477577364.html)
 for more information. 

### Access Analytics from the Confluence navigation

When you access analytics from the navigation, you get an overview of your site's usage and access to insights on spaces, users, and searches.

Go to Analytics in the Confluence header. 

See [View insights on your site](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/view-insights-on-your-site-1477576549.html)
 for more information. 

### Access Analytics from a space

When you access analytics from a space, you get an overview of all the pages and attached files in that space. 

Go to Analytics in the space sidebar. 

See [View insights on spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/view-insights-on-spaces-1477576551.html)
 for more information. 

### Access Analytics from a page

When you access analytics from a page, you can get a quick summary of insights on your page, such as total page views. From there, you can view more detailed page analytics, such as which users have viewed the page, which versions of the page they viewed, and more.

At the top of any page, click  Analytics.  Click View analytics to see the detailed analytics about your page.

See [View insights on pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/view-insights-on-pages-1477576555.html)
 for more information. 

Limit who can view analytics reports for specific spaces
--------------------------------------------------------

You can further limit who can view analytics reports for specific spaces. You need Space Administrator permission to do this. 

To change who can see analytics reports in a space:

1.  Go to the space and select **Space tools** > **Permissions** from the bottom of the sidebar.
2.  Select the **Analytics Permissions** tab. 
3.  Select **Viewing Analytics Restricted** from the drop down.
4.  Enter the users and/or groups you want to allow to view analytics reports. 
5.  Save your changes. 

Good to know:

*   If a Confluence administrator has denied a group permission to view analytics, adding the group at the space level will not grant this permission. 
*   The space will still appear in the site analytics report (if the user has permission to see the space, and use analytics globally), but they will be prevented from viewing the space analytics report if they don't have space permissions.

Analytics for Confluence marketplace app
----------------------------------------

If you previously installed the Good Software Analytics for Confluence marketplace app, you can continue to use the app until it [reaches end of life](https://confluence.atlassian.com/support/atlassian-end-of-support-policy-201851003.html)
, but your version may not contain the same features. 

  

**Analytics for Confluence** **is now part of Confluence Data Center**  
To get access to the features described on this page upgrade to Confluence Data Center 7.11 or later. [See our FAQ for all the details](https://www.atlassian.com/migration/faqs#data-center)

Last modified on Dec 10, 2024

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Confluence gives you a few ways to find what you're looking for. Here's an overview of Confluence search, and a few tips to help you find things more easily.  

How Confluence search works
---------------------------

When you enter a search term, Confluence looks for content in all spaces (including personal spaces), pages, mail, personal profiles, and space descriptions. It also looks at the content of some attached file types (Word, Text, PowerPoint, Excel, PDF, and HTML).

Search results are based on your Confluence permissions, so you'll only see content you're allowed to view.

[Learn how Confluence ranks search results](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/ranking-of-search-results-1477576590.html)

**On this page:**

**Related pages:**

*   [Confluence Search Syntax](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/confluence-search-syntax-1477576579.html)
    
*   [Confluence Search Fields](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/confluence-search-fields-1477576582.html)
    
*   [Ranking of Search Results](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/ranking-of-search-results-1477576590.html)
    
*   [Recently Viewed Pages and Blog Posts](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/recently-viewed-pages-and-blog-posts-1477576586.html)
    
*   [Search Results Macro](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/search-results-macro-1477576375.html)
    
*   [Livesearch Macro](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/livesearch-macro-1477576274.html)
    

*   [Search the People Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/search-the-people-directory-1477576583.html)
    

  

Start a search
--------------

To search Confluence:

1.  Click the search field in the top-right of Confluence to open the search panel.
2.  Start typing your search term.

Results will appear as you type — you don't need to hit enter. 

We exclude comments from your search results unless you select the comment option from the type filter.

_Screenshot: the search panel_

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/files/1477576557/1477576578/1/1733814992427/Confluence-search-results.png)

1.  **Search filters** – refine your results by space, contributor, type, date, label, or space category.
2.  **Advanced search** – go the the advanced search page.
3.  **Search tips** – get search help, and tips for refining your search.

tip/resting Created with Sketch.

**Tip:** Type **/** on your keyboard to quickly open the search panel.

Filter your search results
--------------------------

You can refine your search using interactive filters on the left-hand side of the search panel.

### Search within a space

Use the **space** filter to find content within a particular space or list of spaces. The space you're currently in will appear at the top of the list by default. Start typing the space name and choose from the list of suggested spaces.

Click the ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/files/1477576557/1477576566/1/1733814991460/search-archived-spaces-toggle.png) toggle to search within archived spaces.

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/files/1477576557/1477576577/1/1733814992341/space-dropdown-filter.png)

### Search for content by a specific person

Use the **contributor** filter to restrict your search to content modified (created, updated, or commented on) by particular people. Start typing the person's username, or part of their name, and we'll show you a list of possible matches. You can add as many people as you like.

tip/resting Created with Sketch.

**Tip:** To search for your own work, click the **Contributor** filter, then select your profile from the drop-down menu. Your name appears here by default, so it's easy to find.

### Filter by content type

Use the **type** filter to only show content of a certain type, such as pages, blog posts, comments or user profiles.

### Search within a specific time frame

Use the **date** filter to search for content last modified (created, updated, or commented on) within a particular period of time. 

To search within a specific date range:

1.  Click **Advanced Search** on the left-side of the search panel.
2.  In the **Last modified** section, choose **Custom**.
3.  Select the date range from the drop-down date picker.

### Search for content with a specific label

Use the **label** filter to search for content containing a specific label. Start typing the name of the label and choose from the list of possible matches.

### Search within a space category

Use the space category filter to search within a group of related spaces. Start typing the category name and choose from the list of possible matches. You can browse existing categories from the [Space directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf614/the-space-directory-965545160.html)
.

tip/resting Created with Sketch.

**Tip:** Space admins can organize spaces into categories. You can create space categories for departments, subject areas, office locations — whatever works for your team. [Learn how to create a space category](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/use-labels-to-categorize-spaces-136647.html)

  

Search for admin options
------------------------

As a Confluence admin, you can can quickly access admin options from the search panel.

Start typing what you want to do. We'll show the top three matching admin items at the top of your search results. You'll only see options you have permission to perform.

If you apply a search filter, admin items will no longer appear in your results.

Advanced search
---------------

The advanced search page allows you to add more search filters, such as creator, title, date range or ancestor page. 

To use advanced search:

1.  Click the search field at the top-right of Confluence.
2.  Click **Advanced search** on the left-side of the search panel.
3.  Type your keyword in the search field and hit enter.

### Adding a filter from the advanced search page

To add a search filter from the advanced search page:

1.  Click **Add a filter** on the bottom left of the advanced search page.
2.  From the drop-down menu, select the relevant filter.

_Screenshot: advanced search page_

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/files/1477576557/1477576558/1/1733814990762/advanced-search.png)

1.  **Search filters** – narrow your search by adding filters.
2.  **Add a filter** – add more filters for even more precise search.

You can choose from these additional filters:

*   **Creator** – Restrict your search to content created by a particular person. Start typing the person's username or part of their name and Confluence will offer you a list of possible matches.
*   **Label** – Only search for content containing specific labels.
*   **With parent** – Only search for direct children of a specific parent page.
*   **With ancestor** – Only search for pages below a certain page in the hierarchy.
*   **Created** – Choose or enter a date to only show content created within a particular period of time.
*   **Mentioning user** – Only search for content that mentions a particular Confluence user.
*   **With title** – Only search within page or blog titles.

These filters are provided by [Confluence Query Language (CQL)](https://developer.atlassian.com/server/confluence/advanced-searching-using-cql/)
.

Advanced search syntax
----------------------

You can also refine your search using Confluence search syntax. These are words or symbols you enter into the search field to help narrow down your results. Learn more about [Confluence Search Syntax](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/confluence-search-syntax-1477576579.html)
. 

Last modified on Dec 10, 2024

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Share your team's PDFs, Office documents, images, and more in one place by uploading your files to Confluence. Automatic versioning, instant previews, permissions, and full-text search, means shared network drives can be a thing of the past for your team.

When you upload a file it is _attached_ to the current page or blog post. This is why files are often referred to as _attachments_ in Confluence. 

You can attach anything from project plans and design mockups to video and audio files. You and your colleagues can also collaborate by [commenting on files](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/share-and-comment-on-files-1387595162.html)
 displayed on Confluence pages.

Using Files
-----------

*   [Upload Files](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/upload-files-1387595136.html)
    
*   [Display Files and Images](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/display-files-and-images-1387595139.html)
    
*   [Manage Files](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/manage-files-1387595156.html)
    
*   [Share and Comment on Files](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/share-and-comment-on-files-1387595162.html)
    
*   [Edit Files](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/edit-files-1387595169.html)
    
*   [Edit in Office using the Office Connector](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/edit-in-office-using-the-office-connector-1387595191.html)
    
*   [Install Atlassian Companion](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/install-atlassian-companion-1387595195.html)
    

**On this page:**

**Related pages:**

*   [Configuring Attachment Size](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/configuring-attachment-size-1387596209.html)
    
*   [Configuring your Attachment Storage](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/attachment-storage-configuration-1387596197.html)
    

Permissions
-----------

The 'Add Attachment' and 'Delete Attachment' permissions are used to control who can upload and delete attachments in a space.

Users with 'Add Page' or 'Add Blog' permissions can insert existing attachments to their pages, but not upload new attachments unless they also have the 'Add Attachment' permission.  

There is no permission that controls downloading attachments. See our knowledge base article about [disabling the download of attachments](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/how-to-disable-attachment-downloads-215484007.html)
 if you need to do this.

Last modified on Apr 2, 2024

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

What is a space?
----------------

Spaces are Confluence's way of organizing content into meaningful categories. Think of it like having different folders into which you can put your work.

  
Spaces come in two main varieties:

*   **Site spaces** – These spaces are found in the **[Space Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/the-space-directory-1333822110.html)** and are the areas where you create content and collaborate with others. They are sometimes called global spaces.
*   **Personal spaces** – Every Confluence user can set up a personal space which they can keep private or make public so others can view and edit. Personal spaces are listed in the **[People Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/search-the-people-directory-1333823175.html)** and found under your personal profile.

How do I use a space?
---------------------

**On this page:**

**Related pages:**

*   [Create a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/create-a-space-1333822153.html)
    
*   [Space Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/space-permissions-overview-1333822167.html)
    
*   [Navigate Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/navigate-spaces-1333822164.html)
    
*   [Organize your Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/organize-your-space-1333822184.html)
    
*   [Customize your Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/customize-your-space-1333822200.html)
    
*   [Archive a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/archive-a-space-1333822231.html)
    
*   [Export Content to Word, PDF, HTML and XML](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml-1333822239.html)
    
*   [Delete a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/delete-a-space-1333822236.html)
    

**[Create](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/create-a-space-1333822153.html)
 as many spaces as you need to get things done:**

*   **Team spaces** – Give each team (QA, HR, Engineering, Support, ...) their own space so they can focus and make their information easier for everyone to find.
*   **Project spaces** – Put all the information related to your project in one place. This allows everyone to work together in Confluence instead of emailing back and forth.
*   **Personal space** – Store everything you're working on individually, keep your to-do lists, and polish any content before you move it into another shared space.

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/files/1333822147/1333822148/1/1701933064342/dashboard-space-page-hierarchy_02.png)

**Stay up to date with spaces**

*   [Watch a space to stay up-to-date with any changes](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-1333823112.html)
    .
    
*   [Add important spaces to your dashboard so that they're easy to find again](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/organize-your-space-1333822184.html#OrganizeyourSpace-my)
    .

**Administer spaces**  
If you have admin permissions for a space you can:

*   [Change the](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/customize-your-space-1333822200.html)
     [colors, logo, sidebar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/customize-your-space-1333822200.html)
     [and](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/set-up-a-space-home-page-1333822192.html)
     [homepage](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/set-up-a-space-home-page-1333822192.html)
     [of that space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/set-up-a-space-home-page-1333822192.html)
    .
*   [Control who can view and edit your space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/space-permissions-overview-1333822167.html)
    .
*   [Make your space public to share it with people who don't have access to Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/make-a-space-public-1333822180.html)
    .

**Space Permissions**  
Some things we should make clear about space admin permissions:

*   The person who creates a space automatically has admin permissions for that space.
*   Space admins can grant admin permissions to others.
*   Space admins don't have to be Confluence admins and can have special permissions for a single space. For example, you are the admin for your personal space, no matter what kind of access you have anywhere else.

Want more ideas for using spaces? Check out our kickass guides on how to:

*   [Use Confluence as a Knowledge Base](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/use-confluence-as-a-knowledge-base-1333823431.html)
    
*   [Develop Technical Documentation in Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/develop-technical-documentation-in-confluence-1333823426.html)
    
*   [Use Confluence as your Intranet](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/use-confluence-as-your-intranet-1333823432.html)
    
*   [Use Confluence for Software Teams](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf87/confluence-for-software-teams-1333823442.html)
    

Last modified on Dec 7, 2023

*   The person who creates a space automatically has admin permissions for that space.
*   Space admins can grant admin permissions to others.
*   Space admins don't have to be Confluence admins and can have special permissions for a single space. For example, you are the admin for your personal space, no matter what kind of access you have anywhere else.

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Confluence has a wide range of features on its own, but you can also extend those features with Marketplace apps, and by integrating Confluence with other applications. Integrating with [Jira](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/use-jira-applications-and-confluence-together-1502350960.html)
 applications can really take your Confluence experience to the next level by improving the way your teams track vital work, and plan and release new products.

If there's an extra piece of functionality you need, the [Atlassian Marketplace](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/)
 is the place to look for useful [Confluence apps](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/home/confluence)
. Whether you need to create diagrams, like the ones you can create with [Gliffy](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/plugins/com.gliffy.integration.confluence)
, or you want to make awesome mockups and wireframes with [Balsamiq](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/plugins/com.balsamiq.confluence.plugins.mockups)
, there are heaps of great apps in the marketplace. You may even find a really useful app you never knew you needed, but now can't live without.

**In this section:**

*   [Use Jira applications and Confluence together](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/use-jira-applications-and-confluence-together-1502350960.html)
    
*   [Request Marketplace Apps](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/request-marketplace-apps-1502350967.html)
    
*   [Use a WebDAV Client to Work with Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/use-a-webdav-client-to-work-with-pages-1502350979.html)
    
*   [Mail Archives](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/mail-archives-1502350984.html)
    

**Related pages:**

*   [Use Jira applications and Confluence together](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/use-jira-applications-and-confluence-together-1502350960.html)
    
*   [Use a WebDAV Client to Work with Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/use-a-webdav-client-to-work-with-pages-1502350979.html)
    
*   [Request Marketplace Apps](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/request-marketplace-apps-1502350967.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

This section describes some specific use cases for Confluence.

*   **Using Confluence for technical documentation  
    **
    
    A technical communicator's guide to using Confluence – see [Develop Technical Documentation in Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/develop-technical-documentation-in-confluence-1502350998.html)
    .  
      
*   **Setting up a knowledge base  
    **
    
    A support engineer's guide to using Confluence as a knowledge base – see [Use Confluence as a Knowledge Base](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/use-confluence-as-a-knowledge-base-1502351003.html)
    .  
      
*   **Setting up an intranet  
    **
    
    A quick guide to setting up an intranet – see [Use Confluence as your Intranet](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/use-confluence-as-your-intranet-1502351004.html)
    .  
      
*   **Confluence for software teams**  
    A series of blog posts to help your agile team get the most out of Confluence. See [Confluence for Software Teams](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/confluence-for-software-teams-1502351014.html)
    .

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Confluence has a wide range of features on its own, but you can also extend those features with Marketplace apps, and by integrating Confluence with other applications. Integrating with [Jira](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/use-jira-applications-and-confluence-together-1442848552.html)
 applications can really take your Confluence experience to the next level by improving the way your teams track vital work, and plan and release new products.

If there's an extra piece of functionality you need, the [Atlassian Marketplace](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/)
 is the place to look for useful [Confluence apps](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/home/confluence)
. Whether you need to create diagrams, like the ones you can create with [Gliffy](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/plugins/com.gliffy.integration.confluence)
, or you want to make awesome mockups and wireframes with [Balsamiq](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/plugins/com.balsamiq.confluence.plugins.mockups)
, there are heaps of great apps in the marketplace. You may even find a really useful app you never knew you needed, but now can't live without.

**In this section:**

*   [Use Jira applications and Confluence together](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/use-jira-applications-and-confluence-together-1442848552.html)
