#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

As a tool for communication and collaboration, we believe Confluence is at its best when everyone can participate fully. Confluence keeps a history of all changes to pages and other content, so it's easy to see who has changed what, and reverse any changes if you need to.

Confluence does, however, give you the choice to make your site, spaces, and pages as open or closed as you want to.

Levels of permission
--------------------

There are three levels of permissions in Confluence:

*   [global permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/permissions-and-restrictions-1477576591.html#Permissionsandrestrictions-global)
    
*   [space permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/permissions-and-restrictions-1477576591.html#Permissionsandrestrictions-space)
    , and 
*   [page restrictions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/permissions-and-restrictions-1477576591.html#Permissionsandrestrictions-page)
    .

**On this page:**

**Related pages:**

*   [Confluence Groups](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/confluence-groups-1477576594.html)
    
*   [Global Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/global-permissions-overview-1477576887.html)
    
*   [Space Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/space-permissions-overview-1477575550.html)
    
*   [Page restrictions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/page-restrictions-1477575747.html)
    
*   [Configuring Confluence Security](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/configuring-confluence-security-1477577251.html)
    

### Global permissions

[Global permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/global-permissions-overview-1477576887.html)
 are site-wide permissions, and are assigned by a Confluence administrator or system administrator.

Global permissions cover things like whether a user can log in or create a space. They don't really interact with space permissions or page restrictions.

For full details, check out the [Global Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/global-permissions-overview-1477576887.html)
.

### Space permissions

Every space has its [own independent set of permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/space-permissions-overview-1477575550.html)
, managed by the space administrators, which determine the access settings for different users and groups.

They can be used to [grant or revoke permission](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/assign-space-permissions-1477575553.html)
 to view, add, edit, and delete content within that space, and can be applied to groups, users, and even to anonymous users (people who aren't logged in) if need be.

For full details, check out the [Space Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/space-permissions-overview-1477575550.html)
.

tip/resting Created with Sketch.

One thing to watch out for is where a user is a member of multiple groups. You may have revoked permission for that individual user to add pages, for example, but if they're a member of a group that _is_ allowed to add pages, they'll still be able to create new pages in the space.

If you can't get the result you want from space permissions, or you're not sure, check with one of your Confluence administrators to determine what permissions you should apply to individuals and groups.

  

### Page restrictions

Page restrictions work a little differently to global and space permissions. **Pages are open for viewing or editing by default**, but you can [restrict either viewing or editing](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/page-restrictions-1477575747.html)
 to certain users or groups if you need to. Page restrictions can be applied to published or unpublished pages and blog posts (drafts). 

Don't forget, every page in Confluence lives within a space, and space permissions allow the [space admin](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/space-permissions-overview-1477575550.html)
 to revoke permission to view content for the whole space. Even the ability to apply restrictions to pages is controlled by the 'restrict pages' space permission.

For full details, check out [Page restrictions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/page-restrictions-1477575747.html)
.

How do permissions and restrictions interact?
---------------------------------------------

You can restrict viewing of a page or blog post to certain users or groups, so that even if someone has the 'view' permission for the space, they won't be able to view the content of the page or blog post.

If someone is a space admin and you've used page restrictions to prevent them viewing a page, they won't be able to see the page when they navigate to it. As a space admin though, they can see a list of restricted pages in the space and remove the restrictions. 

Check who can view a page
-------------------------

To check who can view a page, go to 

**More options** ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/files/1477575218/1477575219/1/1733814851456/ellipsis.png) 

 > **People who can view**. This will show a list of people, including administrators, who have space permission to see the space, and are not prevented by page restrictions from seeing the page.  See [Check who can view a page](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/check-who-can-view-a-page-1477576595.html)
 for more information. 

The diagram below shows the points at which someone could be prevented from viewing a page. 

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/files/1477576591/1477576592/1/1733814993950/permissions-restrictions-bar-chart.png)

*   **Site** - they don't have permission to log in to Confluence.
*   **Space** - they don't have permission to view the space.
*   **Parent page** - the current page has a view restriction preventing them from viewing.
*   **Child page** - the current page is inheriting a view restriction from another page higher up in the page hierarchy, preventing them from viewing.  

  

**What about links?**

Space permissions and page restrictions affect how [links between Confluence pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/links-1477575764.html)
 are displayed.

*   If someone doesn't have 'View' space permission, links to pages in that space will be visible, but they'll get a "page not found" message. The space key is not revealed in the link URL. 
*   If someone has the "View" space permission, but the page has view restrictions, the link will be visible but they'll get an "access denied" message when they click the link. 

[Links to attachments](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/links-1477575764.html)
 are also affected. If the visitor doesn't have permission to view the page the attachment lives on, the link won't be rendered.

Last modified on Dec 10, 2024

Your profile and settings
=========================

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Confluence is very flexible – not only in the many ways you can create and share content, but also in how you can tailor your own Confluence experience. Things like your [profile picture](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/set-your-profile-picture-1477576454.html)
, [favorite spaces and pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/save-for-later-1477576473.html)
, and your [personal space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/create-a-space-1477575536.html)
 can say a lot about you, and can also make navigating Confluence much quicker and easier. Even a simple thing like [adding shortcut links to the sidebar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/configure-the-sidebar-1477575587.html)
 of your personal space, can save you a lot of time in finding the things you use all the time.

[Set up your personal space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/create-a-space-1477575536.html)
, and take a look at any of the pages below, to start making Confluence feel like home.

*   [Your user profile](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/your-user-profile-1477576446.html)
    
*   [Change Your Password](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/change-your-password-1477576449.html)
    
*   [Edit Your User Settings](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/edit-your-user-settings-1477576451.html)
    
*   [Set Your Profile Picture](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/set-your-profile-picture-1477576454.html)
    
*   [Choose Your Home Page](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/choose-your-home-page-1477576468.html)
    
*   [Save for later](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/save-for-later-1477576473.html)
    
*   [View and revoke OAuth access tokens](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/view-and-revoke-oauth-access-tokens-1477576482.html)
    
*   [Set and use your preferred theme](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/set-and-use-your-preferred-theme-1477576485.html)
    

**Related pages:**

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Pages and blog posts allow you to capture and share information in Confluence.

Whether it's taking down some quick [notes from a meeting](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/meeting-notes-blueprint-1502350097.html)
, writing a [requirements page](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/product-requirements-blueprint-1502350106.html)
, or letting your teammates know about the company's latest marketing push – you can create it as a Confluence page or blog post.

[Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/create-and-edit-pages-1502349796.html)
 are great for when you want the information to last and evolve over time. If it's a point-in-time update or one-time communication then a [blog post](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/blog-posts-1502349802.html)
 is the way to go. These aren't hard-and-fast rules; they're just pointers to give you a place to start.

Each Confluence space, including your personal space, allows you to create pages in it, and has its own blog where you can create posts. If you're not sure what a space is, or what you can do with spaces, check out our page on [Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/spaces-1502349694.html)
.

**Related pages:**

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Pages and blog posts allow you to capture and share information in Confluence.

Whether it's taking down some quick [notes from a meeting](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/meeting-notes-blueprint-1477575932.html)
, writing a [requirements page](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/product-requirements-blueprint-1477575941.html)
, or letting your teammates know about the company's latest marketing push – you can create it as a Confluence page or blog post.

[Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/create-and-edit-pages-1477575632.html)
 are great for when you want the information to last and evolve over time. If it's a point-in-time update or one-time communication then a [blog post](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/blog-posts-1477575638.html)
 is the way to go. These aren't hard-and-fast rules; they're just pointers to give you a place to start.

Each Confluence space, including your personal space, allows you to create pages in it, and has its own blog where you can create posts. If you're not sure what a space is, or what you can do with spaces, check out our page on [Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/spaces-1477575530.html)
.

**Related pages:**

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

What is a space?
----------------

Spaces are Confluence's way of organizing content into meaningful categories. Think of it like having different folders into which you can put your work.

  
Spaces come in two main varieties:

*   **Site spaces** – These spaces are found in the **[Space Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/the-space-directory-1502349656.html)** and are the areas where you create content and collaborate with others. They are sometimes called global spaces.
*   **Personal spaces** – Every Confluence user can set up a personal space which they can keep private or make public so others can view and edit. Personal spaces are listed in the **[People Directory](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/search-the-people-directory-1502350752.html)** and found under your personal profile.

How do I use a space?
---------------------

**On this page:**

**Related pages:**

*   [Create a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/create-a-space-1502349700.html)
    
*   [Space Permissions Overview](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/space-permissions-overview-1502349714.html)
    
*   [Navigate Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/navigate-spaces-1502349711.html)
    
*   [Organize your Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/organize-your-space-1502349732.html)
    
*   [Customize your Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/customize-your-space-1502349748.html)
    
*   [Archive a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/archive-a-space-1502349779.html)
    
*   [Export Content to Word, PDF, HTML and XML](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml-1502349787.html)
    
*   [Delete a Space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/delete-a-space-1502349784.html)
    

**[Create](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/create-a-space-1502349700.html)
 as many spaces as you need to get things done:**

*   **Team spaces** – Give each team (QA, HR, Engineering, Support, ...) their own space so they can focus and make their information easier for everyone to find.
*   **Project spaces** – Put all the information related to your project in one place. This allows everyone to work together in Confluence instead of emailing back and forth.
*   **Personal space** – Store everything you're working on individually, keep your to-do lists, and polish any content before you move it into another shared space.

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/files/1502349694/1502349695/1/1738669665933/dashboard-space-page-hierarchy_02.png)

**Stay up to date with spaces**

*   [Watch a space to stay up-to-date with any changes](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-1502350689.html)
    .
    
*   [Add important spaces to your dashboard so that they're easy to find again](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/organize-your-space-1502349732.html#OrganizeyourSpace-my)
    .

**Administer spaces**  
If you have admin permissions for a space you can:

*   [Change the](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/customize-your-space-1502349748.html)
     [colors, logo, sidebar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/customize-your-space-1502349748.html)
     [and](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/set-up-a-space-home-page-1502349740.html)
     [homepage](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/set-up-a-space-home-page-1502349740.html)
     [of that space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/set-up-a-space-home-page-1502349740.html)
    .
*   [Control who can view and edit your space](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/space-permissions-overview-1502349714.html)
    .
*   [Make your space public to share it with people who don't have access to Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/make-a-space-public-1502349728.html)
    .

**Space Permissions**  
Some things we should make clear about space admin permissions:

*   The person who creates a space automatically has admin permissions for that space.
*   Space admins can grant admin permissions to others.
*   Space admins don't have to be Confluence admins and can have special permissions for a single space. For example, you are the admin for your personal space, no matter what kind of access you have anywhere else.

Want more ideas for using spaces? Check out our kickass guides on how to:

*   [Use Confluence as a Knowledge Base](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/use-confluence-as-a-knowledge-base-1502351003.html)
    
*   [Develop Technical Documentation in Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/develop-technical-documentation-in-confluence-1502350998.html)
    
*   [Use Confluence as your Intranet](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/use-confluence-as-your-intranet-1502351004.html)
    
*   [Use Confluence for Software Teams](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/confluence-for-software-teams-1502351014.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

**Team Calendars for Confluence** **is now part of Confluence Data Center**  
To get access to the features described on this page upgrade to Confluence Data Center 7.11 or later. Can’t upgrade yet? Depending on your current Data Center version, you can access these features by installing the latest version of the app (at no cost). [See our FAQ for all the details](https://www.atlassian.com/migration/faqs#data-center)

Team Calendars provides one place for fast-moving teams working in Confluence to connect their schedules of leave, Jira projects and events, or whatever use case your team has.

*   [Team Calendars Quick Tour](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/team-calendars-quick-tour-1442848372.html)
    
*   [Create, Add, and Edit Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/create-add-and-edit-calendars-1442848392.html)
    
*   [Restrict a Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/restrict-a-calendar-1442848403.html)
    
*   [Watch a Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/watch-a-calendar-1442848457.html)
    
*   [Share Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/share-calendars-1442848464.html)
    
*   [Export Team Calendars Content to Other Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/export-team-calendars-content-to-other-calendars-1442848537.html)
    
*   [Delete or Remove a Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/delete-or-remove-a-calendar-1442848543.html)
    

Create, organize, and find events easily. Team Calendars offers great tools for planning and managing your events, whether they're travel, conferences, birthdays, or JIRA issues and sprints. 

*   [Add Events](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/add-events-1442848409.html)
    
*   [Add Jira Events](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/add-jira-events-1442848418.html)
    
*   [Event Types](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/event-types-1442848436.html)
    
*   [Custom Event Types](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/custom-event-types-1442848440.html)
    
*   [Reminders](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/reminders-1442848445.html)
    
*   [Embed Calendars on Confluence Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/embed-calendars-on-confluence-pages-1442848452.html)
    

Calendar subscriptions provide a simple and effective way to stay up to date with holidays, conferences, and more. 

You can subscribe to team calendars from many of your your favourite third-party calendar apps:

*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Microsoft Outlook](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-microsoft-outlook-1442848477.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Outlook on the web](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-outlook-on-the-web-1442848492.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Apple Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-apple-calendar-1442848493.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Apple iOS Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-apple-ios-calendar-1442848498.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Google Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-google-calendar-1442848503.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Android Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-android-calendar-1442848513.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Thunderbird](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-thunderbird-1442848516.html)
    

Alternatively, subscribe to a third-party calendar in Team Calendars:

*   [Subscribe to Outlook Calendar from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/subscribe-to-outlook-calendar-from-team-calendars-1442848520.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Apple Calendar from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/subscribe-to-apple-calendar-from-team-calendars-1442848522.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Google Calendar from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/subscribe-to-google-calendar-from-team-calendars-1442848525.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Opsgenie Calendars from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/subscribe-to-opsgenie-calendars-from-team-calendars-1442848530.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to PagerDuty Schedules from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/subscribe-to-pagerduty-schedules-from-team-calendars-1442848532.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Teamup Calendars from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf91/subscribe-to-teamup-calendars-from-team-calendars-1442848534.html)

Pages and blogs
===============

#### Confluence Data Center documentation

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Pages and blog posts allow you to capture and share information in Confluence.

Whether it's taking down some quick [notes from a meeting](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/meeting-notes-blueprint-1387595044.html)
, writing a [requirements page](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/product-requirements-blueprint-1387595053.html)
, or letting your teammates know about the company's latest marketing push – you can create it as a Confluence page or blog post.

[Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/create-and-edit-pages-1387594747.html)
 are great for when you want the information to last and evolve over time. If it's a point-in-time update or one-time communication then a [blog post](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/blog-posts-1387594752.html)
 is the way to go. These aren't hard-and-fast rules; they're just pointers to give you a place to start.

Each Confluence space, including your personal space, allows you to create pages in it, and has its own blog where you can create posts. If you're not sure what a space is, or what you can do with spaces, check out our page on [Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/spaces-1387594646.html)
.

**Related pages:**

*   [Create and Edit Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/create-and-edit-pages-1387594747.html)
    
*   [Blog Posts](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/blog-posts-1387594752.html)

*   [Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf89/spaces-1387594646.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Pages and blog posts allow you to capture and share information in Confluence.

Whether it's taking down some quick [notes from a meeting](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/meeting-notes-blueprint-1283359916.html)
, writing a [requirements page](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/product-requirements-blueprint-1283359925.html)
, or letting your teammates know about the company's latest marketing push – you can create it as a Confluence page or blog post.

[Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/create-and-edit-pages-1283359622.html)
 are great for when you want the information to last and evolve over time. If it's a point-in-time update or one-time communication then a [blog post](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/blog-posts-1283359627.html)
 is the way to go. These aren't hard-and-fast rules; they're just pointers to give you a place to start.

Each Confluence space, including your personal space, allows you to create pages in it, and has its own blog where you can create posts. If you're not sure what a space is, or what you can do with spaces, check out our page on [Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/spaces-1283359521.html)
.

**Related pages:**

*   [Create and Edit Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/create-and-edit-pages-1283359622.html)
    
*   [Blog Posts](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf85/blog-posts-1283359627.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Pages and blog posts allow you to capture and share information in Confluence.

Whether it's taking down some quick [notes from a meeting](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/meeting-notes-blueprint-1425052273.html)
, writing a [requirements page](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/product-requirements-blueprint-1425052282.html)
, or letting your teammates know about the company's latest marketing push – you can create it as a Confluence page or blog post.

[Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/create-and-edit-pages-1425051973.html)
 are great for when you want the information to last and evolve over time. If it's a point-in-time update or one-time communication then a [blog post](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/blog-posts-1425051979.html)
 is the way to go. These aren't hard-and-fast rules; they're just pointers to give you a place to start.

Each Confluence space, including your personal space, allows you to create pages in it, and has its own blog where you can create posts. If you're not sure what a space is, or what you can do with spaces, check out our page on [Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/spaces-1425051872.html)
.

**Related pages:**

*   [Create and Edit Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/create-and-edit-pages-1425051973.html)

*   [Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/spaces-1425051872.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

**Team Calendars for Confluence** **is now part of Confluence Data Center**  
To get access to the features described on this page upgrade to Confluence Data Center 7.11 or later. Can’t upgrade yet? Depending on your current Data Center version, you can access these features by installing the latest version of the app (at no cost). [See our FAQ for all the details](https://www.atlassian.com/migration/faqs#data-center)

Team Calendars provides one place for fast-moving teams working in Confluence to connect their schedules of leave, Jira projects and events, or whatever use case your team has.

*   [Team Calendars Quick Tour](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/team-calendars-quick-tour-1477576611.html)
    
*   [Create, Add, and Edit Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/create-add-and-edit-calendars-1477576631.html)
    
*   [Restrict a Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/restrict-a-calendar-1477576642.html)
    
*   [Watch a Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/watch-a-calendar-1477576696.html)
    
*   [Share Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/share-calendars-1477576703.html)
    
*   [Export Team Calendars Content to Other Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/export-team-calendars-content-to-other-calendars-1477576776.html)
    
*   [Delete or Remove a Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/delete-or-remove-a-calendar-1477576782.html)
    

Create, organize, and find events easily. Team Calendars offers great tools for planning and managing your events, whether they're travel, conferences, birthdays, or JIRA issues and sprints. 

*   [Add Events](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/add-events-1477576648.html)
    
*   [Add Jira Events](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/add-jira-events-1477576657.html)
    
*   [Event Types](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/event-types-1477576675.html)
    
*   [Custom Event Types](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/custom-event-types-1477576679.html)
    
*   [Reminders](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/reminders-1477576684.html)
    
*   [Embed Calendars on Confluence Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/embed-calendars-on-confluence-pages-1477576691.html)
    

Calendar subscriptions provide a simple and effective way to stay up to date with holidays, conferences, and more. 

You can subscribe to team calendars from many of your your favourite third-party calendar apps:

*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Microsoft Outlook](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-microsoft-outlook-1477576716.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Outlook on the web](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-outlook-on-the-web-1477576731.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Apple Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-apple-calendar-1477576732.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Apple iOS Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-apple-ios-calendar-1477576737.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Google Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-google-calendar-1477576742.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Android Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-android-calendar-1477576752.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Thunderbird](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-thunderbird-1477576755.html)
    

Alternatively, subscribe to a third-party calendar in Team Calendars:

*   [Subscribe to Outlook Calendar from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/subscribe-to-outlook-calendar-from-team-calendars-1477576759.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Apple Calendar from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/subscribe-to-apple-calendar-from-team-calendars-1477576761.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Google Calendar from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/subscribe-to-google-calendar-from-team-calendars-1477576764.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Opsgenie Calendars from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/subscribe-to-opsgenie-calendars-from-team-calendars-1477576769.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to PagerDuty Schedules from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/subscribe-to-pagerduty-schedules-from-team-calendars-1477576771.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Teamup Calendars from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/subscribe-to-teamup-calendars-from-team-calendars-1477576773.html)

#### Creating your Own Local Version of our Documentation

We provide XML zip files of the documentation space, for you to download and use to create your own local version of our documentation on your Confluence site. Each XML zip file contains a Confluence-specific XML format plus attachments (screenshots etc), which you can upload to your Confluence site. Please take a look at the [instructions for uploading an XML file](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/restore-a-space-or-multiple-spaces-152036.html)
.

_Note:_ When you create your new space from our XML source code, the space will inherit the Confluence 'Documentation' theme and the customizations we have made to the header, footer and left-hand navigation bar. You will need to adjust or remove these customizations. Please refer to the [Confluence documentation](https://confluence.atlassian.com/spaces/CONF54/pages/428804673/Local+Confluence+Documentation)
 or [Jira documentation](https://confluence.atlassian.com/jira/local-jira-documentation-185729488.html)
 for details.

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

**Team Calendars for Confluence** **is now part of Confluence Data Center**  
To get access to the features described on this page upgrade to Confluence Data Center 7.11 or later. Can’t upgrade yet? Depending on your current Data Center version, you can access these features by installing the latest version of the app (at no cost). [See our FAQ for all the details](https://www.atlassian.com/migration/faqs#data-center)

Team Calendars provides one place for fast-moving teams working in Confluence to connect their schedules of leave, Jira projects and events, or whatever use case your team has.

*   [Team Calendars Quick Tour](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/team-calendars-quick-tour-1425052940.html)
    
*   [Create, Add, and Edit Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/create-add-and-edit-calendars-1425052960.html)
    
*   [Restrict a Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/restrict-a-calendar-1425052971.html)
    
*   [Watch a Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/watch-a-calendar-1425053025.html)
    
*   [Share Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/share-calendars-1425053032.html)
    
*   [Export Team Calendars Content to Other Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/export-team-calendars-content-to-other-calendars-1425053105.html)
    
*   [Delete or Remove a Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/delete-or-remove-a-calendar-1425053111.html)
    

Create, organize, and find events easily. Team Calendars offers great tools for planning and managing your events, whether they're travel, conferences, birthdays, or JIRA issues and sprints. 

*   [Add Events](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/add-events-1425052977.html)
    
*   [Add Jira Events](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/add-jira-events-1425052986.html)
    
*   [Event Types](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/event-types-1425053004.html)
    
*   [Custom Event Types](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/custom-event-types-1425053008.html)
    
*   [Reminders](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/reminders-1425053013.html)
    
*   [Embed Calendars on Confluence Pages](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/embed-calendars-on-confluence-pages-1425053020.html)
    

Calendar subscriptions provide a simple and effective way to stay up to date with holidays, conferences, and more. 

You can subscribe to team calendars from many of your your favourite third-party calendar apps:

*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Microsoft Outlook](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-microsoft-outlook-1425053045.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Outlook on the web](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-outlook-on-the-web-1425053060.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Apple Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-apple-calendar-1425053061.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Apple iOS Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-apple-ios-calendar-1425053066.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Google Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-google-calendar-1425053071.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Android Calendar](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-android-calendar-1425053081.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Thunderbird](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-thunderbird-1425053084.html)
    

Alternatively, subscribe to a third-party calendar in Team Calendars:

*   [Subscribe to Outlook Calendar from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/subscribe-to-outlook-calendar-from-team-calendars-1425053088.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Apple Calendar from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/subscribe-to-apple-calendar-from-team-calendars-1425053090.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Google Calendar from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/subscribe-to-google-calendar-from-team-calendars-1425053093.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Opsgenie Calendars from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/subscribe-to-opsgenie-calendars-from-team-calendars-1425053098.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to PagerDuty Schedules from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/subscribe-to-pagerduty-schedules-from-team-calendars-1425053100.html)
    
*   [Subscribe to Teamup Calendars from Team Calendars](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf90/subscribe-to-teamup-calendars-from-team-calendars-1425053102.html)

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Macros are how you supercharge your Confluence pages. 

You can use macros to:

*   change the format and layout of your page
*   display media like video, audio, and social media content
*   collate and organise Confluence pages, blogs, and files
*   perform actions from a page, such as creating a page from a template. 

Take your Confluence space to the next level using macros. 

**On this page:**

_Screenshot: Page containing_ _Status, Page Properties Report, Livesearch, and Profile Picture macros to_ _help people find information about particular projects._

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/files/1573750550/1573750551/1/1749821476182/many-macros-view.png)

Macro basics
------------

### Add a macro to your page

To add a macro to your page:

1.  From the editor toolbar, select **Insert** ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/files/1573749640/1573749641/1/1749821370736/insert-macro-icon.png), then **Other Macros**.
    
2.  Select a macro from the list.
3.  Enter any required parameters.
4.  Choose **Insert**.

In the editor you'll see a placeholder that represents the macro. Once you publish your page, you'll see the macro in its full glory. 

### Edit a macro

Macro parameters are used to change the behavior of a macro.

To change the macro parameters:

1.  In the editor, click the macro placeholder and select **Edit**.  
    ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/files/1573749545/1573749546/1/1749821360798/edit-cheese.png)
2.  Update the parameters as required then select **Insert**.

Head to the documentation for specific macros below for full details of the parameters available in each macro.

Confluence macros
-----------------

Here's a list of macros currently available with Confluence Data Center.

Click a macro name for details of the usage, including optional parameters and examples.

*   [Advanced Roadmaps Macro](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/advanced-roadmaps-macro-1573750553.html)

Get more macros
---------------

This documentation provides information on all the macros that are provided with Confluence by default. There may be other macros in your site, from Marketplace Apps, or perhaps developed in-house. 

### Third-party macros from the Atlassian Marketplace

You can find a wide range of Atlassian and third party macros at [The Marketplace](http://marketplace.atlassian.com/)
. These are distributed as apps and can be installed by a Confluence Administrator.

### Create your own macros

Users with System Administrator permissions can create user macros - see [Writing User Macros](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/writing-user-macros-1573751359.html)
. 

If you want to create something more complex, you can develop your own plugin - see [Writing Confluence Plugins](https://developer.atlassian.com/display/CONFDEV/Writing+Confluence+Plugins)
.

Macro usage statistics
----------------------

Confluence administrators can check how often each macro is used in your site.

To see how often a macro is used, go to **Administration** menu ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/files/1573749741/1573749742/1/1749821380723/cog.png) , then **General Configuration**  > **Macro Usage**.  This lists how often each macro is used in current spaces but doesn't include any macros used on pages in archived spaces or macros provided by disabled apps.

Unknown macro
-------------

In view page mode, disabled macros show a placeholder image:

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/files/1573750550/1573750552/1/1749821476352/unknown-macro.png)

When macros with body content are disabled, the content will be preserved and display in the editor. 

To display the content in view mode:

1.  In the editor, select the macro and choose **Edit**.
2.  In the macro browser, select **Back.**
3.  Search for a new macro and select it.
4.  Check your content displays in the **Preview** pane.
5.  Select **Insert**.

In the editor, the disabled macro will switch to the new macro and display your content. Update the page to display the content in view mode.

  

tip/resting Created with Sketch.

**Do more with Confluence**

Extend Confluence with one of the hundreds of other macros in the [Atlassian Marketplace](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/search?category=Macros&hosting=server)
, such as:

*   [Composition Tabs & Page Layouts](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/245/composition-tabs-page-layout?hosting=server&tab=overview)
    : Toggle or expand the visible of portions of your pages with the Toggle and Cloak macro
*   [Content Formatting for Confluence](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/247/content-formatting-for-confluence?hosting=server&tab=overview)
    : Over 30 easy-to-use Confluence macros gives you the ability to create better, more engaging content

Last modified on Jun 13, 2025

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Advanced+Roadmaps+for+Jira)

We've created this set of support guides to help you with any GDPR-specific obligations that you may have. The guides are designed to identify where personal data may be located within the product, and, where possible, assist with responding to data subject access requests, including in modifying, restricting or deleting data.

Where a workaround involves running SQL scripts directly on your database, we strongly recommend you do this first on a non-production, staging or test environment before running the scripts on your production database. We also strongly recommend you take a backup of your data before making any modifications via SQL scripts.

*   [Right to erasure in Advanced Roadmaps Server and Data Center](https://confluence.atlassian.com/advancedroadmapsserver/right-to-erasure-inadvanced-roadmaps-server-and-data-center-962968642.html)
    
*   [Right to rectification in Advanced Roadmaps Server and Data Center](https://confluence.atlassian.com/advancedroadmapsserver/right-to-rectificationinadvanced-roadmaps-server-and-data-center-962968643.html)
    

GDPR and Atlassian Server and Data Center applications
------------------------------------------------------

We understand that customers using Atlassian Server or Data Center applications may have GDPR-specific obligations with respect to the data stored in and processed through those applications. For example, customers may need to access, modify or delete personal data upon request by certain individuals.  We've prepared a GDPR FAQ, and guides for our Server and Data Center products, and hope they'll be helpful in assisting our customers with their GDPR-specific obligations.

Please note, when you use Atlassian Server or Data Center applications, Atlassian does not access, collect, store or otherwise process personal data you choose to store within those applications, except in limited cases where such data is provided for incidental support services.  For this reason, Atlassian is neither a data processor nor data controller under GDPR for the purposes of the personal data you choose to store within Atlassian Server or Data Center applications.  Atlassian may access analytics events associated with your technical use of the products, but only where permitted by your administrator.  Such analytics information is filtered to exclude any personal data prior to the analytics information leaving your environment.  For more information on the analytics information collected through Atlassian downloadable products, please see our [Privacy Policy](https://www.atlassian.com/legal/privacy-policy)
.  

**Using multiple Atlassian products?**

Check our our GDPR support guides for your Atlassian products:

*   [Bamboo Server GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/gdpr/bamboo-server-gdpr-support-guides-949245655.html)
    
*   [Bitbucket Server and Data Center GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/bitbucketserver/bitbucket-server-and-data-center-gdpr-support-guides-962360101.html)
    
*   [Confluence Server and Data Center GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/gdpr-support-guides-for-confluence-data-center-960696666.html)
    
*   [Crowd Server and Data Center GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/gdpr/crowd-server-and-data-center-gdpr-support-guides-949245650.html)
    
*   [Fisheye and Crucible Server GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/gdpr/fisheye-and-crucible-server-gdpr-support-guides-949245659.html)
    
*   [Hipchat Server GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/hc/hipchat-server-gdpr-support-guides-962356659.html)
    
*   [Hipchat Data Center GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/hipchatdc3/hipchat-data-center-gdpr-support-guides-962356685.html)
    
*   [Jira Core, Jira Software and Jira Service Management (formerly Jira Service Desk) Server and Data Center GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/gdpr/jira-core-jira-software-and-jira-service-desk-server-and-data-center-gdpr-support-guides-949245619.html)
     
*   [Advanced Roadmaps (formerly Portfolio for Jira) Server and Data Center GDPR support guides](https://confluence.atlassian.com/advancedroadmapsserver/advanced-roadmaps-gdpr-support-guides-962968641.html)

Crashes and Performance Troubleshooting
=======================================

#### Related content

*   No related content found

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Troubleshooting Confluence performance problems requires distinguishing between different kinds of problems. The definitions below will help to clarify the problem for your own research and for the quick resolution of an Atlassian support issue.

Confluence "crashed", "hangs" or "went down"
--------------------------------------------

The terms _crash_, _hang_, and _went down_ do not describe the problem with Confluence specifically enough to help with diagnosing or resolving the problem.

See the list of more specific descriptions below to see which matches the problem most closely.

Confluence fails to start
-------------------------

A very serious problem is a _startup issue_, where Confluence prints an error message to the logs when it starts and will not respond to any requests at all. If Confluence does not resume processing requests normally after being restarted, follow [Confluence Startup Problems Troubleshooting](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/confluence-startup-problems-troubleshooting-180846988.html)
.

**Articles on Confluence failing to start:**

*   Page:
    
    [Confluence with JNDI Connector Crashes Under Heavy Load Due to Database Connection Issues](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/confluence-with-jndi-connector-crashes-under-heavy-load-due-to-database-connection-issues-790961558.html)
    
*   Page:
    
    [IBM JDK\_1.6 prevents starting on Zlinux Suse Sles10](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/ibm-jdk_1-6-prevents-starting-on-zlinux-suse-sles10-170494112.html)
    

Confluence is not responding
----------------------------

If Confluence is _not responding_ during a period of normal operation, there is usually a serious performance problem on the server which is preventing requests being processed normally. If the server is not responding, the Confluence administrator should follow the [Troubleshooting Confluence hanging or crashing](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/troubleshooting-confluence-hanging-or-crashing-163415613.html)
 guide. Additionally, verify if the issue is related to a JVM memory problem, as insufficient heap size can cause unresponsiveness. Refer to the 'JVM Issues' section for details on addressing heap memory errors.

**Articles that address Confluence not responding:**

*   Page:
    
    [Confluence crashes due to insufficient disk space and truncated confluence.cfg.xml file](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/confluence-crashes-due-to-insufficient-disk-space-and-truncated-confluence-cfg-xml-file-119800044.html)
    
*   Page:
    
    [Confluence fails to rebuild index and hangs](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/confluence-fails-to-rebuild-index-and-hangs-129171492.html)
    
*   Page:
    
    [Confluence unresponsive in virtual environment during garbage collection](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/confluence-unresponsive-in-virtual-environment-during-garbage-collection-302809717.html)
    
*   Page:
    
    [Segfault error - Confluence unavailable due to Java crash](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/segfault-error-confluence-unavailable-due-to-java-crash-300810722.html)
    
*   Page:
    
    [Unable to compile Soy template in plugin module](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/unable-to-compile-soy-template-in-plugin-module-415367799.html)
    
*   Page:
    
    [XML backups cause performance problems or 'java.lang.OutOfMemoryError Java heap space'](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/xml-backups-cause-performance-problems-or-java-lang-outofmemoryerror-java-heap-space-182681992.html)
    

Confluence is responding slowly

A _performance issue_ is when Confluence responds slowly to requests. You should take note of whether some kinds of pages load faster than others. If all the pages in the application are slow to respond, review the [Troubleshooting Confluence hanging or crashing](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/troubleshooting-confluence-hanging-or-crashing-163415613.html)
 guide.

If Confluence is responding slowly on just a few pages, you can look at [Performance Tuning](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/performance-tuning-130289.html)
 to look for specific performance tuning ideas. This is the point when you should begin to gather all the information in [Requesting Performance Support](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/requesting-performance-support-165851.html)
. [Troubleshooting Slow Performance Using Page Request Profiling](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/troubleshooting-slow-performance-using-page-request-profiling-200987.html)
 is especially useful when particular pages are loading slowly.

**Articles that address Confluence responding slowly:**

*   Page:
    
    [Assigning permissions to a page and People Directory performance is slow due to database latency](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/assigning-permissions-to-a-page-and-people-directory-performance-is-slow-due-to-database-latency-218278049.html)
    
*   Page:
    
    [Confluence process takes up 100% CPU due to legacy Calendar plugin](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/confluence-process-takes-up-100-cpu-due-to-legacy-calendar-plugin-181538048.html)
    
*   Page:
    
    [Editing a page loads slowly in Internet Explorer](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/editing-a-page-loads-slowly-in-internet-explorer-198806737.html)
    
*   Page:
    
    [Index flush trigger causes poor performance](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/index-flush-trigger-causes-poor-performance-182682014.html)
    
*   Page:
    
    [Performance impact due to index optimization](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/performance-impact-due-to-index-optimization-182682004.html)
    
*   Page:
    
    [Poor performance due to Confluence Usage Tracking plugin](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/poor-performance-due-to-confluence-usage-tracking-plugin-181537459.html)
    
*   Page:
    
    [Poor performance from excessive garbage collection in the Excel plugin](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/poor-performance-from-excessive-garbage-collection-in-the-excel-plugin-213517148.html)
    
*   Page:
    
    [Poor performance on bandana table queries](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/poor-performance-on-bandana-table-queries-192053505.html)
    
*   Page:
    
    [Search and dashboard load slowly - index timestamp is not updated due to file permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/search-and-dashboard-load-slowly-index-timestamp-is-not-updated-due-to-file-permissions-104300767.html)
    

Confluence is displaying errors in the UI
-----------------------------------------

If Confluence _returns error pages_, take note of:

*   which operations in the application return error pages
*   which users are affected.

If the error pages are displayed only after a long wait, you should look at the _Confluence is responding slowly_ section above. If the error pages return quickly, follow the [Troubleshooting Problems and Requesting Technical Support](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/troubleshooting-problems-and-requesting-technical-support-776659671.html)
 guide.

JVM Issues
----------

If Confluence does not respond to requests at all, the java process for Confluence is no longer running, and there is an hs\_err\_pid\*.log file, the [JVM has crashed](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/java-crashes-235669496.html)
. One common cause is an 'OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space'. This error occurs when the allocated Java heap memory is insufficient for Confluence's requirements. Symptoms may include unresponsiveness, instability, or crashes during operation. To resolve this issue, increase the Java heap memory allocation by adjusting the Xms (initial memory) and Xmx (maximum memory) parameters. For most environments, a minimum of 4 GB heap size is recommended, and this value can be adjusted based on server resources and instance size. Refer to the '[Configuring System Properties](https://support.atlassian.com/confluence/kb/how-to-set-system-properties-for-confluence-running-as-a-service-on-windows)
' or '[How to Increase Heap Memory for Confluence](https://support.atlassian.com/confluence/kb/fix-javalangoutofmemoryerror-in-confluence/)
' articles for step-by-step instructions.

**Articles that address JVM issues:**

*   Page:
    
    ['Not enough storage is available to process this command' thrown when using jstack to generate external thread dumps](https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/not-enough-storage-is-available-to-process-this-command-thrown-when-using-jstack-to-generate-external-thread-dumps-267256354.html)
    
*   Page:
    
    [\_Blacklisted JDK versions for Confluence](https://confluence.atlassian.com/kb/_blacklisted-jdk-versions-for-confluence-709558378.html)
    

Performance Data Collector
--------------------------

The [Performance Data Collector](https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/performance-data-collection)
 is a server-side, standalone application that exposes a number of REST APIs for collecting performance data. It can be used to collect data, such as thread dumps, disk speed and CPU usage information, to troubleshoot performance problems.

See [How to use the Performance Data Collector](https://confluence.atlassian.com/kb/how-to-use-the-performance-data-collector-957980877.html)
 for more information. 

Last modified on Jan 21, 2026

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Macros are how you supercharge your Confluence pages. 

You can use macros to:

*   change the format and layout of your page
*   display media like video, audio, and social media content
*   collate and organise Confluence pages, blogs, and files
*   perform actions from a page, such as creating a page from a template. 

Take your Confluence space to the next level using macros. 

**On this page:**

_Screenshot: Page containing_ _Status, Page Properties Report, Livesearch, and Profile Picture macros to_ _help people find information about particular projects._

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/files/1627457040/1627457041/1/1755867900164/many-macros-view.png)

Macro basics
------------

### Add a macro to your page

To add a macro to your page:

1.  From the editor toolbar, select **Insert** ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/files/1627456130/1627456131/1/1755867803683/insert-macro-icon.png), then **Other Macros**.
    
2.  Select a macro from the list.
3.  Enter any required parameters.
4.  Choose **Insert**.

In the editor you'll see a placeholder that represents the macro. Once you publish your page, you'll see the macro in its full glory. 

### Edit a macro

Macro parameters are used to change the behavior of a macro.

To change the macro parameters:

1.  In the editor, click the macro placeholder and select **Edit**.  
    ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/files/1627456035/1627456036/1/1755867793950/edit-cheese.png)
2.  Update the parameters as required then select **Insert**.

Head to the documentation for specific macros below for full details of the parameters available in each macro.

Confluence macros
-----------------

Here's a list of macros currently available with Confluence Data Center.

Click a macro name for details of the usage, including optional parameters and examples.

Get more macros
---------------

This documentation provides information on all the macros that are provided with Confluence by default. There may be other macros in your site, from Marketplace Apps, or perhaps developed in-house. 

### Third-party macros from the Atlassian Marketplace

You can find a wide range of Atlassian and third party macros at [The Marketplace](http://marketplace.atlassian.com/)
. These are distributed as apps and can be installed by a Confluence Administrator.

### Create your own macros

Users with System Administrator permissions can create user macros - see [Writing User Macros](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/writing-user-macros-1627457859.html)
. 

If you want to create something more complex, you can develop your own plugin - see [Writing Confluence Plugins](https://developer.atlassian.com/display/CONFDEV/Writing+Confluence+Plugins)
.

Macro usage statistics
----------------------

Confluence administrators can check how often each macro is used in your site.

To see how often a macro is used, go to **Administration** menu ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/files/1627456231/1627456232/1/1755867814320/cog.png) , then **General Configuration**  > **Macro Usage**.  This lists how often each macro is used in current spaces but doesn't include any macros used on pages in archived spaces or macros provided by disabled apps.

Unknown macro
-------------

In view page mode, disabled macros show a placeholder image:

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/files/1627457040/1627457042/1/1755867900309/unknown-macro.png)

When macros with body content are disabled, the content will be preserved and display in the editor. 

To display the content in view mode:

1.  In the editor, select the macro and choose **Edit**.
2.  In the macro browser, select **Back.**
3.  Search for a new macro and select it.
4.  Check your content displays in the **Preview** pane.
5.  Select **Insert**.

In the editor, the disabled macro will switch to the new macro and display your content. Update the page to display the content in view mode.

  

tip/resting Created with Sketch.

**Do more with Confluence**

Extend Confluence with one of the hundreds of other macros in the [Atlassian Marketplace](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/search?category=Macros&hosting=server)
, such as:

*   [Composition Tabs & Page Layouts](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/245/composition-tabs-page-layout?hosting=server&tab=overview)
    : Toggle or expand the visible of portions of your pages with the Toggle and Cloak macro
*   [Content Formatting for Confluence](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/247/content-formatting-for-confluence?hosting=server&tab=overview)
    : Over 30 easy-to-use Confluence macros gives you the ability to create better, more engaging content

Last modified on Aug 22, 2025

About the Confluence administrator's guide
------------------------------------------

This guide covers features and functions that are only available to administrators. 

For information on creating and administering spaces, See [Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf100/spaces-1627456438.html)
.

This guide assumes that you are using the Confluence default theme. If your Confluence site has been customized the header may look different, and menu items appear in different locations to the examples given in this guide.

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Macros are how you supercharge your Confluence pages. 

You can use macros to:

*   change the format and layout of your page
*   display media like video, audio, and social media content
*   collate and organise Confluence pages, blogs, and files
*   perform actions from a page, such as creating a page from a template. 

Take your Confluence space to the next level using macros. 

**On this page:**

_Screenshot: Page containing_ _Status, Page Properties Report, Livesearch, and Profile Picture macros to_ _help people find information about particular projects._

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/files/1540721916/1540721917/1/1743762037578/many-macros-view.png)

Macro basics
------------

### Add a macro to your page

To add a macro to your page:

1.  From the editor toolbar, select **Insert** ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/files/1540721007/1540721008/1/1743761950285/insert-macro-icon.png), then **Other Macros**.
    
2.  Select a macro from the list.
3.  Enter any required parameters.
4.  Choose **Insert**.

In the editor you'll see a placeholder that represents the macro. Once you publish your page, you'll see the macro in its full glory. 

### Edit a macro

Macro parameters are used to change the behavior of a macro.

To change the macro parameters:

1.  In the editor, click the macro placeholder and select **Edit**.  
    ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/files/1540720912/1540720913/1/1743761942533/edit-cheese.png)
2.  Update the parameters as required then select **Insert**.

Head to the documentation for specific macros below for full details of the parameters available in each macro.

-----------------

Here's a list of macros currently available with Confluence Data Center.

Click a macro name for details of the usage, including optional parameters and examples.

Get more macros
---------------

This documentation provides information on all the macros that are provided with Confluence by default. There may be other macros in your site, from Marketplace Apps, or perhaps developed in-house. 

### Third-party macros from the Atlassian Marketplace

You can find a wide range of Atlassian and third party macros at [The Marketplace](http://marketplace.atlassian.com/)
. These are distributed as apps and can be installed by a Confluence Administrator.

### Create your own macros

Users with System Administrator permissions can create user macros - see [Writing User Macros](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/writing-user-macros-1540722725.html)
. 

If you want to create something more complex, you can develop your own plugin - see [Writing Confluence Plugins](https://developer.atlassian.com/display/CONFDEV/Writing+Confluence+Plugins)
.

Macro usage statistics
----------------------

Confluence administrators can check how often each macro is used in your site.

To see how often a macro is used, go to **Administration** menu ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/files/1540721108/1540721109/1/1743761958507/cog.png) , then **General Configuration**  > **Macro Usage**.  This lists how often each macro is used in current spaces but doesn't include any macros used on pages in archived spaces or macros provided by disabled apps.

Unknown macro
-------------

In view page mode, disabled macros show a placeholder image:

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/files/1540721916/1540721918/1/1743762037689/unknown-macro.png)

When macros with body content are disabled, the content will be preserved and display in the editor. 

To display the content in view mode:

1.  In the editor, select the macro and choose **Edit**.
2.  In the macro browser, select **Back.**
3.  Search for a new macro and select it.
4.  Check your content displays in the **Preview** pane.
5.  Select **Insert**.

In the editor, the disabled macro will switch to the new macro and display your content. Update the page to display the content in view mode.

  

tip/resting Created with Sketch.

**Do more with Confluence**

Extend Confluence with one of the hundreds of other macros in the [Atlassian Marketplace](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/search?category=Macros&hosting=server)
, such as:

*   [Composition Tabs & Page Layouts](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/245/composition-tabs-page-layout?hosting=server&tab=overview)
    : Toggle or expand the visible of portions of your pages with the Toggle and Cloak macro
*   [Content Formatting for Confluence](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/247/content-formatting-for-confluence?hosting=server&tab=overview)
    : Over 30 easy-to-use Confluence macros gives you the ability to create better, more engaging content

#### Still need help?

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

About the Confluence administrator's guide
------------------------------------------

This guide covers features and functions that are only available to administrators. 

For information on creating and administering spaces, See [Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/spaces-1573749948.html)
.

This guide assumes that you are using the Confluence default theme. If your Confluence site has been customized the header may look different, and menu items appear in different locations to the examples given in this guide. 

*   [Getting Started as Confluence Administrator](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/getting-started-as-confluence-administrator-1573751283.html)
    
*   [Manage Users](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf95/manage-users-1573751289.html)

#### Creating your Own Local Version of our Documentation

We provide XML zip files of the documentation space, for you to download and use to create your own local version of our documentation on your Confluence site. Each XML zip file contains a Confluence-specific XML format plus attachments (screenshots etc), which you can upload to your Confluence site. Please take a look at the [instructions for uploading an XML file](https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/restore-a-space-or-multiple-spaces-152036.html)
.

_Note:_ When you create your new space from our XML source code, the space will inherit the Confluence 'Documentation' theme and the customizations we have made to the header, footer and left-hand navigation bar. You will need to adjust or remove these customizations. Please refer to the [Confluence documentation](https://confluence.atlassian.com/spaces/CONF54/pages/428804673/Local+Confluence+Documentation)
 or [Jira documentation](https://confluence.atlassian.com/jira/local-jira-documentation-185729488.html)
 for details.

About the Confluence administrator's guide
------------------------------------------

This guide covers features and functions that are only available to administrators. 

For information on creating and administering spaces, See [Spaces](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf94/spaces-1540721315.html)
.

This guide assumes that you are using the Confluence default theme. If your Confluence site has been customized the header may look different, and menu items appear in different locations to the examples given in this guide.

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Macros are how you supercharge your Confluence pages. 

You can use macros to:

*   change the format and layout of your page
*   display media like video, audio, and social media content
*   collate and organise Confluence pages, blogs, and files
*   perform actions from a page, such as creating a page from a template. 

Take your Confluence space to the next level using macros. 

**On this page:**

_Screenshot: Page containing_ _Status, Page Properties Report, Livesearch, and Profile Picture macros to_ _help people find information about particular projects._

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/files/1502350295/1502350296/1/1738669728033/many-macros-view.png)

Macro basics
------------

### Add a macro to your page

To add a macro to your page:

1.  From the editor toolbar, select **Insert** ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/files/1502349386/1502349387/1/1738669639424/insert-macro-icon.png), then **Other Macros**.
    
2.  Select a macro from the list.
3.  Enter any required parameters.
4.  Choose **Insert**.

In the editor you'll see a placeholder that represents the macro. Once you publish your page, you'll see the macro in its full glory. 

### Edit a macro

Macro parameters are used to change the behavior of a macro.

To change the macro parameters:

1.  In the editor, click the macro placeholder and select **Edit**.  
    ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/files/1502349291/1502349292/1/1738669630658/edit-cheese.png)
2.  Update the parameters as required then select **Insert**.

Head to the documentation for specific macros below for full details of the parameters available in each macro.

Confluence macros
-----------------

Here's a list of macros currently available with Confluence Data Center.

Click a macro name for details of the usage, including optional parameters and examples.

Get more macros
---------------

This documentation provides information on all the macros that are provided with Confluence by default. There may be other macros in your site, from Marketplace Apps, or perhaps developed in-house. 

### Third-party macros from the Atlassian Marketplace

You can find a wide range of Atlassian and third party macros at [The Marketplace](http://marketplace.atlassian.com/)
. These are distributed as apps and can be installed by a Confluence Administrator.

### Create your own macros

Users with System Administrator permissions can create user macros - see [Writing User Macros](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/writing-user-macros-1502351104.html)
. 

If you want to create something more complex, you can develop your own plugin - see [Writing Confluence Plugins](https://developer.atlassian.com/display/CONFDEV/Writing+Confluence+Plugins)
.

Macro usage statistics
----------------------

Confluence administrators can check how often each macro is used in your site.

To see how often a macro is used, go to **Administration** menu ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/files/1502349487/1502349488/1/1738669648123/cog.png) , then **General Configuration**  > **Macro Usage**.  This lists how often each macro is used in current spaces but doesn't include any macros used on pages in archived spaces or macros provided by disabled apps.

Unknown macro
-------------

In view page mode, disabled macros show a placeholder image:

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf93/files/1502350295/1502350297/1/1738669728145/unknown-macro.png)

When macros with body content are disabled, the content will be preserved and display in the editor. 

To display the content in view mode:

1.  In the editor, select the macro and choose **Edit**.
2.  In the macro browser, select **Back.**
3.  Search for a new macro and select it.
4.  Check your content displays in the **Preview** pane.
5.  Select **Insert**.

In the editor, the disabled macro will switch to the new macro and display your content. Update the page to display the content in view mode.

  

tip/resting Created with Sketch.

**Do more with Confluence**

Extend Confluence with one of the hundreds of other macros in the [Atlassian Marketplace](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/search?category=Macros&hosting=server)
, such as:

*   [Composition Tabs & Page Layouts](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/245/composition-tabs-page-layout?hosting=server&tab=overview)
    : Toggle or expand the visible of portions of your pages with the Toggle and Cloak macro
*   [Content Formatting for Confluence](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/247/content-formatting-for-confluence?hosting=server&tab=overview)
    : Over 30 easy-to-use Confluence macros gives you the ability to create better, more engaging content

Last modified on Feb 4, 2025

#### Still need help?

The Atlassian Community is here for you.

[Ask the community](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/custom/page/page-id/create-post-step-1?add-tags=Confluence)

Macros are how you supercharge your Confluence pages. 

You can use macros to:

*   change the format and layout of your page
*   display media like video, audio, and social media content
*   collate and organise Confluence pages, blogs, and files
*   perform actions from a page, such as creating a page from a template. 

Take your Confluence space to the next level using macros. 

**On this page:**

_Screenshot: Page containing_ _Status, Page Properties Report, Livesearch, and Profile Picture macros to_ _help people find information about particular projects._

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/files/1477576130/1477576131/1/1733814944879/many-macros-view.png)

Macro basics
------------

### Add a macro to your page

To add a macro to your page:

1.  From the editor toolbar, select **Insert** ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/files/1477575223/1477575224/1/1733814851827/insert-macro-icon.png), then **Other Macros**.
    
2.  Select a macro from the list.
3.  Enter any required parameters.
4.  Choose **Insert**.

In the editor you'll see a placeholder that represents the macro. Once you publish your page, you'll see the macro in its full glory. 

### Edit a macro

Macro parameters are used to change the behavior of a macro.

To change the macro parameters:

1.  In the editor, click the macro placeholder and select **Edit**.  
    ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/files/1477575128/1477575129/1/1733814843438/edit-cheese.png)
2.  Update the parameters as required then select **Insert**.

Head to the documentation for specific macros below for full details of the parameters available in each macro.

Confluence macros
-----------------

Here's a list of macros currently available with Confluence Data Center.

Click a macro name for details of the usage, including optional parameters and examples.

Get more macros
---------------

This documentation provides information on all the macros that are provided with Confluence by default. There may be other macros in your site, from Marketplace Apps, or perhaps developed in-house. 

### Third-party macros from the Atlassian Marketplace

You can find a wide range of Atlassian and third party macros at [The Marketplace](http://marketplace.atlassian.com/)
. These are distributed as apps and can be installed by a Confluence Administrator.

### Create your own macros

Users with System Administrator permissions can create user macros - see [Writing User Macros](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/writing-user-macros-1477576935.html)
. 

If you want to create something more complex, you can develop your own plugin - see [Writing Confluence Plugins](https://developer.atlassian.com/display/CONFDEV/Writing+Confluence+Plugins)
.

Macro usage statistics
----------------------

Confluence administrators can check how often each macro is used in your site.

To see how often a macro is used, go to **Administration** menu ![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/files/1477575324/1477575325/1/1733814861019/cog.png) , then **General Configuration**  > **Macro Usage**.  This lists how often each macro is used in current spaces but doesn't include any macros used on pages in archived spaces or macros provided by disabled apps.

Unknown macro
-------------

In view page mode, disabled macros show a placeholder image:

![](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/files/1477576130/1477576132/1/1733814944985/unknown-macro.png)

When macros with body content are disabled, the content will be preserved and display in the editor. 

To display the content in view mode:

1.  In the editor, select the macro and choose **Edit**.
2.  In the macro browser, select **Back.**
3.  Search for a new macro and select it.
4.  Check your content displays in the **Preview** pane.
5.  Select **Insert**.

In the editor, the disabled macro will switch to the new macro and display your content. Update the page to display the content in view mode.

  

tip/resting Created with Sketch.

**Do more with Confluence**

Extend Confluence with one of the hundreds of other macros in the [Atlassian Marketplace](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/search?category=Macros&hosting=server)
, such as:

*   [Composition Tabs & Page Layouts](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/245/composition-tabs-page-layout?hosting=server&tab=overview)
    : Toggle or expand the visible of portions of your pages with the Toggle and Cloak macro
*   [Content Formatting for Confluence](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/247/content-formatting-for-confluence?hosting=server&tab=overview)
    : Over 30 easy-to-use Confluence macros gives you the ability to create better, more engaging content

Confluence administrator's guide
================================

#### Confluence Data Center documentation

*   [Get started](https://confluence.atlassian.com/conf92/get-started-1477575467.html)

#### Still need help?
